Deuteronomy28:1 If you fully obey the LORD your God and carefully follow all his commands I give you today
Deuteronomy28:2 All these blessings will come upon you and accompany you if you obey the LORD your God:
Deuteronomy28:3 You will be blessed in the city and blessed in the country.
Deuteronomy28:4 The fruit of your womb will be blessed
Deuteronomy28:5 Your basket and your kneading trough will be blessed.
Deuteronomy28:6 You will be blessed when you come in and blessed when you go out.
Deuteronomy28:7 The LORD will grant that the enemies who rise up against you will be defeated before you. They will come at you from one direction but flee from you in seven.
Deuteronomy28:8 The LORD will send a blessing on your barns and on everything you put your hand to. The LORD your God will bless you in the land he is giving you.
Deuteronomy28:9 The LORD will establish you as his holy people
Deuteronomy28:10 Then all the peoples on earth will see that you are called by the name of the LORD
Deuteronomy28:11 The LORD will grant you abundant prosperity-in the fruit of your womb
Deuteronomy28:12 The LORD will open the heavens
Deuteronomy28:13 The LORD will make you the head
Deuteronomy28:14 Do not turn aside from any of the commands I give you today
Deuteronomy28:15 However
Deuteronomy28:16 You will be cursed in the city and cursed in the country.
Deuteronomy28:17 Your basket and your kneading trough will be cursed.
Deuteronomy28:18 The fruit of your womb will be cursed
Deuteronomy28:19 You will be cursed when you come in and cursed when you go out.
Deuteronomy28:20 The LORD will send on you curses
Deuteronomy28:21 The LORD will plague you with diseases until he has destroyed you from the land you are entering to possess.
Deuteronomy28:22 The LORD will strike you with wasting disease
Deuteronomy28:23 The sky over your head will be bronze
Deuteronomy28:24 The LORD will turn the rain of your country into dust and powder; it will come down from the skies until you are destroyed.
Deuteronomy28:25 The LORD will cause you to be defeated before your enemies. You will come at them from one direction but flee from them in seven
Deuteronomy28:26 Your carcasses will be food for all the birds of the air and the beasts of the earth
Deuteronomy28:27 The LORD will afflict you with the boils of Egypt and with tumors
Deuteronomy28:28 The LORD will afflict you with madness
Deuteronomy28:29 At midday you will grope about like a blind man in the dark. You will be unsuccessful in everything you do; day after day you will be oppressed and robbed
Deuteronomy28:30 You will be pledged to be married to a woman
Deuteronomy28:31 Your ox will be slaughtered before your eyes
Deuteronomy28:32 Your sons and daughters will be given to another nation
Deuteronomy28:33 A people that you do not know will eat what your land and labor produce
Deuteronomy28:34 The sights you see will drive you mad.
Deuteronomy28:35 The LORD will afflict your knees and legs with painful boils that cannot be cured
Deuteronomy28:36 The LORD will drive you and the king you set over you to a nation unknown to you or your fathers. There you will worship other gods
Deuteronomy28:37 You will become a thing of horror and an object of scorn and ridicule to all the nations where the LORD will drive you.
Deuteronomy28:38 You will sow much seed in the field but you will harvest little
Deuteronomy28:39 You will plant vineyards and cultivate them but you will not drink the wine or gather the grapes
Deuteronomy28:40 You will have olive trees throughout your country but you will not use the oil
Deuteronomy28:41 You will have sons and daughters but you will not keep them
Deuteronomy28:42 Swarms of locusts will take over all your trees and the crops of your land.
Deuteronomy28:43 The alien who lives among you will rise above you higher and higher
Deuteronomy28:44 He will lend to you
Deuteronomy28:45 All these curses will come upon you. They will pursue you and overtake you until you are destroyed
Deuteronomy28:46 They will be a sign and a wonder to you and your descendants forever.
Deuteronomy28:47 Because you did not serve the LORD your God joyfully and gladly in the time of prosperity
Deuteronomy28:48 therefore in hunger and thirst
Deuteronomy28:49 The LORD will bring a nation against you from far away
Deuteronomy28:50 a fierce-looking nation without respect for the old or pity for the young.
Deuteronomy28:51 They will devour the young of your livestock and the crops of your land until you are destroyed. They will leave you no grain
Deuteronomy28:52 They will lay siege to all the cities throughout your land until the high fortified walls in which you trust fall down. They will besiege all the cities throughout the land the LORD your God is giving you.
Deuteronomy28:53 Because of the suffering that your enemy will inflict on you during the siege
Deuteronomy28:54 Even the most gentle and sensitive man among you will have no compassion on his own brother or the wife he loves or his surviving children
Deuteronomy28:55 and he will not give to one of them any of the flesh of his children that he is eating. It will be all he has left because of the suffering your enemy will inflict on you during the siege of all your cities.
Deuteronomy28:56 The most gentle and sensitive woman among you-so sensitive and gentle that she would not venture to touch the ground with the sole of her foot-will begrudge the husband she loves and her own son or daughter
Deuteronomy28:57 the afterbirth from her womb and the children she bears. For she intends to eat them secretly during the siege and in the distress that your enemy will inflict on you in your cities.
Deuteronomy28:58 If you do not carefully follow all the words of this law
Deuteronomy28:59 the LORD will send fearful plagues on you and your descendants
Deuteronomy28:60 He will bring upon you all the diseases of Egypt that you dreaded
Deuteronomy28:61 The LORD will also bring on you every kind of sickness and disaster not recorded in this Book of the Law
Deuteronomy28:62 You who were as numerous as the stars in the sky will be left but few in number
Deuteronomy28:63 Just as it pleased the LORD to make you prosper and increase in number
Deuteronomy28:64 Then the LORD will scatter you among all nations
Deuteronomy28:65 Among those nations you will find no repose
Deuteronomy28:66 You will live in constant suspense
Deuteronomy28:67 In the morning you will say
Deuteronomy28:68 The LORD will send you back in ships to Egypt on a journey I said you should never make again. There you will offer yourselves for sale to your enemies as male and female slaves

Deuteronomy33:4 the law that Moses gave us
Deuteronomy33:5 He was king over Jeshurun when the leaders of the people assembled
Deuteronomy33:6 Let Reuben live and not die
Deuteronomy33:7 And this he said about Judah: Hear
Deuteronomy33:8 About Levi he said: Your Thummim and Urim belong to the man you favored. You tested him at Massah; you contended with him at the waters of Meribah.
Deuteronomy33:9 He said of his father and mother
Deuteronomy33:10 He teaches your precepts to Jacob and your law to Israel. He offers incense before you and whole burnt offerings on your altar.
Deuteronomy33:11 Bless all his skills
Deuteronomy33:12 About Benjamin he said: Let the beloved of the LORD rest secure in him
Deuteronomy33:13 About Joseph he said: May the LORD bless his land with the precious dew from heaven above and with the deep waters that lie below;
Deuteronomy33:14 with the best the sun brings forth and the finest the moon can yield;
Deuteronomy33:15 with the choicest gifts of the ancient mountains and the fruitfulness of the everlasting hills;
Deuteronomy33:16 with the best gifts of the earth and its fullness and the favor of him who dwelt in the burning bush. Let all these rest on the head of Joseph
Deuteronomy33:17 In majesty he is like a firstborn bull; his horns are the horns of a wild ox. With them he will gore the nations
Deuteronomy33:18 About Zebulun he said: Rejoice
Deuteronomy33:19 They will summon peoples to the mountain and there offer sacrifices of righteousness; they will feast on the abundance of the seas
Deuteronomy33:20 About Gad he said: Blessed is he who enlarges Gad's domain! Gad lives there like a lion
Deuteronomy33:21 He chose the best land for himself; the leader's portion was kept for him. When the heads of the people assembled
Deuteronomy33:22 About Dan he said: Dan is a lion's cub
Deuteronomy33:23 About Naphtali he said: Naphtali is abounding with the favor of the LORD and is full of his blessing; he will inherit southward to the lake.
Deuteronomy33:24 About Asher he said: Most blessed of sons is Asher; let him be favored by his brothers
Deuteronomy33:25 The bolts of your gates will be iron and bronze
Deuteronomy33:26 There is no one like the God of Jeshurun
Deuteronomy33:27 The eternal God is your refuge
Deuteronomy33:28 So Israel will live in safety alone; Jacob's spring is secure in a land of grain and new wine
Deuteronomy33:29 Blessed are you
Deuteronomy34:1 Then Moses climbed Mount Nebo from the plains of Moab to the top of Pisgah
Deuteronomy34:2 all of Naphtali
Deuteronomy34:3 the Negev and the whole region from the Valley of Jericho
Deuteronomy34:4 Then the LORD said to him
Deuteronomy34:5 And Moses the servant of the LORD died there in Moab
Deuteronomy34:6 He buried him in Moab
Deuteronomy34:7 Moses was a hundred and twenty years old when he died
Deuteronomy34:8 The Israelites grieved for Moses in the plains of Moab thirty days
Deuteronomy34:9 Now Joshua son of Nun was filled with the spirit of wisdom because Moses had laid his hands on him. So the Israelites listened to him and did what the LORD had commanded Moses.
Deuteronomy34:10 Since then
Deuteronomy34:11 who did all those miraculous signs and wonders the LORD sent him to do in Egypt-to Pharaoh and to all his officials and to his whole land.
Deuteronomy34:12 For no one has ever shown the mighty power or performed the awesome deeds that Moses did in the sight of all Israel.
Joshua1:1 After the death of Moses the servant of the LORD
Joshua1:2 Moses my servant is dead. Now then
Joshua1:3 I will give you every place where you set your foot
Joshua1:4 Your territory will extend from the desert to Lebanon
Joshua1:5 No one will be able to stand up against you all the days of your life. As I was with Moses
Joshua1:6 Be strong and courageous
Joshua1:7 Be strong and very courageous. Be careful to obey all the law my servant Moses gave you; do not turn from it to the right or to the left
Joshua1:8 Do not let this Book of the Law depart from your mouth; meditate on it day and night
Joshua1:9 Have I not commanded you? Be strong and courageous. Do not be terrified; do not be discouraged
Joshua1:10 So Joshua ordered the officers of the people:
Joshua1:11 Go through the camp and tell the people
Joshua1:12 But to the Reubenites
Joshua1:13 Remember the command that Moses the servant of the LORD gave you: 'The LORD your God is giving you rest and has granted you this land.'
Joshua1:14 Your wives
Joshua1:15 until the LORD gives them rest
Joshua1:16 Then they answered Joshua
Joshua1:17 Just as we fully obeyed Moses
Joshua1:18 Whoever rebels against your word and does not obey your words
Joshua2:1 Then Joshua son of Nun secretly sent two spies from Shittim. Go
Joshua2:2 The king of Jericho was told
Joshua2:3 So the king of Jericho sent this message to Rahab: Bring out the men who came to you and entered your house
Joshua2:4 But the woman had taken the two men and hidden them. She said
Joshua2:5 At dusk
Joshua2:6 (But she had taken them up to the roof and hidden them under the stalks of flax she had laid out on the roof.)
Joshua2:7 So the men set out in pursuit of the spies on the road that leads to the fords of the Jordan
Joshua2:8 Before the spies lay down for the night
Joshua2:9 and said to them
Joshua2:10 We have heard how the LORD dried up the water of the Red Sea for you when you came out of Egypt
Joshua2:11 When we heard of it
Joshua2:12 Now then
Joshua2:13 that you will spare the lives of my father and mother
Joshua2:14 Our lives for your lives! the men assured her. If you don't tell what we are doing
Joshua2:15 So she let them down by a rope through the window
Joshua2:16 Now she had said to them
Joshua2:17 The men said to her
Joshua2:18 unless
Joshua2:19 If anyone goes outside your house into the street
Joshua2:20 But if you tell what we are doing
Joshua2:21 Agreed
Joshua2:22 When they left
Joshua2:23 Then the two men started back. They went down out of the hills
Joshua2:24 They said to Joshua
Joshua3:1 Early in the morning Joshua and all the Israelites set out from Shittim and went to the Jordan
Joshua3:2 After three days the officers went throughout the camp
Joshua3:3 giving orders to the people: When you see the ark of the covenant of the LORD your God
Joshua3:4 Then you will know which way to go
Joshua3:5 Joshua told the people
Joshua3:6 Joshua said to the priests
Joshua3:7 And the LORD said to Joshua
Joshua3:8 Tell the priests who carry the ark of the covenant: 'When you reach the edge of the Jordan's waters
Joshua3:9 Joshua said to the Israelites
Joshua3:10 This is how you will know that the living God is among you and that he will certainly drive out before you the Canaanites
Joshua3:11 See
Joshua3:12 Now then
Joshua3:13 And as soon as the priests who carry the ark of the LORD -the Lord of all the earth-set foot in the Jordan
Joshua3:14 So when the people broke camp to cross the Jordan
Joshua3:15 Now the Jordan is at flood stage all during harvest. Yet as soon as the priests who carried the ark reached the Jordan and their feet touched the water's edge
Joshua3:16 the water from upstream stopped flowing. It piled up in a heap a great distance away
Joshua3:17 The priests who carried the ark of the covenant of the LORD stood firm on dry ground in the middle of the Jordan
Joshua4:1 When the whole nation had finished crossing the Jordan
Joshua4:2 Choose twelve men from among the people
Joshua4:3 and tell them to take up twelve stones from the middle of the Jordan from right where the priests stood and to carry them over with you and put them down at the place where you stay tonight.
Joshua4:4 So Joshua called together the twelve men he had appointed from the Israelites
Joshua4:5 and said to them
Joshua4:6 to serve as a sign among you. In the future
Joshua4:7 tell them that the flow of the Jordan was cut off before the ark of the covenant of the LORD. When it crossed the Jordan
Joshua4:8 So the Israelites did as Joshua commanded them. They took twelve stones from the middle of the Jordan
Joshua4:9 Joshua set up the twelve stones that had been in the middle of the Jordan at the spot where the priests who carried the ark of the covenant had stood. And they are there to this day.
Joshua4:10 Now the priests who carried the ark remained standing in the middle of the Jordan until everything the LORD had commanded Joshua was done by the people
Joshua4:11 and as soon as all of them had crossed
Joshua4:12 The men of Reuben
Joshua4:13 About forty thousand armed for battle crossed over before the LORD to the plains of Jericho for war.
Joshua4:14 That day the LORD exalted Joshua in the sight of all Israel; and they revered him all the days of his life
Joshua4:15 Then the LORD said to Joshua
Joshua4:16 Command the priests carrying the ark of the Testimony to come up out of the Jordan.
Joshua4:17 So Joshua commanded the priests
Joshua4:18 And the priests came up out of the river carrying the ark of the covenant of the LORD. No sooner had they set their feet on the dry ground than the waters of the Jordan returned to their place and ran at flood stage as before.
Joshua4:19 On the tenth day of the first month the people went up from the Jordan and camped at Gilgal on the eastern border of Jericho.
Joshua4:20 And Joshua set up at Gilgal the twelve stones they had taken out of the Jordan.
Joshua4:21 He said to the Israelites
Joshua4:22 tell them
Joshua4:23 For the LORD your God dried up the Jordan before you until you had crossed over. The LORD your God did to the Jordan just what he had done to the Red Sea when he dried it up before us until we had crossed over.
Joshua4:24 He did this so that all the peoples of the earth might know that the hand of the LORD is powerful and so that you might always fear the LORD your God.
Joshua5:1 Now when all the Amorite kings west of the Jordan and all the Canaanite kings along the coast heard how the LORD had dried up the Jordan before the Israelites until we had crossed over
Joshua5:2 At that time the LORD said to Joshua
Joshua5:3 So Joshua made flint knives and circumcised the Israelites at Gibeath Haaraloth.
Joshua5:4 Now this is why he did so: All those who came out of Egypt-all the men of military age-died in the desert on the way after leaving Egypt.
Joshua5:5 All the people that came out had been circumcised
Joshua5:6 The Israelites had moved about in the desert forty years until all the men who were of military age when they left Egypt had died
Joshua5:7 So he raised up their sons in their place
Joshua5:8 And after the whole nation had been circumcised
Joshua5:9 Then the LORD said to Joshua
Joshua5:10 On the evening of the fourteenth day of the month
Joshua5:11 The day after the Passover
Joshua5:12 The manna stopped the day after they ate this food from the land; there was no longer any manna for the Israelites
Joshua5:13 Now when Joshua was near Jericho
Joshua5:14 Neither
Joshua5:15 The commander of the LORD's army replied
Joshua6:1 Now Jericho was tightly shut up because of the Israelites. No one went out and no one came in.
Joshua6:2 Then the LORD said to Joshua
Joshua6:3 March around the city once with all the armed men. Do this for six days.
Joshua6:4 Have seven priests carry trumpets of rams' horns in front of the ark. On the seventh day
Joshua6:5 When you hear them sound a long blast on the trumpets
Joshua6:6 So Joshua son of Nun called the priests and said to them
Joshua6:7 And he ordered the people
Joshua6:8 When Joshua had spoken to the people
Joshua6:9 The armed guard marched ahead of the priests who blew the trumpets
Joshua6:10 But Joshua had commanded the people
Joshua6:11 So he had the ark of the LORD carried around the city
Joshua6:12 Joshua got up early the next morning and the priests took up the ark of the LORD.
Joshua6:13 The seven priests carrying the seven trumpets went forward
Joshua6:14 So on the second day they marched around the city once and returned to the camp. They did this for six days.
Joshua6:15 On the seventh day
Joshua6:16 The seventh time around
Joshua6:17 The city and all that is in it are to be devoted to the LORD. Only Rahab the prostitute and all who are with her in her house shall be spared
Joshua6:18 But keep away from the devoted things
Joshua6:19 All the silver and gold and the articles of bronze and iron are sacred to the LORD and must go into his treasury.
Joshua6:20 When the trumpets sounded
Joshua6:21 They devoted the city to the LORD and destroyed with the sword every living thing in it-men and women
Joshua6:22 Joshua said to the two men who had spied out the land
Joshua6:23 So the young men who had done the spying went in and brought out Rahab
Joshua6:24 Then they burned the whole city and everything in it
Joshua6:25 But Joshua spared Rahab the prostitute
Joshua6:26 At that time Joshua pronounced this solemn oath: Cursed before the LORD is the man who undertakes to rebuild this city
Joshua6:27 So the LORD was with Joshua
Joshua7:1 But the Israelites acted unfaithfully in regard to the devoted things; Achan son of Carmi
Joshua7:2 Now Joshua sent men from Jericho to Ai
Joshua7:3 When they returned to Joshua
Joshua7:4 So about three thousand men went up; but they were routed by the men of Ai
Joshua7:5 who killed about thirty-six of them. They chased the Israelites from the city gate as far as the stone quarries and struck them down on the slopes. At this the hearts of the people melted and became like water.
Joshua7:6 Then Joshua tore his clothes and fell facedown to the ground before the ark of the LORD
Joshua7:7 And Joshua said
Joshua7:8 O Lord
Joshua7:9 The Canaanites and the other people of the country will hear about this and they will surround us and wipe out our name from the earth. What then will you do for your own great name?
Joshua7:10 The LORD said to Joshua
Joshua7:11 Israel has sinned; they have violated my covenant
Joshua7:12 That is why the Israelites cannot stand against their enemies; they turn their backs and run because they have been made liable to destruction. I will not be with you anymore unless you destroy whatever among you is devoted to destruction.
Joshua7:13 Go
Joshua7:14 'In the morning
Joshua7:15 He who is caught with the devoted things shall be destroyed by fire
Joshua7:16 Early the next morning Joshua had Israel come forward by tribes
Joshua7:17 The clans of Judah came forward
Joshua7:18 Joshua had his family come forward man by man

Joshua7:20 Achan replied
Joshua7:21 When I saw in the plunder a beautiful robe from Babylonia
Joshua7:22 So Joshua sent messengers
Joshua7:23 They took the things from the tent
Joshua7:24 Then Joshua
Joshua7:25 Joshua said
Joshua7:26 Over Achan they heaped up a large pile of rocks
Joshua8:1 Then the LORD said to Joshua
Joshua8:2 You shall do to Ai and its king as you did to Jericho and its king
Joshua8:3 So Joshua and the whole army moved out to attack Ai. He chose thirty thousand of his best fighting men and sent them out at night
Joshua8:4 with these orders: Listen carefully. You are to set an ambush behind the city. Don't go very far from it. All of you be on the alert.
Joshua8:5 I and all those with me will advance on the city
Joshua8:6 They will pursue us until we have lured them away from the city
Joshua8:7 you are to rise up from ambush and take the city. The LORD your God will give it into your hand.
Joshua8:8 When you have taken the city
Joshua8:9 Then Joshua sent them off
Joshua8:10 Early the next morning Joshua mustered his men
Joshua8:11 The entire force that was with him marched up and approached the city and arrived in front of it. They set up camp north of Ai
Joshua8:12 Joshua had taken about five thousand men and set them in ambush between Bethel and Ai
Joshua8:13 They had the soldiers take up their positions-all those in the camp to the north of the city and the ambush to the west of it. That night Joshua went into the valley.
Joshua8:14 When the king of Ai saw this
Joshua8:15 Joshua and all Israel let themselves be driven back before them
Joshua8:16 All the men of Ai were called to pursue them
Joshua8:17 Not a man remained in Ai or Bethel who did not go after Israel. They left the city open and went in pursuit of Israel.
Joshua8:18 Then the LORD said to Joshua
Joshua8:19 As soon as he did this
Joshua8:20 The men of Ai looked back and saw the smoke of the city rising against the sky
Joshua8:21 For when Joshua and all Israel saw that the ambush had taken the city and that smoke was going up from the city
Joshua8:22 The men of the ambush also came out of the city against them
Joshua8:23 But they took the king of Ai alive and brought him to Joshua.
Joshua8:24 When Israel had finished killing all the men of Ai in the fields and in the desert where they had chased them
Joshua8:25 Twelve thousand men and women fell that day-all the people of Ai.
Joshua8:26 For Joshua did not draw back the hand that held out his javelin until he had destroyed all who lived in Ai.
Joshua8:27 But Israel did carry off for themselves the livestock and plunder of this city
Joshua8:28 So Joshua burned Ai and made it a permanent heap of ruins
Joshua8:29 He hung the king of Ai on a tree and left him there until evening. At sunset
Joshua8:30 Then Joshua built on Mount Ebal an altar to the LORD
Joshua8:31 as Moses the servant of the LORD had commanded the Israelites. He built it according to what is written in the Book of the Law of Moses-an altar of uncut stones
Joshua8:32 There
Joshua8:33 All Israel
Joshua8:34 Afterward
Joshua8:35 There was not a word of all that Moses had commanded that Joshua did not read to the whole assembly of Israel
Joshua9:1 Now when all the kings west of the Jordan heard about these things-those in the hill country
Joshua9:2 they came together to make war against Joshua and Israel.
Joshua9:3 However
Joshua9:4 they resorted to a ruse: They went as a delegation whose donkeys were loaded with worn-out sacks and old wineskins
Joshua9:5 The men put worn and patched sandals on their feet and wore old clothes. All the bread of their food supply was dry and moldy.
Joshua9:6 Then they went to Joshua in the camp at Gilgal and said to him and the men of Israel
Joshua9:7 The men of Israel said to the Hivites
Joshua9:8 We are your servants
Joshua9:9 They answered: Your servants have come from a very distant country because of the fame of the LORD your God. For we have heard reports of him: all that he did in Egypt
Joshua9:10 and all that he did to the two kings of the Amorites east of the Jordan-Sihon king of Heshbon
Joshua9:11 And our elders and all those living in our country said to us
Joshua9:12 This bread of ours was warm when we packed it at home on the day we left to come to you. But now see how dry and moldy it is.
Joshua9:13 And these wineskins that we filled were new
Joshua9:14 The men of Israel sampled their provisions but did not inquire of the LORD.
Joshua9:15 Then Joshua made a treaty of peace with them to let them live
Joshua9:16 Three days after they made the treaty with the Gibeonites
Joshua9:17 So the Israelites set out and on the third day came to their cities: Gibeon
Joshua9:18 But the Israelites did not attack them
Joshua9:19 but all the leaders answered
Joshua9:20 This is what we will do to them: We will let them live
Joshua9:21 They continued
Joshua9:22 Then Joshua summoned the Gibeonites and said
Joshua9:23 You are now under a curse: You will never cease to serve as woodcutters and water carriers for the house of my God.
Joshua9:24 They answered Joshua
Joshua9:25 We are now in your hands. Do to us whatever seems good and right to you.
Joshua9:26 So Joshua saved them from the Israelites
Joshua9:27 That day he made the Gibeonites woodcutters and water carriers for the community and for the altar of the LORD at the place the LORD would choose. And that is what they are to this day.
Joshua10:1 Now Adoni-Zedek king of Jerusalem heard that Joshua had taken Ai and totally destroyed it
Joshua10:2 He and his people were very much alarmed at this
Joshua10:3 So Adoni-Zedek king of Jerusalem appealed to Hoham king of Hebron
Joshua10:4 Come up and help me attack Gibeon
Joshua10:5 Then the five kings of the Amorites-the kings of Jerusalem
Joshua10:6 The Gibeonites then sent word to Joshua in the camp at Gilgal: Do not abandon your servants. Come up to us quickly and save us! Help us
Joshua10:7 So Joshua marched up from Gilgal with his entire army
Joshua10:8 The LORD said to Joshua
Joshua10:9 After an all-night march from Gilgal
Joshua10:10 The LORD threw them into confusion before Israel
Joshua10:11 As they fled before Israel on the road down from Beth Horon to Azekah
Joshua10:12 On the day the LORD gave the Amorites over to Israel
Joshua10:13 So the sun stood still
Joshua10:14 There has never been a day like it before or since
Joshua10:15 Then Joshua returned with all Israel to the camp at Gilgal.
Joshua10:16 Now the five kings had fled and hidden in the cave at Makkedah.
Joshua10:17 When Joshua was told that the five kings had been found hiding in the cave at Makkedah
Joshua10:18 he said
Joshua10:19 But don't stop! Pursue your enemies
Joshua10:20 So Joshua and the Israelites destroyed them completely-almost to a man-but the few who were left reached their fortified cities.
Joshua10:21 The whole army then returned safely to Joshua in the camp at Makkedah
Joshua10:22 Joshua said
Joshua10:23 So they brought the five kings out of the cave-the kings of Jerusalem
Joshua10:24 When they had brought these kings to Joshua
Joshua10:25 Joshua said to them
Joshua10:26 Then Joshua struck and killed the kings and hung them on five trees
Joshua10:27 At sunset Joshua gave the order and they took them down from the trees and threw them into the cave where they had been hiding. At the mouth of the cave they placed large rocks
Joshua10:28 That day Joshua took Makkedah. He put the city and its king to the sword and totally destroyed everyone in it. He left no survivors. And he did to the king of Makkedah as he had done to the king of Jericho.
Joshua10:29 Then Joshua and all Israel with him moved on from Makkedah to Libnah and attacked it.
Joshua10:30 The LORD also gave that city and its king into Israel's hand. The city and everyone in it Joshua put to the sword. He left no survivors there. And he did to its king as he had done to the king of Jericho.
Joshua10:31 Then Joshua and all Israel with him moved on from Libnah to Lachish; he took up positions against it and attacked it.
Joshua10:32 The LORD handed Lachish over to Israel
Joshua10:33 Meanwhile
Joshua10:34 Then Joshua and all Israel with him moved on from Lachish to Eglon; they took up positions against it and attacked it.
Joshua10:35 They captured it that same day and put it to the sword and totally destroyed everyone in it
Joshua10:36 Then Joshua and all Israel with him went up from Eglon to Hebron and attacked it.
Joshua10:37 They took the city and put it to the sword
Joshua10:38 Then Joshua and all Israel with him turned around and attacked Debir.
Joshua10:39 They took the city
Joshua10:40 So Joshua subdued the whole region
Joshua10:41 Joshua subdued them from Kadesh Barnea to Gaza and from the whole region of Goshen to Gibeon.
Joshua10:42 All these kings and their lands Joshua conquered in one campaign
Joshua10:43 Then Joshua returned with all Israel to the camp at Gilgal.

Joshua11:2 and to the northern kings who were in the mountains
Joshua11:3 to the Canaanites in the east and west; to the Amorites
Joshua11:4 They came out with all their troops and a large number of horses and chariots-a huge army
Joshua11:5 All these kings joined forces and made camp together at the Waters of Merom
Joshua11:6 The LORD said to Joshua
Joshua11:7 So Joshua and his whole army came against them suddenly at the Waters of Merom and attacked them
Joshua11:8 and the LORD gave them into the hand of Israel. They defeated them and pursued them all the way to Greater Sidon
Joshua11:9 Joshua did to them as the LORD had directed: He hamstrung their horses and burned their chariots.
Joshua11:10 At that time Joshua turned back and captured Hazor and put its king to the sword. (Hazor had been the head of all these kingdoms.)
Joshua11:11 Everyone in it they put to the sword. They totally destroyed them
Joshua11:12 Joshua took all these royal cities and their kings and put them to the sword. He totally destroyed them
Joshua11:13 Yet Israel did not burn any of the cities built on their mounds-except Hazor
Joshua11:14 The Israelites carried off for themselves all the plunder and livestock of these cities
Joshua11:15 As the LORD commanded his servant Moses
Joshua11:16 So Joshua took this entire land: the hill country
Joshua11:17 from Mount Halak
Joshua11:18 Joshua waged war against all these kings for a long time.
Joshua11:19 Except for the Hivites living in Gibeon
Joshua11:20 For it was the LORD himself who hardened their hearts to wage war against Israel
Joshua11:21 At that time Joshua went and destroyed the Anakites from the hill country: from Hebron
Joshua11:22 No Anakites were left in Israelite territory; only in Gaza
Joshua11:23 So Joshua took the entire land

Joshua14:1 Now these are the areas the Israelites received as an inheritance in the land of Canaan
Joshua14:2 Their inheritances were assigned by lot to the nine-and-a-half tribes

Joshua20:1 Then the LORD said to Joshua:
Joshua20:2 Tell the Israelites to designate the cities of refuge
Joshua20:3 so that anyone who kills a person accidentally and unintentionally may flee there and find protection from the avenger of blood.
Joshua20:4 When he flees to one of these cities
Joshua20:5 If the avenger of blood pursues him
Joshua20:6 He is to stay in that city until he has stood trial before the assembly and until the death of the high priest who is serving at that time. Then he may go back to his own home in the town from which he fled.
Joshua20:7 So they set apart Kedesh in Galilee in the hill country of Naphtali
Joshua20:8 On the east side of the Jordan of Jericho they designated Bezer in the desert on the plateau in the tribe of Reuben
Joshua20:9 Any of the Israelites or any alien living among them who killed someone accidentally could flee to these designated cities and not be killed by the avenger of blood prior to standing trial before the assembly.

Joshua23:1 After a long time had passed and the LORD had given Israel rest from all their enemies around them
Joshua23:2 summoned all Israel-their elders
Joshua23:3 You yourselves have seen everything the LORD your God has done to all these nations for your sake; it was the LORD your God who fought for you.
Joshua23:4 Remember how I have allotted as an inheritance for your tribes all the land of the nations that remain-the nations I conquered-between the Jordan and the Great Sea in the west.
Joshua23:5 The LORD your God himself will drive them out of your way. He will push them out before you
Joshua23:6 Be very strong; be careful to obey all that is written in the Book of the Law of Moses
Joshua23:7 Do not associate with these nations that remain among you; do not invoke the names of their gods or swear by them. You must not serve them or bow down to them.
Joshua23:8 But you are to hold fast to the LORD your God
Joshua23:9 The LORD has driven out before you great and powerful nations; to this day no one has been able to withstand you.
Joshua23:10 One of you routs a thousand
Joshua23:11 So be very careful to love the LORD your God.
Joshua23:12 But if you turn away and ally yourselves with the survivors of these nations that remain among you and if you intermarry with them and associate with them
Joshua23:13 then you may be sure that the LORD your God will no longer drive out these nations before you. Instead
Joshua23:14 Now I am about to go the way of all the earth. You know with all your heart and soul that not one of all the good promises the LORD your God gave you has failed. Every promise has been fulfilled; not one has failed.
Joshua23:15 But just as every good promise of the LORD your God has come true
Joshua23:16 If you violate the covenant of the LORD your God

Judges2:23 The LORD had allowed those nations to remain; he did not drive them out at once by giving them into the hands of Joshua.

1Samuel23:17 Don't be afraid
1Samuel23:18 The two of them made a covenant before the LORD. Then Jonathan went home
1Samuel23:19 The Ziphites went up to Saul at Gibeah and said
1Samuel23:20 Now
1Samuel23:21 Saul replied
1Samuel23:22 Go and make further preparation. Find out where David usually goes and who has seen him there. They tell me he is very crafty.
1Samuel23:23 Find out about all the hiding places he uses and come back to me with definite information. Then I will go with you; if he is in the area
1Samuel23:24 So they set out and went to Ziph ahead of Saul. Now David and his men were in the Desert of Maon
1Samuel23:25 Saul and his men began the search
1Samuel23:26 Saul was going along one side of the mountain
1Samuel23:27 a messenger came to Saul
1Samuel23:28 Then Saul broke off his pursuit of David and went to meet the Philistines. That is why they call this place Sela Hammahlekoth.
1Samuel23:29 And David went up from there and lived in the strongholds of En Gedi.
1Samuel24:1 After Saul returned from pursuing the Philistines
1Samuel24:2 So Saul took three thousand chosen men from all Israel and set out to look for David and his men near the Crags of the Wild Goats.
1Samuel24:3 He came to the sheep pens along the way; a cave was there
1Samuel24:4 The men said
1Samuel24:5 Afterward
1Samuel24:6 He said to his men
1Samuel24:7 With these words David rebuked his men and did not allow them to attack Saul. And Saul left the cave and went his way.
1Samuel24:8 Then David went out of the cave and called out to Saul
1Samuel24:9 He said to Saul
1Samuel24:10 This day you have seen with your own eyes how the LORD delivered you into my hands in the cave. Some urged me to kill you
1Samuel24:11 See
1Samuel24:12 May the LORD judge between you and me. And may the LORD avenge the wrongs you have done to me
1Samuel24:13 As the old saying goes
1Samuel24:14 Against whom has the king of Israel come out? Whom are you pursuing? A dead dog? A flea?
1Samuel24:15 May the LORD be our judge and decide between us. May he consider my cause and uphold it; may he vindicate me by delivering me from your hand.
1Samuel24:16 When David finished saying this
1Samuel24:17 You are more righteous than I
1Samuel24:18 You have just now told me of the good you did to me; the LORD delivered me into your hands
1Samuel24:19 When a man finds his enemy
1Samuel24:20 I know that you will surely be king and that the kingdom of Israel will be established in your hands.
1Samuel24:21 Now swear to me by the LORD that you will not cut off my descendants or wipe out my name from my father's family.
1Samuel24:22 So David gave his oath to Saul. Then Saul returned home
1Samuel25:1 Now Samuel died
1Samuel25:2 A certain man in Maon

1Samuel25:7 'Now I hear that it is sheep-shearing time. When your shepherds were with us

1Samuel25:9 When David's men arrived

1Samuel30:4 So David and his men wept aloud until they had no strength left to weep.

1Samuel30:7 Then David said to Abiathar the priest

1Samuel30:15 David asked him
1Samuel30:16 He led David down

1Samuel30:19 Nothing was missing: young or old
1Samuel30:20 He took all the flocks and herds

2Samuel7:1 After the king was settled in his palace and the LORD had given him rest from all his enemies around him
2Samuel7:2 he said to Nathan the prophet
2Samuel7:3 Nathan replied to the king
2Samuel7:4 That night the word of the LORD came to Nathan
2Samuel7:5 Go and tell my servant David
2Samuel7:6 I have not dwelt in a house from the day I brought the Israelites up out of Egypt to this day. I have been moving from place to place with a tent as my dwelling.
2Samuel7:7 Wherever I have moved with all the Israelites
2Samuel7:8 Now then
2Samuel7:9 I have been with you wherever you have gone
2Samuel7:10 And I will provide a place for my people Israel and will plant them so that they can have a home of their own and no longer be disturbed. Wicked people will not oppress them anymore
2Samuel7:11 and have done ever since the time I appointed leaders over my people Israel. I will also give you rest from all your enemies. 'The LORD declares to you that the LORD himself will establish a house for you:
2Samuel7:12 When your days are over and you rest with your fathers
2Samuel7:13 He is the one who will build a house for my Name
2Samuel7:14 I will be his father
2Samuel7:15 But my love will never be taken away from him
2Samuel7:16 Your house and your kingdom will endure forever before me; your throne will be established forever.'
2Samuel7:17 Nathan reported to David all the words of this entire revelation.
2Samuel7:18 Then King David went in and sat before the LORD
2Samuel7:19 And as if this were not enough in your sight
2Samuel7:20 What more can David say to you? For you know your servant
2Samuel7:21 For the sake of your word and according to your will

2Samuel21:17 But Abishai son of Zeruiah came to David's rescue; he struck the Philistine down and killed him. Then David's men swore to him
2Samuel21:18 In the course of time
2Samuel21:19 In another battle with the Philistines at Gob
2Samuel21:20 In still another battle
2Samuel21:21 When he taunted Israel
2Samuel21:22 These four were descendants of Rapha in Gath
2Samuel22:1 David sang to the LORD the words of this song when the LORD delivered him from the hand of all his enemies and from the hand of Saul.
2Samuel22:2 He said: The LORD is my rock
2Samuel22:3 my God is my rock
2Samuel22:4 I call to the LORD
2Samuel22:5 The waves of death swirled about me; the torrents of destruction overwhelmed me.
2Samuel22:6 The cords of the grave coiled around me; the snares of death confronted me.
2Samuel22:7 In my distress I called to the LORD; I called out to my God. From his temple he heard my voice; my cry came to his ears.
2Samuel22:8 The earth trembled and quaked
2Samuel22:9 Smoke rose from his nostrils; consuming fire came from his mouth
2Samuel22:10 He parted the heavens and came down; dark clouds were under his feet.
2Samuel22:11 He mounted the cherubim and flew; he soared on the wings of the wind.
2Samuel22:12 He made darkness his canopy around him- the dark rain clouds of the sky.
2Samuel22:13 Out of the brightness of his presence bolts of lightning blazed forth.
2Samuel22:14 The LORD thundered from heaven; the voice of the Most High resounded.
2Samuel22:15 He shot arrows and scattered the enemies
2Samuel22:16 The valleys of the sea were exposed and the foundations of the earth laid bare at the rebuke of the LORD
2Samuel22:17 He reached down from on high and took hold of me; he drew me out of deep waters.
2Samuel22:18 He rescued me from my powerful enemy
2Samuel22:19 They confronted me in the day of my disaster
2Samuel22:20 He brought me out into a spacious place; he rescued me because he delighted in me.
2Samuel22:21 The LORD has dealt with me according to my righteousness; according to the cleanness of my hands he has rewarded me.
2Samuel22:22 For I have kept the ways of the LORD; I have not done evil by turning from my God.
2Samuel22:23 All his laws are before me; I have not turned away from his decrees.
2Samuel22:24 I have been blameless before him and have kept myself from sin.
2Samuel22:25 The LORD has rewarded me according to my righteousness
2Samuel22:26 To the faithful you show yourself faithful
2Samuel22:27 to the pure you show yourself pure
2Samuel22:28 You save the humble
2Samuel22:29 You are my lamp
2Samuel22:30 With your help I can advance against a troop; with my God I can scale a wall.
2Samuel22:31 As for God
2Samuel22:32 For who is God besides the LORD? And who is the Rock except our God?
2Samuel22:33 It is God who arms me with strength and makes my way perfect.
2Samuel22:34 He makes my feet like the feet of a deer; he enables me to stand on the heights.
2Samuel22:35 He trains my hands for battle; my arms can bend a bow of bronze.
2Samuel22:36 You give me your shield of victory; you stoop down to make me great.
2Samuel22:37 You broaden the path beneath me
2Samuel22:38 I pursued my enemies and crushed them; I did not turn back till they were destroyed.
2Samuel22:39 I crushed them completely
2Samuel22:40 You armed me with strength for battle; you made my adversaries bow at my feet.
2Samuel22:41 You made my enemies turn their backs in flight
2Samuel22:42 They cried for help
2Samuel22:43 I beat them as fine as the dust of the earth; I pounded and trampled them like mud in the streets.
2Samuel22:44 You have delivered me from the attacks of my people; you have preserved me as the head of nations. People I did not know are subject to me
2Samuel22:45 and foreigners come cringing to me; as soon as they hear me
2Samuel22:46 They all lose heart; they come trembling from their strongholds.
2Samuel22:47 The LORD lives! Praise be to my Rock! Exalted be God
2Samuel22:48 He is the God who avenges me
2Samuel22:49 who sets me free from my enemies. You exalted me above my foes; from violent men you rescued me.
2Samuel22:50 Therefore I will praise you
2Samuel22:51 He gives his king great victories; he shows unfailing kindness to his anointed
2Samuel23:1 These are the last words of David: The oracle of David son of Jesse
2Samuel23:2 The Spirit of the LORD spoke through me; his word was on my tongue.
2Samuel23:3 The God of Israel spoke
2Samuel23:4 he is like the light of morning at sunrise on a cloudless morning
2Samuel23:5 Is not my house right with God? Has he not made with me an everlasting covenant
2Samuel23:6 But evil men are all to be cast aside like thorns
2Samuel23:7 Whoever touches thorns uses a tool of iron or the shaft of a spear; they are burned up where they lie.
2Samuel23:8 These are the names of David's mighty men: Josheb-Basshebeth
2Samuel23:9 Next to him was Eleazar son of Dodai the Ahohite. As one of the three mighty men
2Samuel23:10 but he stood his ground and struck down the Philistines till his hand grew tired and froze to the sword. The LORD brought about a great victory that day. The troops returned to Eleazar
2Samuel23:11 Next to him was Shammah son of Agee the Hararite. When the Philistines banded together at a place where there was a field full of lentils
2Samuel23:12 But Shammah took his stand in the middle of the field. He defended it and struck the Philistines down
2Samuel23:13 During harvest time
2Samuel23:14 At that time David was in the stronghold
2Samuel23:15 David longed for water and said
2Samuel23:16 So the three mighty men broke through the Philistine lines
2Samuel23:17 Far be it from me
2Samuel23:18 Abishai the brother of Joab son of Zeruiah was chief of the Three. He raised his spear against three hundred men
2Samuel23:19 Was he not held in greater honor than the Three? He became their commander
2Samuel23:20 Benaiah son of Jehoiada was a valiant fighter from Kabzeel
2Samuel23:21 And he struck down a huge Egyptian. Although the Egyptian had a spear in his hand
2Samuel23:22 Such were the exploits of Benaiah son of Jehoiada; he too was as famous as the three mighty men.
2Samuel23:23 He was held in greater honor than any of the Thirty
2Samuel23:24 Among the Thirty were: Asahel the brother of Joab
2Samuel23:25 Shammah the Harodite
2Samuel23:26 Helez the Paltite
2Samuel23:27 Abiezer from Anathoth
2Samuel23:28 Zalmon the Ahohite
2Samuel23:29 Heled son of Baanah the Netophathite
2Samuel23:30 Benaiah the Pirathonite
2Samuel23:31 Abi-Albon the Arbathite
2Samuel23:32 Eliahba the Shaalbonite
2Samuel23:33 son of Shammah the Hararite
2Samuel23:34 Eliphelet son of Ahasbai the Maacathite
2Samuel23:35 Hezro the Carmelite
2Samuel23:36 Igal son of Nathan from Zobah
2Samuel23:37 Zelek the Ammonite
2Samuel23:38 Ira the Ithrite
2Samuel23:39 and Uriah the Hittite. There were thirty-seven in all.

2Samuel24:8 After they had gone through the entire land

2Samuel24:10 David was conscience-stricken after he had counted the fighting men

2Samuel24:12 Go and tell David
2Samuel24:13 So Gad went to David and said to him
2Samuel24:14 David said to Gad

1Kings3:1 Solomon made an alliance with Pharaoh king of Egypt and married his daughter. He brought her to the City of David until he finished building his palace and the temple of the LORD
1Kings3:2 The people
1Kings3:3 Solomon showed his love for the LORD by walking according to the statutes of his father David
1Kings3:4 The king went to Gibeon to offer sacrifices
1Kings3:5 At Gibeon the LORD appeared to Solomon during the night in a dream
1Kings3:6 Solomon answered
1Kings3:7 Now
1Kings3:8 Your servant is here among the people you have chosen
1Kings3:9 So give your servant a discerning heart to govern your people and to distinguish between right and wrong. For who is able to govern this great people of yours?
1Kings3:10 The Lord was pleased that Solomon had asked for this.
1Kings3:11 So God said to him
1Kings3:12 I will do what you have asked. I will give you a wise and discerning heart
1Kings3:13 Moreover
1Kings3:14 And if you walk in my ways and obey my statutes and commands as David your father did
1Kings3:15 Then Solomon awoke-and he realized it had been a dream. He returned to Jerusalem

1Kings3:17 One of them said

1Kings3:20 So she got up in the middle of the night and took my son from my side while I your servant was asleep. She put him by her breast and put her dead son by my breast.
1Kings3:21 The next morning
1Kings3:22 The other woman said
1Kings3:23 The king said

1Kings3:25 He then gave an order: Cut the living child in two and give half to one and half to the other.
1Kings3:26 The woman whose son was alive was filled with compassion for her son and said to the king
1Kings3:27 Then the king gave his ruling: Give the living baby to the first woman. Do not kill him; she is his mother.
1Kings3:28 When all Israel heard the verdict the king had given
1Kings4:1 So King Solomon ruled over all Israel.
1Kings4:2 And these were his chief officials: Azariah son of Zadok-the priest;
1Kings4:3 Elihoreph and Ahijah
1Kings4:4 Benaiah son of Jehoiada-commander in chief; Zadok and Abiathar-priests;
1Kings4:5 Azariah son of Nathan-in charge of the district officers; Zabud son of Nathan-a priest and personal adviser to the king;
1Kings4:6 Ahishar-in charge of the palace; Adoniram son of Abda-in charge of forced labor.
1Kings4:7 Solomon also had twelve district governors over all Israel
1Kings4:8 These are their names: Ben-Hur-in the hill country of Ephraim;
1Kings4:9 Ben-Deker-in Makaz
1Kings4:10 Ben-Hesed-in Arubboth (Socoh and all the land of Hepher were his);
1Kings4:11 Ben-Abinadab-in Naphoth Dor (he was married to Taphath daughter of Solomon);
1Kings4:12 Baana son of Ahilud-in Taanach and Megiddo
1Kings4:13 Ben-Geber-in Ramoth Gilead (the settlements of Jair son of Manasseh in Gilead were his
1Kings4:14 Ahinadab son of Iddo-in Mahanaim;
1Kings4:15 Ahimaaz-in Naphtali (he had married Basemath daughter of Solomon);
1Kings4:16 Baana son of Hushai-in Asher and in Aloth;
1Kings4:17 Jehoshaphat son of Paruah-in Issachar;
1Kings4:18 Shimei son of Ela-in Benjamin;
1Kings4:19 Geber son of Uri-in Gilead (the country of Sihon king of the Amorites and the country of Og king of Bashan). He was the only governor over the district.
1Kings4:20 The people of Judah and Israel were as numerous as the sand on the seashore; they ate
1Kings4:21 And Solomon ruled over all the kingdoms from the River to the land of the Philistines
1Kings4:22 Solomon's daily provisions were thirty cors of fine flour and sixty cors of meal
1Kings4:23 ten head of stall-fed cattle
1Kings4:24 For he ruled over all the kingdoms west of the River
1Kings4:25 During Solomon's lifetime Judah and Israel
1Kings4:26 Solomon had four thousand stalls for chariot horses
1Kings4:27 The district officers
1Kings4:28 They also brought to the proper place their quotas of barley and straw for the chariot horses and the other horses.
1Kings4:29 God gave Solomon wisdom and very great insight
1Kings4:30 Solomon's wisdom was greater than the wisdom of all the men of the East
1Kings4:31 He was wiser than any other man
1Kings4:32 He spoke three thousand proverbs and his songs numbered a thousand and five.
1Kings4:33 He described plant life
1Kings4:34 Men of all nations came to listen to Solomon's wisdom
1Kings5:1 When Hiram king of Tyre heard that Solomon had been anointed king to succeed his father David
1Kings5:2 Solomon sent back this message to Hiram:
1Kings5:3 You know that because of the wars waged against my father David from all sides
1Kings5:4 But now the LORD my God has given me rest on every side
1Kings5:5 I intend
1Kings5:6 So give orders that cedars of Lebanon be cut for me. My men will work with yours
1Kings5:7 When Hiram heard Solomon's message
1Kings5:8 So Hiram sent word to Solomon: I have received the message you sent me and will do all you want in providing the cedar and pine logs.
1Kings5:9 My men will haul them down from Lebanon to the sea
1Kings5:10 In this way Hiram kept Solomon supplied with all the cedar and pine logs he wanted
1Kings5:11 and Solomon gave Hiram twenty thousand cors of wheat as food for his household
1Kings5:12 The LORD gave Solomon wisdom
1Kings5:13 King Solomon conscripted laborers from all Israel-thirty thousand men.
1Kings5:14 He sent them off to Lebanon in shifts of ten thousand a month
1Kings5:15 Solomon had seventy thousand carriers and eighty thousand stonecutters in the hills
1Kings5:16 as well as thirty-three hundred foremen who supervised the project and directed the workmen.
1Kings5:17 At the king's command they removed from the quarry large blocks of quality stone to provide a foundation of dressed stone for the temple.
1Kings5:18 The craftsmen of Solomon and Hiram and the men of Gebal cut and prepared the timber and stone for the building of the temple.
1Kings6:1 In the four hundred and eightieth year after the Israelites had come out of Egypt
1Kings6:2 The temple that King Solomon built for the LORD was sixty cubits long
1Kings6:3 The portico at the front of the main hall of the temple extended the width of the temple
1Kings6:4 He made narrow clerestory windows in the temple.
1Kings6:5 Against the walls of the main hall and inner sanctuary he built a structure around the building
1Kings6:6 The lowest floor was five cubits wide
1Kings6:7 In building the temple
1Kings6:8 The entrance to the lowest floor was on the south side of the temple; a stairway led up to the middle level and from there to the third.
1Kings6:9 So he built the temple and completed it
1Kings6:10 And he built the side rooms all along the temple. The height of each was five cubits
1Kings6:11 The word of the LORD came to Solomon:
1Kings6:12 As for this temple you are building
1Kings6:13 And I will live among the Israelites and will not abandon my people Israel.
1Kings6:14 So Solomon built the temple and completed it.
1Kings6:15 He lined its interior walls with cedar boards
1Kings6:16 He partitioned off twenty cubits at the rear of the temple with cedar boards from floor to ceiling to form within the temple an inner sanctuary
1Kings6:17 The main hall in front of this room was forty cubits long.
1Kings6:18 The inside of the temple was cedar
1Kings6:19 He prepared the inner sanctuary within the temple to set the ark of the covenant of the LORD there.
1Kings6:20 The inner sanctuary was twenty cubits long
1Kings6:21 Solomon covered the inside of the temple with pure gold
1Kings6:22 So he overlaid the whole interior with gold. He also overlaid with gold the altar that belonged to the inner sanctuary.
1Kings6:23 In the inner sanctuary he made a pair of cherubim of olive wood
1Kings6:24 One wing of the first cherub was five cubits long
1Kings6:25 The second cherub also measured ten cubits
1Kings6:26 The height of each cherub was ten cubits.
1Kings6:27 He placed the cherubim inside the innermost room of the temple
1Kings6:28 He overlaid the cherubim with gold.
1Kings6:29 On the walls all around the temple
1Kings6:30 He also covered the floors of both the inner and outer rooms of the temple with gold.
1Kings6:31 For the entrance of the inner sanctuary he made doors of olive wood with five-sided jambs.
1Kings6:32 And on the two olive wood doors he carved cherubim
1Kings6:33 In the same way he made four-sided jambs of olive wood for the entrance to the main hall.
1Kings6:34 He also made two pine doors
1Kings6:35 He carved cherubim
1Kings6:36 And he built the inner courtyard of three courses of dressed stone and one course of trimmed cedar beams.
1Kings6:37 The foundation of the temple of the LORD was laid in the fourth year
1Kings6:38 In the eleventh year in the month of Bul
1Kings7:1 It took Solomon thirteen years
1Kings7:2 He built the Palace of the Forest of Lebanon a hundred cubits long
1Kings7:3 It was roofed with cedar above the beams that rested on the columns-forty-five beams
1Kings7:4 Its windows were placed high in sets of three
1Kings7:5 All the doorways had rectangular frames; they were in the front part in sets of three
1Kings7:6 He made a colonnade fifty cubits long and thirty wide. In front of it was a portico
1Kings7:7 He built the throne hall
1Kings7:8 And the palace in which he was to live
1Kings7:9 All these structures
1Kings7:10 The foundations were laid with large stones of good quality
1Kings7:11 Above were high-grade stones
1Kings7:12 The great courtyard was surrounded by a wall of three courses of dressed stone and one course of trimmed cedar beams
1Kings7:13 King Solomon sent to Tyre and brought Huram
1Kings7:14 whose mother was a widow from the tribe of Naphtali and whose father was a man of Tyre and a craftsman in bronze. Huram was highly skilled and experienced in all kinds of bronze work. He came to King Solomon and did all the work assigned to him.
1Kings7:15 He cast two bronze pillars
1Kings7:16 He also made two capitals of cast bronze to set on the tops of the pillars; each capital was five cubits high.
1Kings7:17 A network of interwoven chains festooned the capitals on top of the pillars
1Kings7:18 He made pomegranates in two rows encircling each network to decorate the capitals on top of the pillars. He did the same for each capital.
1Kings7:19 The capitals on top of the pillars in the portico were in the shape of lilies
1Kings7:20 On the capitals of both pillars
1Kings7:21 He erected the pillars at the portico of the temple. The pillar to the south he named Jakin and the one to the north Boaz.
1Kings7:22 The capitals on top were in the shape of lilies. And so the work on the pillars was completed.
1Kings7:23 He made the Sea of cast metal
1Kings7:24 Below the rim
1Kings7:25 The Sea stood on twelve bulls
1Kings7:26 It was a handbreadth in thickness
1Kings7:27 He also made ten movable stands of bronze; each was four cubits long
1Kings7:28 This is how the stands were made: They had side panels attached to uprights.
1Kings7:29 On the panels between the uprights were lions
1Kings7:30 Each stand had four bronze wheels with bronze axles
1Kings7:31 On the inside of the stand there was an opening that had a circular frame one cubit deep. This opening was round
1Kings7:32 The four wheels were under the panels
1Kings7:33 The wheels were made like chariot wheels; the axles
1Kings7:34 Each stand had four handles
1Kings7:35 At the top of the stand there was a circular band half a cubit deep. The supports and panels were attached to the top of the stand.
1Kings7:36 He engraved cherubim
1Kings7:37 This is the way he made the ten stands. They were all cast in the same molds and were identical in size and shape.
1Kings7:38 He then made ten bronze basins
1Kings7:39 He placed five of the stands on the south side of the temple and five on the north. He placed the Sea on the south side
1Kings7:40 He also made the basins and shovels and sprinkling bowls. So Huram finished all the work he had undertaken for King Solomon in the temple of the LORD:
1Kings7:41 the two pillars; the two bowl-shaped capitals on top of the pillars; the two sets of network decorating the two bowl-shaped capitals on top of the pillars;
1Kings7:42 the four hundred pomegranates for the two sets of network (two rows of pomegranates for each network
1Kings7:43 the ten stands with their ten basins;
1Kings7:44 the Sea and the twelve bulls under it;
1Kings7:45 the pots
1Kings7:46 The king had them cast in clay molds in the plain of the Jordan between Succoth and Zarethan.
1Kings7:47 Solomon left all these things unweighed
1Kings7:48 Solomon also made all the furnishings that were in the LORD's temple: the golden altar; the golden table on which was the bread of the Presence;
1Kings7:49 the lampstands of pure gold (five on the right and five on the left
1Kings7:50 the pure gold basins
1Kings7:51 When all the work King Solomon had done for the temple of the LORD was finished
1Kings8:1 Then King Solomon summoned into his presence at Jerusalem the elders of Israel
1Kings8:2 All the men of Israel came together to King Solomon at the time of the festival in the month of Ethanim
1Kings8:3 When all the elders of Israel had arrived
1Kings8:4 and they brought up the ark of the LORD and the Tent of Meeting and all the sacred furnishings in it. The priests and Levites carried them up
1Kings8:5 and King Solomon and the entire assembly of Israel that had gathered about him were before the ark
1Kings8:6 The priests then brought the ark of the LORD's covenant to its place in the inner sanctuary of the temple
1Kings8:7 The cherubim spread their wings over the place of the ark and overshadowed the ark and its carrying poles.
1Kings8:8 These poles were so long that their ends could be seen from the Holy Place in front of the inner sanctuary
1Kings8:9 There was nothing in the ark except the two stone tablets that Moses had placed in it at Horeb
1Kings8:10 When the priests withdrew from the Holy Place
1Kings8:11 And the priests could not perform their service because of the cloud
1Kings8:12 Then Solomon said
1Kings8:13 I have indeed built a magnificent temple for you
1Kings8:14 While the whole assembly of Israel was standing there
1Kings8:15 Then he said: Praise be to the LORD
1Kings8:16 'Since the day I brought my people Israel out of Egypt
1Kings8:17 My father David had it in his heart to build a temple for the Name of the LORD
1Kings8:18 But the LORD said to my father David
1Kings8:19 Nevertheless
1Kings8:20 The LORD has kept the promise he made: I have succeeded David my father and now I sit on the throne of Israel
1Kings8:21 I have provided a place there for the ark
1Kings8:22 Then Solomon stood before the altar of the LORD in front of the whole assembly of Israel
1Kings8:23 and said: O LORD
1Kings8:24 You have kept your promise to your servant David my father; with your mouth you have promised and with your hand you have fulfilled it-as it is today.
1Kings8:25 Now LORD
1Kings8:26 And now
1Kings8:27 But will God really dwell on earth? The heavens
1Kings8:28 Yet give attention to your servant's prayer and his plea for mercy
1Kings8:29 May your eyes be open toward this temple night and day
1Kings8:30 Hear the supplication of your servant and of your people Israel when they pray toward this place. Hear from heaven
1Kings8:31 When a man wrongs his neighbor and is required to take an oath and he comes and swears the oath before your altar in this temple
1Kings8:32 then hear from heaven and act. Judge between your servants
1Kings8:33 When your people Israel have been defeated by an enemy because they have sinned against you
1Kings8:34 then hear from heaven and forgive the sin of your people Israel and bring them back to the land you gave to their fathers.
1Kings8:35 When the heavens are shut up and there is no rain because your people have sinned against you
1Kings8:36 then hear from heaven and forgive the sin of your servants
1Kings8:37 When famine or plague comes to the land
1Kings8:38 and when a prayer or plea is made by any of your people Israel-each one aware of the afflictions of his own heart
1Kings8:39 then hear from heaven
1Kings8:40 so that they will fear you all the time they live in the land you gave our fathers.
1Kings8:41 As for the foreigner who does not belong to your people Israel but has come from a distant land because of your name-
1Kings8:42 for men will hear of your great name and your mighty hand and your outstretched arm-when he comes and prays toward this temple
1Kings8:43 then hear from heaven
1Kings8:44 When your people go to war against their enemies
1Kings8:45 then hear from heaven their prayer and their plea
1Kings8:46 When they sin against you-for there is no one who does not sin-and you become angry with them and give them over to the enemy
1Kings8:47 and if they have a change of heart in the land where they are held captive
1Kings8:48 and if they turn back to you with all their heart and soul in the land of their enemies who took them captive
1Kings8:49 then from heaven
1Kings8:50 And forgive your people
1Kings8:51 for they are your people and your inheritance
1Kings8:52 May your eyes be open to your servant's plea and to the plea of your people Israel
1Kings8:53 For you singled them out from all the nations of the world to be your own inheritance
1Kings8:54 When Solomon had finished all these prayers and supplications to the LORD
1Kings8:55 He stood and blessed the whole assembly of Israel in a loud voice
1Kings8:56 Praise be to the LORD
1Kings8:57 May the LORD our God be with us as he was with our fathers; may he never leave us nor forsake us.
1Kings8:58 May he turn our hearts to him
1Kings8:59 And may these words of mine
1Kings8:60 so that all the peoples of the earth may know that the LORD is God and that there is no other.
1Kings8:61 But your hearts must be fully committed to the LORD our God
1Kings8:62 Then the king and all Israel with him offered sacrifices before the LORD.
1Kings8:63 Solomon offered a sacrifice of fellowship offerings to the LORD: twenty-two thousand cattle and a hundred and twenty thousand sheep and goats. So the king and all the Israelites dedicated the temple of the LORD.
1Kings8:64 On that same day the king consecrated the middle part of the courtyard in front of the temple of the LORD
1Kings8:65 So Solomon observed the festival at that time
1Kings8:66 On the following day he sent the people away. They blessed the king and then went home
1Kings9:1 When Solomon had finished building the temple of the LORD and the royal palace
1Kings9:2 the LORD appeared to him a second time
1Kings9:3 The LORD said to him: I have heard the prayer and plea you have made before me; I have consecrated this temple
1Kings9:4 As for you
1Kings9:5 I will establish your royal throne over Israel forever
1Kings9:6 But if you or your sons turn away from me and do not observe the commands and decrees I have given you and go off to serve other gods and worship them
1Kings9:7 then I will cut off Israel from the land I have given them and will reject this temple I have consecrated for my Name. Israel will then become a byword and an object of ridicule among all peoples.
1Kings9:8 And though this temple is now imposing
1Kings9:9 People will answer
1Kings9:10 At the end of twenty years
1Kings9:11 King Solomon gave twenty towns in Galilee to Hiram king of Tyre
1Kings9:12 But when Hiram went from Tyre to see the towns that Solomon had given him
1Kings9:13 What kind of towns are these you have given me
1Kings9:14 Now Hiram had sent to the king 120 talents of gold.
1Kings9:15 Here is the account of the forced labor King Solomon conscripted to build the LORD's temple
1Kings9:16 (Pharaoh king of Egypt had attacked and captured Gezer. He had set it on fire. He killed its Canaanite inhabitants and then gave it as a wedding gift to his daughter
1Kings9:17 And Solomon rebuilt Gezer.) He built up Lower Beth Horon
1Kings9:18 Baalath
1Kings9:19 as well as all his store cities and the towns for his chariots and for his horses -whatever he desired to build in Jerusalem
1Kings9:20 All the people left from the Amorites
1Kings9:21 that is
1Kings9:22 But Solomon did not make slaves of any of the Israelites; they were his fighting men
1Kings9:23 They were also the chief officials in charge of Solomon's projects-550 officials supervising the men who did the work.
1Kings9:24 After Pharaoh's daughter had come up from the City of David to the palace Solomon had built for her
1Kings9:25 Three times a year Solomon sacrificed burnt offerings and fellowship offerings on the altar he had built for the LORD
1Kings9:26 King Solomon also built ships at Ezion Geber
1Kings9:27 And Hiram sent his men-sailors who knew the sea-to serve in the fleet with Solomon's men.
1Kings9:28 They sailed to Ophir and brought back 420 talents of gold
1Kings10:1 When the queen of Sheba heard about the fame of Solomon and his relation to the name of the LORD
1Kings10:2 Arriving at Jerusalem with a very great caravan-with camels carrying spices
1Kings10:3 Solomon answered all her questions; nothing was too hard for the king to explain to her.
1Kings10:4 When the queen of Sheba saw all the wisdom of Solomon and the palace he had built
1Kings10:5 the food on his table
1Kings10:6 She said to the king
1Kings10:7 But I did not believe these things until I came and saw with my own eyes. Indeed
1Kings10:8 How happy your men must be! How happy your officials
1Kings10:9 Praise be to the LORD your God
1Kings10:10 And she gave the king 120 talents of gold
1Kings10:11 (Hiram's ships brought gold from Ophir; and from there they brought great cargoes of almugwood and precious stones.
1Kings10:12 The king used the almugwood to make supports for the temple of the LORD and for the royal palace
1Kings10:13 King Solomon gave the queen of Sheba all she desired and asked for
1Kings10:14 The weight of the gold that Solomon received yearly was 666 talents
1Kings10:15 not including the revenues from merchants and traders and from all the Arabian kings and the governors of the land.
1Kings10:16 King Solomon made two hundred large shields of hammered gold; six hundred bekas of gold went into each shield.
1Kings10:17 He also made three hundred small shields of hammered gold
1Kings10:18 Then the king made a great throne inlaid with ivory and overlaid with fine gold.
1Kings10:19 The throne had six steps
1Kings10:20 Twelve lions stood on the six steps
1Kings10:21 All King Solomon's goblets were gold
1Kings10:22 The king had a fleet of trading ships at sea along with the ships of Hiram. Once every three years it returned
1Kings10:23 King Solomon was greater in riches and wisdom than all the other kings of the earth.
1Kings10:24 The whole world sought audience with Solomon to hear the wisdom God had put in his heart.
1Kings10:25 Year after year
1Kings10:26 Solomon accumulated chariots and horses; he had fourteen hundred chariots and twelve thousand horses
1Kings10:27 The king made silver as common in Jerusalem as stones
1Kings10:28 Solomon's horses were imported from Egypt and from Kue - the royal merchants purchased them from Kue.
1Kings10:29 They imported a chariot from Egypt for six hundred shekels of silver
1Kings11:1 King Solomon
1Kings11:2 They were from nations about which the LORD had told the Israelites
1Kings11:3 He had seven hundred wives of royal birth and three hundred concubines
1Kings11:4 As Solomon grew old
1Kings11:5 He followed Ashtoreth the goddess of the Sidonians
1Kings11:6 So Solomon did evil in the eyes of the LORD; he did not follow the LORD completely
1Kings11:7 On a hill east of Jerusalem
1Kings11:8 He did the same for all his foreign wives
1Kings11:9 The LORD became angry with Solomon because his heart had turned away from the LORD
1Kings11:10 Although he had forbidden Solomon to follow other gods
1Kings11:11 So the LORD said to Solomon
1Kings11:12 Nevertheless
1Kings11:13 Yet I will not tear the whole kingdom from him
1Kings11:14 Then the LORD raised up against Solomon an adversary
1Kings11:15 Earlier when David was fighting with Edom
1Kings11:16 Joab and all the Israelites stayed there for six months
1Kings11:17 But Hadad
1Kings11:18 They set out from Midian and went to Paran. Then taking men from Paran with them
1Kings11:19 Pharaoh was so pleased with Hadad that he gave him a sister of his own wife
1Kings11:20 The sister of Tahpenes bore him a son named Genubath
1Kings11:21 While he was in Egypt
1Kings11:22 What have you lacked here that you want to go back to your own country? Pharaoh asked. Nothing
1Kings11:23 And God raised up against Solomon another adversary
1Kings11:24 He gathered men around him and became the leader of a band of rebels when David destroyed the forces of Zobah; the rebels went to Damascus
1Kings11:25 Rezon was Israel's adversary as long as Solomon lived
1Kings11:26 Also
1Kings11:27 Here is the account of how he rebelled against the king: Solomon had built the supporting terraces and had filled in the gap in the wall of the city of David his father.
1Kings11:28 Now Jeroboam was a man of standing
1Kings11:29 About that time Jeroboam was going out of Jerusalem
1Kings11:30 and Ahijah took hold of the new cloak he was wearing and tore it into twelve pieces.
1Kings11:31 Then he said to Jeroboam
1Kings11:32 But for the sake of my servant David and the city of Jerusalem
1Kings11:33 I will do this because they have forsaken me and worshiped Ashtoreth the goddess of the Sidonians
1Kings11:34 'But I will not take the whole kingdom out of Solomon's hand; I have made him ruler all the days of his life for the sake of David my servant
1Kings11:35 I will take the kingdom from his son's hands and give you ten tribes.
1Kings11:36 I will give one tribe to his son so that David my servant may always have a lamp before me in Jerusalem
1Kings11:37 However
1Kings11:38 If you do whatever I command you and walk in my ways and do what is right in my eyes by keeping my statutes and commands
1Kings11:39 I will humble David's descendants because of this
1Kings11:40 Solomon tried to kill Jeroboam
1Kings11:41 As for the other events of Solomon's reign-all he did and the wisdom he displayed-are they not written in the book of the annals of Solomon?
1Kings11:42 Solomon reigned in Jerusalem over all Israel forty years.
1Kings11:43 Then he rested with his fathers and was buried in the city of David his father. And Rehoboam his son succeeded him as king.
1Kings12:1 Rehoboam went to Shechem
1Kings12:2 When Jeroboam son of Nebat heard this (he was still in Egypt
1Kings12:3 So they sent for Jeroboam
1Kings12:4 Your father put a heavy yoke on us
1Kings12:5 Rehoboam answered
1Kings12:6 Then King Rehoboam consulted the elders who had served his father Solomon during his lifetime. How would you advise me to answer these people? he asked.
1Kings12:7 They replied
1Kings12:8 But Rehoboam rejected the advice the elders gave him and consulted the young men who had grown up with him and were serving him.
1Kings12:9 He asked them
1Kings12:10 The young men who had grown up with him replied

1Kings13:1 By the word of the LORD a man of God came from Judah to Bethel
1Kings13:2 He cried out against the altar by the word of the LORD: O altar
1Kings13:3 That same day the man of God gave a sign: This is the sign the LORD has declared: The altar will be split apart and the ashes on it will be poured out.
1Kings13:4 When King Jeroboam heard what the man of God cried out against the altar at Bethel
1Kings13:5 Also
1Kings13:6 Then the king said to the man of God
1Kings13:7 The king said to the man of God
1Kings13:8 But the man of God answered the king
1Kings13:9 For I was commanded by the word of the LORD: 'You must not eat bread or drink water or return by the way you came.'
1Kings13:10 So he took another road and did not return by the way he had come to Bethel.
1Kings13:11 Now there was a certain old prophet living in Bethel
1Kings13:12 Their father asked them
1Kings13:13 So he said to his sons
1Kings13:14 and rode after the man of God. He found him sitting under an oak tree and asked
1Kings13:15 So the prophet said to him
1Kings13:16 The man of God said
1Kings13:17 I have been told by the word of the LORD: 'You must not eat bread or drink water there or return by the way you came.'
1Kings13:18 The old prophet answered
1Kings13:19 So the man of God returned with him and ate and drank in his house.
1Kings13:20 While they were sitting at the table
1Kings13:21 He cried out to the man of God who had come from Judah
1Kings13:22 You came back and ate bread and drank water in the place where he told you not to eat or drink. Therefore your body will not be buried in the tomb of your fathers.'
1Kings13:23 When the man of God had finished eating and drinking
1Kings13:24 As he went on his way
1Kings13:25 Some people who passed by saw the body thrown down there
1Kings13:26 When the prophet who had brought him back from his journey heard of it
1Kings13:27 The prophet said to his sons
1Kings13:28 Then he went out and found the body thrown down on the road
1Kings13:29 So the prophet picked up the body of the man of God
1Kings13:30 Then he laid the body in his own tomb
1Kings13:31 After burying him
1Kings13:32 For the message he declared by the word of the LORD against the altar in Bethel and against all the shrines on the high places in the towns of Samaria will certainly come true.
1Kings13:33 Even after this
1Kings13:34 This was the sin of the house of Jeroboam that led to its downfall and to its destruction from the face of the earth.

1Kings14:2 and Jeroboam said to his wife

1Kings14:21 Rehoboam son of Solomon was king in Judah. He was forty-one years old when he became king
1Kings14:22 Judah did evil in the eyes of the LORD. By the sins they committed they stirred up his jealous anger more than their fathers had done.
1Kings14:23 They also set up for themselves high places
1Kings14:24 There were even male shrine prostitutes in the land; the people engaged in all the detestable practices of the nations the LORD had driven out before the Israelites.

1Kings15:1 In the eighteenth year of the reign of Jeroboam son of Nebat
1Kings15:2 and he reigned in Jerusalem three years. His mother's name was Maacah daughter of Abishalom.
1Kings15:3 He committed all the sins his father had done before him; his heart was not fully devoted to the LORD his God
1Kings15:4 Nevertheless
1Kings15:5 For David had done what was right in the eyes of the LORD and had not failed to keep any of the LORD's commands all the days of his life-except in the case of Uriah the Hittite.
1Kings15:6 There was war between Rehoboam and Jeroboam throughout Abijah's lifetime.
1Kings15:7 As for the other events of Abijah's reign
1Kings15:8 And Abijah rested with his fathers and was buried in the City of David. And Asa his son succeeded him as king.
1Kings15:9 In the twentieth year of Jeroboam king of Israel
1Kings15:10 and he reigned in Jerusalem forty-one years. His grandmother's name was Maacah daughter of Abishalom.
1Kings15:11 Asa did what was right in the eyes of the LORD
1Kings15:12 He expelled the male shrine prostitutes from the land and got rid of all the idols his fathers had made.
1Kings15:13 He even deposed his grandmother Maacah from her position as queen mother
1Kings15:14 Although he did not remove the high places
1Kings15:15 He brought into the temple of the LORD the silver and gold and the articles that he and his father had dedicated.
1Kings15:16 There was war between Asa and Baasha king of Israel throughout their reigns.
1Kings15:17 Baasha king of Israel went up against Judah and fortified Ramah to prevent anyone from leaving or entering the territory of Asa king of Judah.
1Kings15:18 Asa then took all the silver and gold that was left in the treasuries of the LORD's temple and of his own palace. He entrusted it to his officials and sent them to Ben-Hadad son of Tabrimmon
1Kings15:19 Let there be a treaty between me and you
1Kings15:20 Ben-Hadad agreed with King Asa and sent the commanders of his forces against the towns of Israel. He conquered Ijon
1Kings15:21 When Baasha heard this
1Kings15:22 Then King Asa issued an order to all Judah-no one was exempt-and they carried away from Ramah the stones and timber Baasha had been using there. With them King Asa built up Geba in Benjamin
1Kings15:23 As for all the other events of Asa's reign
1Kings15:24 Then Asa rested with his fathers and was buried with them in the city of his father David. And Jehoshaphat his son succeeded him as king.
1Kings15:25 Nadab son of Jeroboam became king of Israel in the second year of Asa king of Judah
1Kings15:26 He did evil in the eyes of the LORD
1Kings15:27 Baasha son of Ahijah of the house of Issachar plotted against him
1Kings15:28 Baasha killed Nadab in the third year of Asa king of Judah and succeeded him as king.
1Kings15:29 As soon as he began to reign
1Kings15:30 because of the sins Jeroboam had committed and had caused Israel to commit
1Kings15:31 As for the other events of Nadab's reign
1Kings15:32 There was war between Asa and Baasha king of Israel throughout their reigns.
1Kings15:33 In the third year of Asa king of Judah
1Kings15:34 He did evil in the eyes of the LORD
1Kings16:1 Then the word of the LORD came to Jehu son of Hanani against Baasha:
1Kings16:2 I lifted you up from the dust and made you leader of my people Israel
1Kings16:3 So I am about to consume Baasha and his house
1Kings16:4 Dogs will eat those belonging to Baasha who die in the city

1Kings16:7 Moreover
