1Kings16:15 In the twenty-seventh year of Asa king of Judah
1Kings16:16 When the Israelites in the camp heard that Zimri had plotted against the king and murdered him

1Kings16:19 because of the sins he had committed
1Kings16:20 As for the other events of Zimri's reign
1Kings16:21 Then the people of Israel were split into two factions; half supported Tibni son of Ginath for king
1Kings16:22 But Omri's followers proved stronger than those of Tibni son of Ginath. So Tibni died and Omri became king.
1Kings16:23 In the thirty-first year of Asa king of Judah
1Kings16:24 He bought the hill of Samaria from Shemer for two talents of silver and built a city on the hill
1Kings16:25 But Omri did evil in the eyes of the LORD and sinned more than all those before him.
1Kings16:26 He walked in all the ways of Jeroboam son of Nebat and in his sin
1Kings16:27 As for the other events of Omri's reign
1Kings16:28 Omri rested with his fathers and was buried in Samaria. And Ahab his son succeeded him as king.
1Kings16:29 In the thirty-eighth year of Asa king of Judah
1Kings16:30 Ahab son of Omri did more evil in the eyes of the LORD than any of those before him.
1Kings16:31 He not only considered it trivial to commit the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat
1Kings16:32 He set up an altar for Baal in the temple of Baal that he built in Samaria.
1Kings16:33 Ahab also made an Asherah pole and did more to provoke the LORD
1Kings16:34 In Ahab's time
1Kings17:1 Now Elijah the Tishbite
1Kings17:2 Then the word of the LORD came to Elijah:
1Kings17:3 Leave here
1Kings17:4 You will drink from the brook
1Kings17:5 So he did what the LORD had told him. He went to the Kerith Ravine
1Kings17:6 The ravens brought him bread and meat in the morning and bread and meat in the evening
1Kings17:7 Some time later the brook dried up because there had been no rain in the land.
1Kings17:8 Then the word of the LORD came to him:

2Kings17:1 In the twelfth year of Ahaz king of Judah
2Kings17:2 He did evil in the eyes of the LORD
2Kings17:3 Shalmaneser king of Assyria came up to attack Hoshea
2Kings17:4 But the king of Assyria discovered that Hoshea was a traitor
2Kings17:5 The king of Assyria invaded the entire land
2Kings17:6 In the ninth year of Hoshea
2Kings17:7 All this took place because the Israelites had sinned against the LORD their God
2Kings17:8 and followed the practices of the nations the LORD had driven out before them
2Kings17:9 The Israelites secretly did things against the LORD their God that were not right. From watchtower to fortified city they built themselves high places in all their towns.
2Kings17:10 They set up sacred stones and Asherah poles on every high hill and under every spreading tree.
2Kings17:11 At every high place they burned incense
2Kings17:12 They worshiped idols
2Kings17:13 The LORD warned Israel and Judah through all his prophets and seers: Turn from your evil ways. Observe my commands and decrees
2Kings17:14 But they would not listen and were as stiff-necked as their fathers
2Kings17:15 They rejected his decrees and the covenant he had made with their fathers and the warnings he had given them. They followed worthless idols and themselves became worthless. They imitated the nations around them although the LORD had ordered them
2Kings17:16 They forsook all the commands of the LORD their God and made for themselves two idols cast in the shape of calves
2Kings17:17 They sacrificed their sons and daughters in the fire. They practiced divination and sorcery and sold themselves to do evil in the eyes of the LORD
2Kings17:18 So the LORD was very angry with Israel and removed them from his presence. Only the tribe of Judah was left
2Kings17:19 and even Judah did not keep the commands of the LORD their God. They followed the practices Israel had introduced.
2Kings17:20 Therefore the LORD rejected all the people of Israel; he afflicted them and gave them into the hands of plunderers
2Kings17:21 When he tore Israel away from the house of David
2Kings17:22 The Israelites persisted in all the sins of Jeroboam and did not turn away from them
2Kings17:23 until the LORD removed them from his presence
2Kings17:24 The king of Assyria brought people from Babylon
2Kings17:25 When they first lived there
2Kings17:26 It was reported to the king of Assyria: The people you deported and resettled in the towns of Samaria do not know what the god of that country requires. He has sent lions among them
2Kings17:27 Then the king of Assyria gave this order: Have one of the priests you took captive from Samaria go back to live there and teach the people what the god of the land requires.
2Kings17:28 So one of the priests who had been exiled from Samaria came to live in Bethel and taught them how to worship the LORD.
2Kings17:29 Nevertheless
2Kings17:30 The men from Babylon made Succoth Benoth
2Kings17:31 the Avvites made Nibhaz and Tartak
2Kings17:32 They worshiped the LORD
2Kings17:33 They worshiped the LORD
2Kings17:34 To this day they persist in their former practices. They neither worship the LORD nor adhere to the decrees and ordinances
2Kings17:35 When the LORD made a covenant with the Israelites
2Kings17:36 But the LORD
2Kings17:37 You must always be careful to keep the decrees and ordinances
2Kings17:38 Do not forget the covenant I have made with you
2Kings17:39 Rather
2Kings17:40 They would not listen
2Kings17:41 Even while these people were worshiping the LORD

2Kings18:2 He was twenty-five years old when he became king
2Kings18:3 He did what was right in the eyes of the LORD
2Kings18:4 He removed the high places
2Kings18:5 Hezekiah trusted in the LORD
2Kings18:6 He held fast to the LORD and did not cease to follow him; he kept the commands the LORD had given Moses.
2Kings18:7 And the LORD was with him; he was successful in whatever he undertook. He rebelled against the king of Assyria and did not serve him.
2Kings18:8 From watchtower to fortified city
2Kings18:9 In King Hezekiah's fourth year
2Kings18:10 At the end of three years the Assyrians took it. So Samaria was captured in Hezekiah's sixth year
2Kings18:11 The king of Assyria deported Israel to Assyria and settled them in Halah
2Kings18:12 This happened because they had not obeyed the LORD their God
2Kings18:13 In the fourteenth year of King Hezekiah's reign
2Kings18:14 So Hezekiah king of Judah sent this message to the king of Assyria at Lachish: I have done wrong. Withdraw from me
2Kings18:15 So Hezekiah gave him all the silver that was found in the temple of the LORD and in the treasuries of the royal palace.
2Kings18:16 At this time Hezekiah king of Judah stripped off the gold with which he had covered the doors and doorposts of the temple of the LORD
2Kings18:17 The king of Assyria sent his supreme commander
2Kings18:18 They called for the king; and Eliakim son of Hilkiah the palace administrator
2Kings18:19 The field commander said to them
2Kings18:20 You say you have strategy and military strength-but you speak only empty words. On whom are you depending
2Kings18:21 Look now
2Kings18:22 And if you say to me
2Kings18:23 'Come now
2Kings18:24 How can you repulse one officer of the least of my master's officials
2Kings18:25 Furthermore
2Kings18:26 Then Eliakim son of Hilkiah
2Kings18:27 But the commander replied
2Kings18:28 Then the commander stood and called out in Hebrew: Hear the word of the great king
2Kings18:29 This is what the king says: Do not let Hezekiah deceive you. He cannot deliver you from my hand.
2Kings18:30 Do not let Hezekiah persuade you to trust in the LORD when he says
2Kings18:31 Do not listen to Hezekiah. This is what the king of Assyria says: Make peace with me and come out to me. Then every one of you will eat from his own vine and fig tree and drink water from his own cistern
2Kings18:32 until I come and take you to a land like your own
2Kings18:33 Has the god of any nation ever delivered his land from the hand of the king of Assyria?
2Kings18:34 Where are the gods of Hamath and Arpad? Where are the gods of Sepharvaim
2Kings18:35 Who of all the gods of these countries has been able to save his land from me? How then can the LORD deliver Jerusalem from my hand?
2Kings18:36 But the people remained silent and said nothing in reply

2Kings19:21 This is the word that the LORD has spoken against him: 'The Virgin Daughter of Zion despises you and mocks you. The Daughter of Jerusalem tosses her head as you flee.
2Kings19:22 Who is it you have insulted and blasphemed? Against whom have you raised your voice and lifted your eyes in pride? Against the Holy One of Israel!
2Kings19:23 By your messengers you have heaped insults on the Lord. And you have said
2Kings19:24 I have dug wells in foreign lands and drunk the water there. With the soles of my feet I have dried up all the streams of Egypt.
2Kings19:25 'Have you not heard? Long ago I ordained it. In days of old I planned it; now I have brought it to pass
2Kings19:26 Their people

2Kings19:28 Because you rage against me and your insolence has reached my ears

2Kings19:30 Once more a remnant of the house of Judah will take root below and bear fruit above.
2Kings19:31 For out of Jerusalem will come a remnant
2Kings19:32 Therefore this is what the LORD says concerning the king of Assyria: He will not enter this city or shoot an arrow here. He will not come before it with shield or build a siege ramp against it.
2Kings19:33 By the way that he came he will return; he will not enter this city
2Kings19:34 I will defend this city and save it
2Kings19:35 That night the angel of the LORD went out and put to death a hundred and eighty-five thousand men in the Assyrian camp. When the people got up the next morning-there were all the dead bodies!
2Kings19:36 So Sennacherib king of Assyria broke camp and withdrew. He returned to Nineveh and stayed there.

2Kings21:1 Manasseh was twelve years old when he became king

2Kings21:26 He was buried in his grave in the garden of Uzza. And Josiah his son succeeded him as king.

2Kings23:1 Then the king called together all the elders of Judah and Jerusalem.
2Kings23:2 He went up to the temple of the LORD with the men of Judah

2Kings23:4 The king ordered Hilkiah the high priest
2Kings23:5 He did away with the pagan priests appointed by the kings of Judah to burn incense on the high places of the towns of Judah and on those around Jerusalem-those who burned incense to Baal
2Kings23:6 He took the Asherah pole from the temple of the LORD to the Kidron Valley outside Jerusalem and burned it there. He ground it to powder and scattered the dust over the graves of the common people.
2Kings23:7 He also tore down the quarters of the male shrine prostitutes
2Kings23:8 Josiah brought all the priests from the towns of Judah and desecrated the high places
2Kings23:9 Although the priests of the high places did not serve at the altar of the LORD in Jerusalem
2Kings23:10 He desecrated Topheth
2Kings23:11 He removed from the entrance to the temple of the LORD the horses that the kings of Judah had dedicated to the sun. They were in the court near the room of an official named Nathan-Melech. Josiah then burned the chariots dedicated to the sun.
2Kings23:12 He pulled down the altars the kings of Judah had erected on the roof near the upper room of Ahaz
2Kings23:13 The king also desecrated the high places that were east of Jerusalem on the south of the Hill of Corruption-the ones Solomon king of Israel had built for Ashtoreth the vile goddess of the Sidonians

1Chronicles16:7 That day David first committed to Asaph and his associates this psalm of thanks to the LORD:
1Chronicles16:8 Give thanks to the LORD
1Chronicles16:9 Sing to him
1Chronicles16:10 Glory in his holy name; let the hearts of those who seek the LORD rejoice.
1Chronicles16:11 Look to the LORD and his strength; seek his face always.
1Chronicles16:12 Remember the wonders he has done
1Chronicles16:13 O descendants of Israel his servant
1Chronicles16:14 He is the LORD our God; his judgments are in all the earth.
1Chronicles16:15 He remembers his covenant forever
1Chronicles16:16 the covenant he made with Abraham
1Chronicles16:17 He confirmed it to Jacob as a decree
1Chronicles16:18 To you I will give the land of Canaan as the portion you will inherit.
1Chronicles16:19 When they were but few in number
1Chronicles16:20 they wandered from nation to nation
1Chronicles16:21 He allowed no man to oppress them; for their sake he rebuked kings:
1Chronicles16:22 Do not touch my anointed ones; do my prophets no harm.
1Chronicles16:23 Sing to the LORD
1Chronicles16:24 Declare his glory among the nations
1Chronicles16:25 For great is the LORD and most worthy of praise; he is to be feared above all gods.
1Chronicles16:26 For all the gods of the nations are idols
1Chronicles16:27 Splendor and majesty are before him; strength and joy in his dwelling place.
1Chronicles16:28 Ascribe to the LORD
1Chronicles16:29 ascribe to the LORD the glory due his name. Bring an offering and come before him; worship the LORD in the splendor of his holiness.
1Chronicles16:30 Tremble before him
1Chronicles16:31 Let the heavens rejoice
1Chronicles16:32 Let the sea resound
1Chronicles16:33 Then the trees of the forest will sing
1Chronicles16:34 Give thanks to the LORD
1Chronicles16:35 Cry out
1Chronicles16:36 Praise be to the LORD
1Chronicles16:37 David left Asaph and his associates before the ark of the covenant of the LORD to minister there regularly
1Chronicles16:38 He also left Obed-Edom and his sixty-eight associates to minister with them. Obed-Edom son of Jeduthun
1Chronicles16:39 David left Zadok the priest and his fellow priests before the tabernacle of the LORD at the high place in Gibeon
1Chronicles16:40 to present burnt offerings to the LORD on the altar of burnt offering regularly
1Chronicles16:41 With them were Heman and Jeduthun and the rest of those chosen and designated by name to give thanks to the LORD
1Chronicles16:42 Heman and Jeduthun were responsible for the sounding of the trumpets and cymbals and for the playing of the other instruments for sacred song. The sons of Jeduthun were stationed at the gate.
1Chronicles16:43 Then all the people left
1Chronicles17:1 After David was settled in his palace
1Chronicles17:2 Nathan replied to David
1Chronicles17:3 That night the word of God came to Nathan
1Chronicles17:4 Go and tell my servant David
1Chronicles17:5 I have not dwelt in a house from the day I brought Israel up out of Egypt to this day. I have moved from one tent site to another
1Chronicles17:6 Wherever I have moved with all the Israelites
1Chronicles17:7 Now then
1Chronicles17:8 I have been with you wherever you have gone
1Chronicles17:9 And I will provide a place for my people Israel and will plant them so that they can have a home of their own and no longer be disturbed. Wicked people will not oppress them anymore
1Chronicles17:10 and have done ever since the time I appointed leaders over my people Israel. I will also subdue all your enemies. 'I declare to you that the LORD will build a house for you:
1Chronicles17:11 When your days are over and you go to be with your fathers
1Chronicles17:12 He is the one who will build a house for me
1Chronicles17:13 I will be his father
1Chronicles17:14 I will set him over my house and my kingdom forever; his throne will be established forever.'
1Chronicles17:15 Nathan reported to David all the words of this entire revelation.
1Chronicles17:16 Then King David went in and sat before the LORD
1Chronicles17:17 And as if this were not enough in your sight
1Chronicles17:18 What more can David say to you for honoring your servant? For you know your servant
1Chronicles17:19 O LORD. For the sake of your servant and according to your will
1Chronicles17:20 There is no one like you
1Chronicles17:21 And who is like your people Israel-the one nation on earth whose God went out to redeem a people for himself
1Chronicles17:22 You made your people Israel your very own forever
1Chronicles17:23 And now
1Chronicles17:24 so that it will be established and that your name will be great forever. Then men will say
1Chronicles17:25 You
1Chronicles17:26 O LORD
1Chronicles17:27 Now you have been pleased to bless the house of your servant

1Chronicles23:25 For David had said

1Chronicles23:29 They were in charge of the bread set out on the table
1Chronicles23:30 They were also to stand every morning to thank and praise the LORD. They were to do the same in the evening
1Chronicles23:31 and whenever burnt offerings were presented to the LORD on Sabbaths and at New Moon festivals and at appointed feasts. They were to serve before the LORD regularly in the proper number and in the way prescribed for them.

1Chronicles29:23 So Solomon sat on the throne of the LORD as king in place of his father David. He prospered and all Israel obeyed him.
1Chronicles29:24 All the officers and mighty men
1Chronicles29:25 The LORD highly exalted Solomon in the sight of all Israel and bestowed on him royal splendor such as no king over Israel ever had before.
1Chronicles29:26 David son of Jesse was king over all Israel.
1Chronicles29:27 He ruled over Israel forty years-seven in Hebron and thirty-three in Jerusalem.
1Chronicles29:28 He died at a good old age
1Chronicles29:29 As for the events of King David's reign
1Chronicles29:30 together with the details of his reign and power
2Chronicles1:1 Solomon son of David established himself firmly over his kingdom
2Chronicles1:2 Then Solomon spoke to all Israel-to the commanders of thousands and commanders of hundreds
2Chronicles1:3 and Solomon and the whole assembly went to the high place at Gibeon
2Chronicles1:4 Now David had brought up the ark of God from Kiriath Jearim to the place he had prepared for it
2Chronicles1:5 But the bronze altar that Bezalel son of Uri
2Chronicles1:6 Solomon went up to the bronze altar before the LORD in the Tent of Meeting and offered a thousand burnt offerings on it.
2Chronicles1:7 That night God appeared to Solomon and said to him
2Chronicles1:8 Solomon answered God
2Chronicles1:9 Now
2Chronicles1:10 Give me wisdom and knowledge
2Chronicles1:11 God said to Solomon
2Chronicles1:12 therefore wisdom and knowledge will be given you. And I will also give you wealth
2Chronicles1:13 Then Solomon went to Jerusalem from the high place at Gibeon
2Chronicles1:14 Solomon accumulated chariots and horses; he had fourteen hundred chariots and twelve thousand horses
2Chronicles1:15 The king made silver and gold as common in Jerusalem as stones
2Chronicles1:16 Solomon's horses were imported from Egypt and from Kue - the royal merchants purchased them from Kue.
2Chronicles1:17 They imported a chariot from Egypt for six hundred shekels of silver
2Chronicles2:1 Solomon gave orders to build a temple for the Name of the LORD and a royal palace for himself.
2Chronicles2:2 He conscripted seventy thousand men as carriers and eighty thousand as stonecutters in the hills and thirty-six hundred as foremen over them.
2Chronicles2:3 Solomon sent this message to Hiram king of Tyre: Send me cedar logs as you did for my father David when you sent him cedar to build a palace to live in.
2Chronicles2:4 Now I am about to build a temple for the Name of the LORD my God and to dedicate it to him for burning fragrant incense before him
2Chronicles2:5 The temple I am going to build will be great
2Chronicles2:6 But who is able to build a temple for him
2Chronicles2:7 Send me
2Chronicles2:8 Send me also cedar
2Chronicles2:9 to provide me with plenty of lumber
2Chronicles2:10 I will give your servants
2Chronicles2:11 Hiram king of Tyre replied by letter to Solomon: Because the LORD loves his people
2Chronicles2:12 And Hiram added: Praise be to the LORD
2Chronicles2:13 I am sending you Huram-Abi
2Chronicles2:14 whose mother was from Dan and whose father was from Tyre. He is trained to work in gold and silver
2Chronicles2:15 Now let my Lord send his servants the wheat and barley and the olive oil and wine he promised
2Chronicles2:16 and we will cut all the logs from Lebanon that you need and will float them in rafts by sea down to Joppa. You can then take them up to Jerusalem.
2Chronicles2:17 Solomon took a census of all the aliens who were in Israel
2Chronicles2:18 He assigned 70
2Chronicles3:1 Then Solomon began to build the temple of the LORD in Jerusalem on Mount Moriah
2Chronicles3:2 He began building on the second day of the second month in the fourth year of his reign.
2Chronicles3:3 The foundation Solomon laid for building the temple of God was sixty cubits long and twenty cubits wide (using the cubit of the old standard).
2Chronicles3:4 The portico at the front of the temple was twenty cubits long across the width of the building and twenty cubits high. He overlaid the inside with pure gold.
2Chronicles3:5 He paneled the main hall with pine and covered it with fine gold and decorated it with palm tree and chain designs.
2Chronicles3:6 He adorned the temple with precious stones. And the gold he used was gold of Parvaim.
2Chronicles3:7 He overlaid the ceiling beams
2Chronicles3:8 He built the Most Holy Place
2Chronicles3:9 The gold nails weighed fifty shekels. He also overlaid the upper parts with gold.
2Chronicles3:10 In the Most Holy Place he made a pair of sculptured cherubim and overlaid them with gold.
2Chronicles3:11 The total wingspan of the cherubim was twenty cubits. One wing of the first cherub was five cubits long and touched the temple wall
2Chronicles3:12 Similarly one wing of the second cherub was five cubits long and touched the other temple wall
2Chronicles3:13 The wings of these cherubim extended twenty cubits. They stood on their feet
2Chronicles3:14 He made the curtain of blue
2Chronicles3:15 In the front of the temple he made two pillars
2Chronicles3:16 He made interwoven chains and put them on top of the pillars. He also made a hundred pomegranates and attached them to the chains.
2Chronicles3:17 He erected the pillars in the front of the temple
2Chronicles4:1 He made a bronze altar twenty cubits long
2Chronicles4:2 He made the Sea of cast metal
2Chronicles4:3 Below the rim
2Chronicles4:4 The Sea stood on twelve bulls
2Chronicles4:5 It was a handbreadth in thickness
2Chronicles4:6 He then made ten basins for washing and placed five on the south side and five on the north. In them the things to be used for the burnt offerings were rinsed
2Chronicles4:7 He made ten gold lampstands according to the specifications for them and placed them in the temple
2Chronicles4:8 He made ten tables and placed them in the temple
2Chronicles4:9 He made the courtyard of the priests
2Chronicles4:10 He placed the Sea on the south side
2Chronicles4:11 He also made the pots and shovels and sprinkling bowls. So Huram finished the work he had undertaken for King Solomon in the temple of God:
2Chronicles4:12 the two pillars; the two bowl-shaped capitals on top of the pillars; the two sets of network decorating the two bowl-shaped capitals on top of the pillars;
2Chronicles4:13 the four hundred pomegranates for the two sets of network (two rows of pomegranates for each network
2Chronicles4:14 the stands with their basins;
2Chronicles4:15 the Sea and the twelve bulls under it;
2Chronicles4:16 the pots
2Chronicles4:17 The king had them cast in clay molds in the plain of the Jordan between Succoth and Zarethan.
2Chronicles4:18 All these things that Solomon made amounted to so much that the weight of the bronze was not determined.
2Chronicles4:19 Solomon also made all the furnishings that were in God's temple: the golden altar; the tables on which was the bread of the Presence;
2Chronicles4:20 the lampstands of pure gold with their lamps
2Chronicles4:21 the gold floral work and lamps and tongs (they were solid gold);
2Chronicles4:22 the pure gold wick trimmers

2Chronicles5:2 Then Solomon summoned to Jerusalem the elders of Israel

2Chronicles5:5 and they brought up the ark and the Tent of Meeting and all the sacred furnishings in it. The priests
2Chronicles5:6 and King Solomon and the entire assembly of Israel that had gathered about him were before the ark

2Chronicles5:9 These poles were so long that their ends
2Chronicles5:10 There was nothing in the ark except the two tablets that Moses had placed in it at Horeb

2Chronicles5:12 All the Levites who were musicians-Asaph

2Chronicles5:14 and the priests could not perform their service because of the cloud
2Chronicles6:1 Then Solomon said
2Chronicles6:2 I have built a magnificent temple for you
2Chronicles6:3 While the whole assembly of Israel was standing there
2Chronicles6:4 Then he said: Praise be to the LORD
2Chronicles6:5 'Since the day I brought my people out of Egypt
2Chronicles6:6 But now I have chosen Jerusalem for my Name to be there
2Chronicles6:7 My father David had it in his heart to build a temple for the Name of the LORD
2Chronicles6:8 But the LORD said to my father David
2Chronicles6:9 Nevertheless
2Chronicles6:10 The LORD has kept the promise he made. I have succeeded David my father and now I sit on the throne of Israel
2Chronicles6:11 There I have placed the ark
2Chronicles6:12 Then Solomon stood before the altar of the LORD in front of the whole assembly of Israel and spread out his hands.
2Chronicles6:13 Now he had made a bronze platform
2Chronicles6:14 He said: O LORD
2Chronicles6:15 You have kept your promise to your servant David my father; with your mouth you have promised and with your hand you have fulfilled it-as it is today.
2Chronicles6:16 Now LORD
2Chronicles6:17 And now
2Chronicles6:18 But will God really dwell on earth with men? The heavens
2Chronicles6:19 Yet give attention to your servant's prayer and his plea for mercy
2Chronicles6:20 May your eyes be open toward this temple day and night
2Chronicles6:21 Hear the supplications of your servant and of your people Israel when they pray toward this place. Hear from heaven
2Chronicles6:22 When a man wrongs his neighbor and is required to take an oath and he comes and swears the oath before your altar in this temple
2Chronicles6:23 then hear from heaven and act. Judge between your servants
2Chronicles6:24 When your people Israel have been defeated by an enemy because they have sinned against you and when they turn back and confess your name
2Chronicles6:25 then hear from heaven and forgive the sin of your people Israel and bring them back to the land you gave to them and their fathers.
2Chronicles6:26 When the heavens are shut up and there is no rain because your people have sinned against you
2Chronicles6:27 then hear from heaven and forgive the sin of your servants
2Chronicles6:28 When famine or plague comes to the land
2Chronicles6:29 and when a prayer or plea is made by any of your people Israel-each one aware of his afflictions and pains
2Chronicles6:30 then hear from heaven
2Chronicles6:31 so that they will fear you and walk in your ways all the time they live in the land you gave our fathers.
2Chronicles6:32 As for the foreigner who does not belong to your people Israel but has come from a distant land because of your great name and your mighty hand and your outstretched arm-when he comes and prays toward this temple
2Chronicles6:33 then hear from heaven
2Chronicles6:34 When your people go to war against their enemies
2Chronicles6:35 then hear from heaven their prayer and their plea
2Chronicles6:36 When they sin against you-for there is no one who does not sin-and you become angry with them and give them over to the enemy
2Chronicles6:37 and if they have a change of heart in the land where they are held captive
2Chronicles6:38 and if they turn back to you with all their heart and soul in the land of their captivity where they were taken
2Chronicles6:39 then from heaven
2Chronicles6:40 Now
2Chronicles6:41 Now arise
2Chronicles6:42 O LORD God
2Chronicles7:1 When Solomon finished praying
2Chronicles7:2 The priests could not enter the temple of the LORD because the glory of the LORD filled it.
2Chronicles7:3 When all the Israelites saw the fire coming down and the glory of the LORD above the temple
2Chronicles7:4 Then the king and all the people offered sacrifices before the LORD.
2Chronicles7:5 And King Solomon offered a sacrifice of twenty-two thousand head of cattle and a hundred and twenty thousand sheep and goats. So the king and all the people dedicated the temple of God.
2Chronicles7:6 The priests took their positions
2Chronicles7:7 Solomon consecrated the middle part of the courtyard in front of the temple of the LORD
2Chronicles7:8 So Solomon observed the festival at that time for seven days
2Chronicles7:9 On the eighth day they held an assembly
2Chronicles7:10 On the twenty-third day of the seventh month he sent the people to their homes
2Chronicles7:11 When Solomon had finished the temple of the LORD and the royal palace
2Chronicles7:12 the LORD appeared to him at night and said: I have heard your prayer and have chosen this place for myself as a temple for sacrifices.
2Chronicles7:13 When I shut up the heavens so that there is no rain
2Chronicles7:14 if my people
2Chronicles7:15 Now my eyes will be open and my ears attentive to the prayers offered in this place.
2Chronicles7:16 I have chosen and consecrated this temple so that my Name may be there forever. My eyes and my heart will always be there.
2Chronicles7:17 As for you
2Chronicles7:18 I will establish your royal throne
2Chronicles7:19 But if you turn away and forsake the decrees and commands I have given you and go off to serve other gods and worship them
2Chronicles7:20 then I will uproot Israel from my land
2Chronicles7:21 And though this temple is now so imposing
2Chronicles7:22 People will answer
2Chronicles8:1 At the end of twenty years
2Chronicles8:2 Solomon rebuilt the villages that Hiram had given him
2Chronicles8:3 Solomon then went to Hamath Zobah and captured it.
2Chronicles8:4 He also built up Tadmor in the desert and all the store cities he had built in Hamath.
2Chronicles8:5 He rebuilt Upper Beth Horon and Lower Beth Horon as fortified cities
2Chronicles8:6 as well as Baalath and all his store cities
2Chronicles8:7 All the people left from the Hittites
2Chronicles8:8 that is
2Chronicles8:9 But Solomon did not make slaves of the Israelites for his work; they were his fighting men
2Chronicles8:10 They were also King Solomon's chief officials-two hundred and fifty officials supervising the men.

2Chronicles8:14 In keeping with the ordinance of his father David
2Chronicles8:15 They did not deviate from the king's commands to the priests or to the Levites in any matter
2Chronicles8:16 All Solomon's work was carried out

2Chronicles9:1 When the queen of Sheba heard of Solomon's fame
2Chronicles9:2 Solomon answered all her questions; nothing was too hard for him to explain to her.
2Chronicles9:3 When the queen of Sheba saw the wisdom of Solomon
2Chronicles9:4 the food on his table
2Chronicles9:5 She said to the king
2Chronicles9:6 But I did not believe what they said until I came and saw with my own eyes. Indeed
2Chronicles9:7 How happy your men must be! How happy your officials
2Chronicles9:8 Praise be to the LORD your God
2Chronicles9:9 Then she gave the king 120 talents of gold
2Chronicles9:10 (The men of Hiram and the men of Solomon brought gold from Ophir; they also brought algumwood and precious stones.
2Chronicles9:11 The king used the algumwood to make steps for the temple of the LORD and for the royal palace
2Chronicles9:12 King Solomon gave the queen of Sheba all she desired and asked for; he gave her more than she had brought to him. Then she left and returned with her retinue to her own country.
2Chronicles9:13 The weight of the gold that Solomon received yearly was 666 talents
2Chronicles9:14 not including the revenues brought in by merchants and traders. Also all the kings of Arabia and the governors of the land brought gold and silver to Solomon.
2Chronicles9:15 King Solomon made two hundred large shields of hammered gold; six hundred bekas of hammered gold went into each shield.
2Chronicles9:16 He also made three hundred small shields of hammered gold
2Chronicles9:17 Then the king made a great throne inlaid with ivory and overlaid with pure gold.
2Chronicles9:18 The throne had six steps
2Chronicles9:19 Twelve lions stood on the six steps
2Chronicles9:20 All King Solomon's goblets were gold
2Chronicles9:21 The king had a fleet of trading ships manned by Hiram's men. Once every three years it returned
2Chronicles9:22 King Solomon was greater in riches and wisdom than all the other kings of the earth.
2Chronicles9:23 All the kings of the earth sought audience with Solomon to hear the wisdom God had put in his heart.
2Chronicles9:24 Year after year
2Chronicles9:25 Solomon had four thousand stalls for horses and chariots
2Chronicles9:26 He ruled over all the kings from the River to the land of the Philistines
2Chronicles9:27 The king made silver as common in Jerusalem as stones
2Chronicles9:28 Solomon's horses were imported from Egypt and from all other countries.
2Chronicles9:29 As for the other events of Solomon's reign
2Chronicles9:30 Solomon reigned in Jerusalem over all Israel forty years.
2Chronicles9:31 Then he rested with his fathers and was buried in the city of David his father. And Rehoboam his son succeeded him as king.

2Chronicles13:22 The other events of Abijah's reign

2Chronicles14:2 Asa did what was good and right in the eyes of the LORD his God.
2Chronicles14:3 He removed the foreign altars and the high places
2Chronicles14:4 He commanded Judah to seek the LORD
2Chronicles14:5 He removed the high places and incense altars in every town in Judah
2Chronicles14:6 He built up the fortified cities of Judah
2Chronicles14:7 Let us build up these towns
2Chronicles14:8 Asa had an army of three hundred thousand men from Judah
2Chronicles14:9 Zerah the Cushite marched out against them with a vast army and three hundred chariots
2Chronicles14:10 Asa went out to meet him
2Chronicles14:11 Then Asa called to the LORD his God and said
2Chronicles14:12 The LORD struck down the Cushites before Asa and Judah. The Cushites fled
2Chronicles14:13 and Asa and his army pursued them as far as Gerar. Such a great number of Cushites fell that they could not recover; they were crushed before the LORD and his forces. The men of Judah carried off a large amount of plunder.
2Chronicles14:14 They destroyed all the villages around Gerar
2Chronicles14:15 They also attacked the camps of the herdsmen and carried off droves of sheep and goats and camels. Then they returned to Jerusalem.

2Chronicles15:2 He went out to meet Asa and said to him

2Chronicles15:4 But in their distress they turned to the LORD
2Chronicles15:5 In those days it was not safe to travel about
2Chronicles15:6 One nation was being crushed by another and one city by another
2Chronicles15:7 But as for you

2Chronicles15:13 All who would not seek the LORD

2Chronicles15:17 Although he did not remove the high places from Israel
2Chronicles15:18 He brought into the temple of God the silver and gold and the articles that he and his father had dedicated.
2Chronicles15:19 There was no more war until the thirty-fifth year of Asa's reign.
2Chronicles16:1 In the thirty-sixth year of Asa's reign Baasha king of Israel went up against Judah and fortified Ramah to prevent anyone from leaving or entering the territory of Asa king of Judah.
2Chronicles16:2 Asa then took the silver and gold out of the treasuries of the LORD's temple and of his own palace and sent it to Ben-Hadad king of Aram
2Chronicles16:3 Let there be a treaty between me and you
2Chronicles16:4 Ben-Hadad agreed with King Asa and sent the commanders of his forces against the towns of Israel. They conquered Ijon
2Chronicles16:5 When Baasha heard this
2Chronicles16:6 Then King Asa brought all the men of Judah

2Chronicles16:8 Were not the Cushites and Libyans a mighty army with great numbers of chariots and horsemen? Yet when you relied on the LORD
2Chronicles16:9 For the eyes of the LORD range throughout the earth to strengthen those whose hearts are fully committed to him. You have done a foolish thing
2Chronicles16:10 Asa was angry with the seer because of this; he was so enraged that he put him in prison. At the same time Asa brutally oppressed some of the people.

2Chronicles16:13 Then in the forty-first year of his reign Asa died and rested with his fathers.

2Chronicles17:1 Jehoshaphat his son succeeded him as king and strengthened himself against Israel.
2Chronicles17:2 He stationed troops in all the fortified cities of Judah and put garrisons in Judah and in the towns of Ephraim that his father Asa had captured.
2Chronicles17:3 The LORD was with Jehoshaphat because in his early years he walked in the ways his father David had followed. He did not consult the Baals
2Chronicles17:4 but sought the God of his father and followed his commands rather than the practices of Israel.

2Chronicles32:1 After all that Hezekiah had so faithfully done
2Chronicles32:2 When Hezekiah saw that Sennacherib had come and that he intended to make war on Jerusalem
2Chronicles32:3 he consulted with his officials and military staff about blocking off the water from the springs outside the city
2Chronicles32:4 A large force of men assembled
2Chronicles32:5 Then he worked hard repairing all the broken sections of the wall and building towers on it. He built another wall outside that one and reinforced the supporting terraces of the City of David. He also made large numbers of weapons and shields.
2Chronicles32:6 He appointed military officers over the people and assembled them before him in the square at the city gate and encouraged them with these words:
2Chronicles32:7 Be strong and courageous. Do not be afraid or discouraged because of the king of Assyria and the vast army with him
2Chronicles32:8 With him is only the arm of flesh
2Chronicles32:9 Later
2Chronicles32:10 This is what Sennacherib king of Assyria says: On what are you basing your confidence
2Chronicles32:11 When Hezekiah says
2Chronicles32:12 Did not Hezekiah himself remove this god's high places and altars
2Chronicles32:13 Do you not know what I and my fathers have done to all the peoples of the other lands? Were the gods of those nations ever able to deliver their land from my hand?
2Chronicles32:14 Who of all the gods of these nations that my fathers destroyed has been able to save his people from me? How then can your god deliver you from my hand?
2Chronicles32:15 Now do not let Hezekiah deceive you and mislead you like this. Do not believe him
2Chronicles32:16 Sennacherib's officers spoke further against the LORD God and against his servant Hezekiah.
2Chronicles32:17 The king also wrote letters insulting the LORD
2Chronicles32:18 Then they called out in Hebrew to the people of Jerusalem who were on the wall
2Chronicles32:19 They spoke about the God of Jerusalem as they did about the gods of the other peoples of the world-the work of men's hands.
2Chronicles32:20 King Hezekiah and the prophet Isaiah son of Amoz cried out in prayer to heaven about this.
2Chronicles32:21 And the LORD sent an angel
2Chronicles32:22 So the LORD saved Hezekiah and the people of Jerusalem from the hand of Sennacherib king of Assyria and from the hand of all others. He took care of them on every side.
2Chronicles32:23 Many brought offerings to Jerusalem for the LORD and valuable gifts for Hezekiah king of Judah. From then on he was highly regarded by all the nations.
2Chronicles32:24 In those days Hezekiah became ill and was at the point of death. He prayed to the LORD
2Chronicles32:25 But Hezekiah's heart was proud and he did not respond to the kindness shown him; therefore the LORD's wrath was on him and on Judah and Jerusalem.
2Chronicles32:26 Then Hezekiah repented of the pride of his heart
2Chronicles32:27 Hezekiah had very great riches and honor
2Chronicles32:28 He also made buildings to store the harvest of grain
2Chronicles32:29 He built villages and acquired great numbers of flocks and herds
2Chronicles32:30 It was Hezekiah who blocked the upper outlet of the Gihon spring and channeled the water down to the west side of the City of David. He succeeded in everything he undertook.
2Chronicles32:31 But when envoys were sent by the rulers of Babylon to ask him about the miraculous sign that had occurred in the land
2Chronicles32:32 The other events of Hezekiah's reign and his acts of devotion are written in the vision of the prophet Isaiah son of Amoz in the book of the kings of Judah and Israel.
2Chronicles32:33 Hezekiah rested with his fathers and was buried on the hill where the tombs of David's descendants are. All Judah and the people of Jerusalem honored him when he died. And Manasseh his son succeeded him as king.

Ezra8:30 Then the priests and Levites received the silver and gold and sacred articles that had been weighed out to be taken to the house of our God in Jerusalem.
Ezra8:31 On the twelfth day of the first month we set out from the Ahava Canal to go to Jerusalem. The hand of our God was on us
Ezra8:32 So we arrived in Jerusalem
Ezra8:33 On the fourth day
Ezra8:34 Everything was accounted for by number and weight
Ezra8:35 Then the exiles who had returned from captivity sacrificed burnt offerings to the God of Israel: twelve bulls for all Israel
Ezra8:36 They also delivered the king's orders to the royal satraps and to the governors of Trans-Euphrates
Ezra9:1 After these things had been done
Ezra9:2 They have taken some of their daughters as wives for themselves and their sons
Ezra9:3 When I heard this
Ezra9:4 Then everyone who trembled at the words of the God of Israel gathered around me because of this unfaithfulness of the exiles. And I sat there appalled until the evening sacrifice.
Ezra9:5 Then
Ezra9:6 and prayed: O my God
Ezra9:7 From the days of our forefathers until now
Ezra9:8 But now
Ezra9:9 Though we are slaves
Ezra9:10 But now
Ezra9:11 you gave through your servants the prophets when you said: 'The land you are entering to possess is a land polluted by the corruption of its peoples. By their detestable practices they have filled it with their impurity from one end to the other.
Ezra9:12 Therefore
Ezra9:13 What has happened to us is a result of our evil deeds and our great guilt
Ezra9:14 Shall we again break your commands and intermarry with the peoples who commit such detestable practices? Would you not be angry enough with us to destroy us
Ezra9:15 O LORD
Ezra10:1 While Ezra was praying and confessing
Ezra10:2 Then Shecaniah son of Jehiel
Ezra10:3 Now let us make a covenant before our God to send away all these women and their children
Ezra10:4 Rise up; this matter is in your hands. We will support you
Ezra10:5 So Ezra rose up and put the leading priests and Levites and all Israel under oath to do what had been suggested. And they took the oath.
Ezra10:6 Then Ezra withdrew from before the house of God and went to the room of Jehohanan son of Eliashib. While he was there
Ezra10:7 A proclamation was then issued throughout Judah and Jerusalem for all the exiles to assemble in Jerusalem.
Ezra10:8 Anyone who failed to appear within three days would forfeit all his property
Ezra10:9 Within the three days
Ezra10:10 Then Ezra the priest stood up and said to them
Ezra10:11 Now make confession to the LORD
Ezra10:12 The whole assembly responded with a loud voice: You are right! We must do as you say.
Ezra10:13 But there are many people here and it is the rainy season; so we cannot stand outside. Besides
Ezra10:14 Let our officials act for the whole assembly. Then let everyone in our towns who has married a foreign woman come at a set time
Ezra10:15 Only Jonathan son of Asahel and Jahzeiah son of Tikvah
Ezra10:16 So the exiles did as was proposed. Ezra the priest selected men who were family heads
Ezra10:17 and by the first day of the first month they finished dealing with all the men who had married foreign women.
Ezra10:18 Among the descendants of the priests
Ezra10:19 (They all gave their hands in pledge to put away their wives

Nehemiah1:1 The words of Nehemiah son of Hacaliah: In the month of Kislev in the twentieth year
Nehemiah1:2 Hanani
Nehemiah1:3 They said to me
Nehemiah1:4 When I heard these things
Nehemiah1:5 Then I said: O LORD
Nehemiah1:6 let your ear be attentive and your eyes open to hear the prayer your servant is praying before you day and night for your servants
Nehemiah1:7 We have acted very wickedly toward you. We have not obeyed the commands
Nehemiah1:8 Remember the instruction you gave your servant Moses
Nehemiah1:9 but if you return to me and obey my commands
Nehemiah1:10 They are your servants and your people
Nehemiah1:11 O Lord
Nehemiah2:1 In the month of Nisan in the twentieth year of King Artaxerxes
Nehemiah2:2 so the king asked me
Nehemiah2:3 but I said to the king
Nehemiah2:4 The king said to me
Nehemiah2:5 and I answered the king
Nehemiah2:6 Then the king
Nehemiah2:7 I also said to him
Nehemiah2:8 And may I have a letter to Asaph
Nehemiah2:9 So I went to the governors of Trans-Euphrates and gave them the king's letters. The king had also sent army officers and cavalry with me.
Nehemiah2:10 When Sanballat the Horonite and Tobiah the Ammonite official heard about this
Nehemiah2:11 I went to Jerusalem
Nehemiah2:12 I set out during the night with a few men. I had not told anyone what my God had put in my heart to do for Jerusalem. There were no mounts with me except the one I was riding on.
Nehemiah2:13 By night I went out through the Valley Gate toward the Jackal Well and the Dung Gate
Nehemiah2:14 Then I moved on toward the Fountain Gate and the King's Pool
Nehemiah2:15 so I went up the valley by night
Nehemiah2:16 The officials did not know where I had gone or what I was doing
Nehemiah2:17 Then I said to them
Nehemiah2:18 I also told them about the gracious hand of my God upon me and what the king had said to me. They replied
Nehemiah2:19 But when Sanballat the Horonite
Nehemiah2:20 I answered them by saying
Nehemiah3:1 Eliashib the high priest and his fellow priests went to work and rebuilt the Sheep Gate. They dedicated it and set its doors in place
Nehemiah3:2 The men of Jericho built the adjoining section
Nehemiah3:3 The Fish Gate was rebuilt by the sons of Hassenaah. They laid its beams and put its doors and bolts and bars in place.
Nehemiah3:4 Meremoth son of Uriah
Nehemiah3:5 The next section was repaired by the men of Tekoa
Nehemiah3:6 The Jeshanah Gate was repaired by Joiada son of Paseah and Meshullam son of Besodeiah. They laid its beams and put its doors and bolts and bars in place.
Nehemiah3:7 Next to them
Nehemiah3:8 Uzziel son of Harhaiah
Nehemiah3:9 Rephaiah son of Hur
Nehemiah3:10 Adjoining this
Nehemiah3:11 Malkijah son of Harim and Hasshub son of Pahath-Moab repaired another section and the Tower of the Ovens.
Nehemiah3:12 Shallum son of Hallohesh
Nehemiah3:13 The Valley Gate was repaired by Hanun and the residents of Zanoah. They rebuilt it and put its doors and bolts and bars in place. They also repaired five hundred yards of the wall as far as the Dung Gate.
Nehemiah3:14 The Dung Gate was repaired by Malkijah son of Recab
Nehemiah3:15 The Fountain Gate was repaired by Shallun son of Col-Hozeh

Nehemiah4:1 When Sanballat heard that we were rebuilding the wall
Nehemiah4:2 and in the presence of his associates and the army of Samaria
Nehemiah4:3 Tobiah the Ammonite
Nehemiah4:4 Hear us
Nehemiah4:5 Do not cover up their guilt or blot out their sins from your sight
Nehemiah4:6 So we rebuilt the wall till all of it reached half its height
Nehemiah4:7 But when Sanballat
Nehemiah4:8 They all plotted together to come and fight against Jerusalem and stir up trouble against it.
Nehemiah4:9 But we prayed to our God and posted a guard day and night to meet this threat.
Nehemiah4:10 Meanwhile
Nehemiah4:11 Also our enemies said
Nehemiah4:12 Then the Jews who lived near them came and told us ten times over
Nehemiah4:13 Therefore I stationed some of the people behind the lowest points of the wall at the exposed places
Nehemiah4:14 After I looked things over
Nehemiah4:15 When our enemies heard that we were aware of their plot and that God had frustrated it
Nehemiah4:16 From that day on
Nehemiah4:17 who were building the wall. Those who carried materials did their work with one hand and held a weapon in the other
Nehemiah4:18 and each of the builders wore his sword at his side as he worked. But the man who sounded the trumpet stayed with me.
Nehemiah4:19 Then I said to the nobles
Nehemiah4:20 Wherever you hear the sound of the trumpet
Nehemiah4:21 So we continued the work with half the men holding spears
Nehemiah4:22 At that time I also said to the people
Nehemiah4:23 Neither I nor my brothers nor my men nor the guards with me took off our clothes; each had his weapon
Nehemiah5:1 Now the men and their wives raised a great outcry against their Jewish brothers.
Nehemiah5:2 Some were saying
Nehemiah5:3 Others were saying
Nehemiah5:4 Still others were saying
Nehemiah5:5 Although we are of the same flesh and blood as our countrymen and though our sons are as good as theirs
Nehemiah5:6 When I heard their outcry and these charges
Nehemiah5:7 I pondered them in my mind and then accused the nobles and officials. I told them
Nehemiah5:8 and said: As far as possible
Nehemiah5:9 So I continued
Nehemiah5:10 I and my brothers and my men are also lending the people money and grain. But let the exacting of usury stop!
Nehemiah5:11 Give back to them immediately their fields
Nehemiah5:12 We will give it back
Nehemiah5:13 I also shook out the folds of my robe and said
Nehemiah5:14 Moreover
Nehemiah5:15 But the earlier governors-those preceding me-placed a heavy burden on the people and took forty shekels of silver from them in addition to food and wine. Their assistants also lorded it over the people. But out of reverence for God I did not act like that.
Nehemiah5:16 Instead
Nehemiah5:17 Furthermore
Nehemiah5:18 Each day one ox
Nehemiah5:19 Remember me with favor
Nehemiah6:1 When word came to Sanballat
Nehemiah6:2 Sanballat and Geshem sent me this message: Come
Nehemiah6:3 so I sent messengers to them with this reply: I am carrying on a great project and cannot go down. Why should the work stop while I leave it and go down to you?
Nehemiah6:4 Four times they sent me the same message
Nehemiah6:5 Then
Nehemiah6:6 in which was written: It is reported among the nations-and Geshem says it is true-that you and the Jews are plotting to revolt
Nehemiah6:7 and have even appointed prophets to make this proclamation about you in Jerusalem: 'There is a king in Judah!' Now this report will get back to the king; so come
Nehemiah6:8 I sent him this reply: Nothing like what you are saying is happening; you are just making it up out of your head.
Nehemiah6:9 They were all trying to frighten us
Nehemiah6:10 One day I went to the house of Shemaiah son of Delaiah
Nehemiah6:11 But I said
Nehemiah6:12 I realized that God had not sent him
Nehemiah6:13 He had been hired to intimidate me so that I would commit a sin by doing this
Nehemiah6:14 Remember Tobiah and Sanballat
Nehemiah6:15 So the wall was completed on the twenty-fifth of Elul
Nehemiah6:16 When all our enemies heard about this
Nehemiah6:17 Also
Nehemiah6:18 For many in Judah were under oath to him
Nehemiah6:19 Moreover

Nehemiah8:1 all the people assembled as one man in the square before the Water Gate. They told Ezra the scribe to bring out the Book of the Law of Moses
Nehemiah8:2 So on the first day of the seventh month Ezra the priest brought the Law before the assembly
Nehemiah8:3 He read it aloud from daybreak till noon as he faced the square before the Water Gate in the presence of the men
Nehemiah8:4 Ezra the scribe stood on a high wooden platform built for the occasion. Beside him on his right stood Mattithiah
Nehemiah8:5 Ezra opened the book. All the people could see him because he was standing above them; and as he opened it
Nehemiah8:6 Ezra praised the LORD
Nehemiah8:7 The Levites-Jeshua
Nehemiah8:8 They read from the Book of the Law of God
Nehemiah8:9 Then Nehemiah the governor
Nehemiah8:10 Nehemiah said
Nehemiah8:11 The Levites calmed all the people
Nehemiah8:12 Then all the people went away to eat and drink
Nehemiah8:13 On the second day of the month
Nehemiah8:14 They found written in the Law
Nehemiah8:15 and that they should proclaim this word and spread it throughout their towns and in Jerusalem: Go out into the hill country and bring back branches from olive and wild olive trees
Nehemiah8:16 So the people went out and brought back branches and built themselves booths on their own roofs
Nehemiah8:17 The whole company that had returned from exile built booths and lived in them. From the days of Joshua son of Nun until that day
Nehemiah8:18 Day after day
Nehemiah9:1 On the twenty-fourth day of the same month
Nehemiah9:2 Those of Israelite descent had separated themselves from all foreigners. They stood in their places and confessed their sins and the wickedness of their fathers.
Nehemiah9:3 They stood where they were and read from the Book of the Law of the LORD their God for a quarter of the day
Nehemiah9:4 Standing on the stairs were the Levites-Jeshua
Nehemiah9:5 And the Levites-Jeshua
Nehemiah9:6 You alone are the LORD. You made the heavens
Nehemiah9:7 You are the LORD God
Nehemiah9:8 You found his heart faithful to you
Nehemiah9:9 You saw the suffering of our forefathers in Egypt; you heard their cry at the Red Sea.
Nehemiah9:10 You sent miraculous signs and wonders against Pharaoh
Nehemiah9:11 You divided the sea before them
Nehemiah9:12 By day you led them with a pillar of cloud
Nehemiah9:13 You came down on Mount Sinai; you spoke to them from heaven. You gave them regulations and laws that are just and right
Nehemiah9:14 You made known to them your holy Sabbath and gave them commands
Nehemiah9:15 In their hunger you gave them bread from heaven and in their thirst you brought them water from the rock; you told them to go in and take possession of the land you had sworn with uplifted hand to give them.
Nehemiah9:16 But they
Nehemiah9:17 They refused to listen and failed to remember the miracles you performed among them. They became stiff-necked and in their rebellion appointed a leader in order to return to their slavery. But you are a forgiving God
Nehemiah9:18 even when they cast for themselves an image of a calf and said
Nehemiah9:19 Because of your great compassion you did not abandon them in the desert. By day the pillar of cloud did not cease to guide them on their path
Nehemiah9:20 You gave your good Spirit to instruct them. You did not withhold your manna from their mouths
Nehemiah9:21 For forty years you sustained them in the desert; they lacked nothing
Nehemiah9:22 You gave them kingdoms and nations
Nehemiah9:23 You made their sons as numerous as the stars in the sky
Nehemiah9:24 Their sons went in and took possession of the land. You subdued before them the Canaanites
Nehemiah9:25 They captured fortified cities and fertile land; they took possession of houses filled with all kinds of good things
Nehemiah9:26 But they were disobedient and rebelled against you; they put your law behind their backs. They killed your prophets
Nehemiah9:27 So you handed them over to their enemies
Nehemiah9:28 But as soon as they were at rest
Nehemiah9:29 You warned them to return to your law
Nehemiah9:30 For many years you were patient with them. By your Spirit you admonished them through your prophets. Yet they paid no attention
Nehemiah9:31 But in your great mercy you did not put an end to them or abandon them
Nehemiah9:32 Now therefore
Nehemiah9:33 In all that has happened to us
Nehemiah9:34 Our kings
Nehemiah9:35 Even while they were in their kingdom
Nehemiah9:36 But see
Nehemiah9:37 Because of our sins
Nehemiah9:38 In view of all this

Nehemiah13:26 Was it not because of marriages like these that Solomon king of Israel sinned? Among the many nations there was no king like him. He was loved by his God

Job1:1 In the land of Uz there lived a man whose name was Job. This man was blameless and upright; he feared God and shunned evil.
Job1:2 He had seven sons and three daughters
Job1:3 and he owned seven thousand sheep
Job1:4 His sons used to take turns holding feasts in their homes
Job1:5 When a period of feasting had run its course
Job1:6 One day the angels came to present themselves before the LORD
Job1:7 The LORD said to Satan
Job1:8 Then the LORD said to Satan
Job1:9 Does Job fear God for nothing? Satan replied.
Job1:10 Have you not put a hedge around him and his household and everything he has? You have blessed the work of his hands
Job1:11 But stretch out your hand and strike everything he has
Job1:12 The LORD said to Satan
Job1:13 One day when Job's sons and daughters were feasting and drinking wine at the oldest brother's house
Job1:14 a messenger came to Job and said

Job1:17 While he was still speaking
Job1:18 While he was still speaking
Job1:19 when suddenly a mighty wind swept in from the desert and struck the four corners of the house. It collapsed on them and they are dead

Job1:21 and said: Naked I came from my mother's womb

Job2:2 And the LORD said to Satan
Job2:3 Then the LORD said to Satan

Job2:5 But stretch out your hand and strike his flesh and bones
Job2:6 The LORD said to Satan
Job2:7 So Satan went out from the presence of the LORD and afflicted Job with painful sores from the soles of his feet to the top of his head.
Job2:8 Then Job took a piece of broken pottery and scraped himself with it as he sat among the ashes.

Job2:10 He replied
Job2:11 When Job's three friends
Job2:12 When they saw him from a distance
Job2:13 Then they sat on the ground with him for seven days and seven nights. No one said a word to him
Job3:1 After this
Job3:2 He said:
Job3:3 May the day of my birth perish
Job3:4 That day-may it turn to darkness; may God above not care about it; may no light shine upon it.
Job3:5 May darkness and deep shadow claim it once more; may a cloud settle over it; may blackness overwhelm its light.
Job3:6 That night-may thick darkness seize it; may it not be included among the days of the year nor be entered in any of the months.
Job3:7 May that night be barren; may no shout of joy be heard in it.
Job3:8 May those who curse days curse that day
Job3:9 May its morning stars become dark; may it wait for daylight in vain and not see the first rays of dawn
Job3:10 for it did not shut the doors of the womb on me to hide trouble from my eyes.
Job3:11 Why did I not perish at birth
Job3:12 Why were there knees to receive me and breasts that I might be nursed?
Job3:13 For now I would be lying down in peace; I would be asleep and at rest
Job3:14 with kings and counselors of the earth
Job3:15 with rulers who had gold
Job3:16 Or why was I not hidden in the ground like a stillborn child
Job3:17 There the wicked cease from turmoil
Job3:18 Captives also enjoy their ease; they no longer hear the slave driver's shout.
Job3:19 The small and the great are there
Job3:20 Why is light given to those in misery
Job3:21 to those who long for death that does not come
Job3:22 who are filled with gladness and rejoice when they reach the grave?
Job3:23 Why is life given to a man whose way is hidden
Job3:24 For sighing comes to me instead of food; my groans pour out like water.
Job3:25 What I feared has come upon me; what I dreaded has happened to me.
Job3:26 I have no peace
Job4:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite replied:
Job4:2 If someone ventures a word with you
Job4:3 Think how you have instructed many
Job4:4 Your words have supported those who stumbled; you have strengthened faltering knees.
Job4:5 But now trouble comes to you
Job4:6 Should not your piety be your confidence and your blameless ways your hope?
Job4:7 Consider now: Who

Job4:9 At the breath of God they are destroyed; at the blast of his anger they perish.

Job4:11 The lion perishes for lack of prey

Job4:13 Amid disquieting dreams in the night
Job4:14 fear and trembling seized me and made all my bones shake.
Job4:15 A spirit glided past my face
Job4:16 It stopped
Job4:17 'Can a mortal be more righteous than God? Can a man be more pure than his Maker?

Job4:19 how much more those who live in houses of clay

Job5:2 Resentment kills a fool
Job5:3 I myself have seen a fool taking root
Job5:4 His children are far from safety
Job5:5 The hungry consume his harvest
Job5:6 For hardship does not spring from the soil
Job5:7 Yet man is born to trouble as surely as sparks fly upward.
Job5:8 But if it were I
Job5:9 He performs wonders that cannot be fathomed
Job5:10 He bestows rain on the earth; he sends water upon the countryside.
Job5:11 The lowly he sets on high
Job5:12 He thwarts the plans of the crafty
Job5:13 He catches the wise in their craftiness
Job5:14 Darkness comes upon them in the daytime; at noon they grope as in the night.
Job5:15 He saves the needy from the sword in their mouth; he saves them from the clutches of the powerful.
Job5:16 So the poor have hope
Job5:17 Blessed is the man whom God corrects; so do not despise the discipline of the Almighty.
Job5:18 For he wounds
Job5:19 From six calamities he will rescue you; in seven no harm will befall you.
Job5:20 In famine he will ransom you from death
Job5:21 You will be protected from the lash of the tongue
Job5:22 You will laugh at destruction and famine
Job5:23 For you will have a covenant with the stones of the field
Job5:24 You will know that your tent is secure; you will take stock of your property and find nothing missing.
Job5:25 You will know that your children will be many
Job5:26 You will come to the grave in full vigor
Job5:27 We have examined this
Job6:1 Then Job replied:
Job6:2 If only my anguish could be weighed and all my misery be placed on the scales!
Job6:3 It would surely outweigh the sand of the seas- no wonder my words have been impetuous.
Job6:4 The arrows of the Almighty are in me
Job6:5 Does a wild donkey bray when it has grass
Job6:6 Is tasteless food eaten without salt
Job6:7 I refuse to touch it; such food makes me ill.
Job6:8 Oh
Job6:9 that God would be willing to crush me
Job6:10 Then I would still have this consolation- my joy in unrelenting pain- that I had not denied the words of the Holy One.
Job6:11 What strength do I have
Job6:12 Do I have the strength of stone? Is my flesh bronze?
Job6:13 Do I have any power to help myself
Job6:14 A despairing man should have the devotion of his friends
Job6:15 But my brothers are as undependable as intermittent streams
Job6:16 when darkened by thawing ice and swollen with melting snow
Job6:17 but that cease to flow in the dry season
Job6:18 Caravans turn aside from their routes; they go up into the wasteland and perish.

Job6:30 Is there any wickedness on my lips? Can my mouth not discern malice?
Job7:1 Does not man have hard service on earth? Are not his days like those of a hired man?
Job7:2 Like a slave longing for the evening shadows
Job7:3 so I have been allotted months of futility
Job7:4 When I lie down I think
Job7:5 My body is clothed with worms and scabs
Job7:6 My days are swifter than a weaver's shuttle
Job7:7 Remember
Job7:8 The eye that now sees me will see me no longer; you will look for me
Job7:9 As a cloud vanishes and is gone
Job7:10 He will never come to his house again; his place will know him no more.
Job7:11 Therefore I will not keep silent; I will speak out in the anguish of my spirit
Job7:12 Am I the sea
Job7:13 When I think my bed will comfort me and my couch will ease my complaint
Job7:14 even then you frighten me with dreams and terrify me with visions
Job7:15 so that I prefer strangling and death
Job7:16 I despise my life; I would not live forever. Let me alone; my days have no meaning.
