Psalms26:1 Vindicate me
Psalms26:2 Test me
Psalms26:3 for your love is ever before me
Psalms26:4 I do not sit with deceitful men
Psalms26:5 I abhor the assembly of evildoers and refuse to sit with the wicked.
Psalms26:6 I wash my hands in innocence
Psalms26:7 proclaiming aloud your praise and telling of all your wonderful deeds.
Psalms26:8 I love the house where you live
Psalms26:9 Do not take away my soul along with sinners
Psalms26:10 in whose hands are wicked schemes
Psalms26:11 But I lead a blameless life; redeem me and be merciful to me.
Psalms26:12 My feet stand on level ground; in the great assembly I will praise the LORD.
Psalms27:1 The LORD is my light and my salvation- whom shall I fear? The LORD is the stronghold of my life- of whom shall I be afraid?
Psalms27:2 When evil men advance against me to devour my flesh
Psalms27:3 Though an army besiege me
Psalms27:4 One thing I ask of the LORD
Psalms27:5 For in the day of trouble he will keep me safe in his dwelling; he will hide me in the shelter of his tabernacle and set me high upon a rock.
Psalms27:6 Then my head will be exalted above the enemies who surround me; at his tabernacle will I sacrifice with shouts of joy; I will sing and make music to the LORD.
Psalms27:7 Hear my voice when I call
Psalms27:8 My heart says of you
Psalms27:9 Do not hide your face from me
Psalms27:10 Though my father and mother forsake me
Psalms27:11 Teach me your way
Psalms27:12 Do not turn me over to the desire of my foes
Psalms27:13 I am still confident of this: I will see the goodness of the LORD in the land of the living.
Psalms27:14 Wait for the LORD; be strong and take heart and wait for the LORD.
Psalms28:1 To you I call
Psalms28:2 Hear my cry for mercy as I call to you for help
Psalms28:3 Do not drag me away with the wicked
Psalms28:4 Repay them for their deeds and for their evil work; repay them for what their hands have done and bring back upon them what they deserve.
Psalms28:5 Since they show no regard for the works of the LORD and what his hands have done
Psalms28:6 Praise be to the LORD
Psalms28:7 The LORD is my strength and my shield; my heart trusts in him
Psalms28:8 The LORD is the strength of his people
Psalms28:9 Save your people and bless your inheritance; be their shepherd and carry them forever.
Psalms29:1 Ascribe to the LORD
Psalms29:2 Ascribe to the LORD the glory due his name; worship the LORD in the splendor of his holiness.
Psalms29:3 The voice of the LORD is over the waters; the God of glory thunders
Psalms29:4 The voice of the LORD is powerful; the voice of the LORD is majestic.
Psalms29:5 The voice of the LORD breaks the cedars; the LORD breaks in pieces the cedars of Lebanon.
Psalms29:6 He makes Lebanon skip like a calf
Psalms29:7 The voice of the LORD strikes with flashes of lightning.
Psalms29:8 The voice of the LORD shakes the desert; the LORD shakes the Desert of Kadesh.
Psalms29:9 The voice of the LORD twists the oaks and strips the forests bare. And in his temple all cry
Psalms29:10 The LORD sits enthroned over the flood; the LORD is enthroned as King forever.
Psalms29:11 The LORD gives strength to his people; the LORD blesses his people with peace.

Psalms31:1 In you
Psalms31:2 Turn your ear to me
Psalms31:3 Since you are my rock and my fortress
Psalms31:4 Free me from the trap that is set for me
Psalms31:5 Into your hands I commit my spirit; redeem me
Psalms31:6 I hate those who cling to worthless idols; I trust in the LORD.
Psalms31:7 I will be glad and rejoice in your love
Psalms31:8 You have not handed me over to the enemy but have set my feet in a spacious place.
Psalms31:9 Be merciful to me
Psalms31:10 My life is consumed by anguish and my years by groaning; my strength fails because of my affliction
Psalms31:11 Because of all my enemies
Psalms31:12 I am forgotten by them as though I were dead; I have become like broken pottery.
Psalms31:13 For I hear the slander of many; there is terror on every side; they conspire against me and plot to take my life.
Psalms31:14 But I trust in you
Psalms31:15 My times are in your hands; deliver me from my enemies and from those who pursue me.
Psalms31:16 Let your face shine on your servant; save me in your unfailing love.
Psalms31:17 Let me not be put to shame
Psalms31:18 Let their lying lips be silenced
Psalms31:19 How great is your goodness
Psalms31:20 In the shelter of your presence you hide them from the intrigues of men; in your dwelling you keep them safe from accusing tongues.
Psalms31:21 Praise be to the LORD
Psalms31:22 In my alarm I said
Psalms31:23 Love the LORD
Psalms31:24 Be strong and take heart
Psalms32:1 Blessed is he whose transgressions are forgiven
Psalms32:2 Blessed is the man whose sin the LORD does not count against him and in whose spirit is no deceit.
Psalms32:3 When I kept silent
Psalms32:4 For day and night your hand was heavy upon me; my strength was sapped as in the heat of summer. Selah
Psalms32:5 Then I acknowledged my sin to you and did not cover up my iniquity. I said
Psalms32:6 Therefore let everyone who is godly pray to you while you may be found; surely when the mighty waters rise
Psalms32:7 You are my hiding place; you will protect me from trouble and surround me with songs of deliverance. Selah
Psalms32:8 I will instruct you and teach you in the way you should go; I will counsel you and watch over you.
Psalms32:9 Do not be like the horse or the mule
Psalms32:10 Many are the woes of the wicked
Psalms32:11 Rejoice in the LORD and be glad
Psalms33:1 Sing joyfully to the LORD
Psalms33:2 Praise the LORD with the harp; make music to him on the ten-stringed lyre.
Psalms33:3 Sing to him a new song; play skillfully
Psalms33:4 For the word of the LORD is right and true; he is faithful in all he does.
Psalms33:5 The LORD loves righteousness and justice; the earth is full of his unfailing love.
Psalms33:6 By the word of the LORD were the heavens made
Psalms33:7 He gathers the waters of the sea into jars; he puts the deep into storehouses.
Psalms33:8 Let all the earth fear the LORD; let all the people of the world revere him.
Psalms33:9 For he spoke
Psalms33:10 The LORD foils the plans of the nations; he thwarts the purposes of the peoples.
Psalms33:11 But the plans of the LORD stand firm forever
Psalms33:12 Blessed is the nation whose God is the LORD
Psalms33:13 From heaven the LORD looks down and sees all mankind;
Psalms33:14 from his dwelling place he watches all who live on earth-
Psalms33:15 he who forms the hearts of all
Psalms33:16 No king is saved by the size of his army; no warrior escapes by his great strength.
Psalms33:17 A horse is a vain hope for deliverance; despite all its great strength it cannot save.
Psalms33:18 But the eyes of the LORD are on those who fear him
Psalms33:19 to deliver them from death and keep them alive in famine.
Psalms33:20 We wait in hope for the LORD; he is our help and our shield.
Psalms33:21 In him our hearts rejoice
Psalms33:22 May your unfailing love rest upon us
Psalms34:1 I will extol the LORD at all times; his praise will always be on my lips.
Psalms34:2 My soul will boast in the LORD; let the afflicted hear and rejoice.
Psalms34:3 Glorify the LORD with me; let us exalt his name together.
Psalms34:4 I sought the LORD
Psalms34:5 Those who look to him are radiant; their faces are never covered with shame.
Psalms34:6 This poor man called
Psalms34:7 The angel of the LORD encamps around those who fear him
Psalms34:8 Taste and see that the LORD is good; blessed is the man who takes refuge in him.
Psalms34:9 Fear the LORD
Psalms34:10 The lions may grow weak and hungry
Psalms34:11 Come
Psalms34:12 Whoever of you loves life and desires to see many good days
Psalms34:13 keep your tongue from evil and your lips from speaking lies.
Psalms34:14 Turn from evil and do good; seek peace and pursue it.
Psalms34:15 The eyes of the LORD are on the righteous and his ears are attentive to their cry;
Psalms34:16 the face of the LORD is against those who do evil
Psalms34:17 The righteous cry out
Psalms34:18 The LORD is close to the brokenhearted and saves those who are crushed in spirit.
Psalms34:19 A righteous man may have many troubles
Psalms34:20 he protects all his bones
Psalms34:21 Evil will slay the wicked; the foes of the righteous will be condemned.
Psalms34:22 The LORD redeems his servants; no one will be condemned who takes refuge in him.
Psalms35:1 Contend
Psalms35:2 Take up shield and buckler; arise and come to my aid.
Psalms35:3 Brandish spear and javelin against those who pursue me. Say to my soul
Psalms35:4 May those who seek my life be disgraced and put to shame; may those who plot my ruin be turned back in dismay.
Psalms35:5 May they be like chaff before the wind
Psalms35:6 may their path be dark and slippery
Psalms35:7 Since they hid their net for me without cause and without cause dug a pit for me
Psalms35:8 may ruin overtake them by surprise- may the net they hid entangle them
Psalms35:9 Then my soul will rejoice in the LORD and delight in his salvation.
Psalms35:10 My whole being will exclaim
Psalms35:11 Ruthless witnesses come forward; they question me on things I know nothing about.
Psalms35:12 They repay me evil for good and leave my soul forlorn.
Psalms35:13 Yet when they were ill
Psalms35:14 I went about mourning as though for my friend or brother. I bowed my head in grief as though weeping for my mother.
Psalms35:15 But when I stumbled
Psalms35:16 Like the ungodly they maliciously mocked; they gnashed their teeth at me.
Psalms35:17 O Lord
Psalms35:18 I will give you thanks in the great assembly; among throngs of people I will praise you.
Psalms35:19 Let not those gloat over me who are my enemies without cause; let not those who hate me without reason maliciously wink the eye.
Psalms35:20 They do not speak peaceably
Psalms35:21 They gape at me and say
Psalms35:22 O LORD
Psalms35:23 Awake
Psalms35:24 Vindicate me in your righteousness
Psalms35:25 Do not let them think
Psalms35:26 May all who gloat over my distress be put to shame and confusion; may all who exalt themselves over me be clothed with shame and disgrace.
Psalms35:27 May those who delight in my vindication shout for joy and gladness; may they always say
Psalms35:28 My tongue will speak of your righteousness and of your praises all day long.
Psalms36:1 An oracle is within my heart concerning the sinfulness of the wicked: There is no fear of God before his eyes.
Psalms36:2 For in his own eyes he flatters himself too much to detect or hate his sin.
Psalms36:3 The words of his mouth are wicked and deceitful; he has ceased to be wise and to do good.
Psalms36:4 Even on his bed he plots evil; he commits himself to a sinful course and does not reject what is wrong.
Psalms36:5 Your love
Psalms36:6 Your righteousness is like the mighty mountains
Psalms36:7 How priceless is your unfailing love! Both high and low among men find refuge in the shadow of your wings.
Psalms36:8 They feast on the abundance of your house; you give them drink from your river of delights.
Psalms36:9 For with you is the fountain of life; in your light we see light.
Psalms36:10 Continue your love to those who know you
Psalms36:11 May the foot of the proud not come against me
Psalms36:12 See how the evildoers lie fallen- thrown down
Psalms37:1 Do not fret because of evil men or be envious of those who do wrong;
Psalms37:2 for like the grass they will soon wither
Psalms37:3 Trust in the LORD and do good; dwell in the land and enjoy safe pasture.
Psalms37:4 Delight yourself in the LORD and he will give you the desires of your heart.
Psalms37:5 Commit your way to the LORD; trust in him and he will do this:
Psalms37:6 He will make your righteousness shine like the dawn
Psalms37:7 Be still before the LORD and wait patiently for him; do not fret when men succeed in their ways
Psalms37:8 Refrain from anger and turn from wrath; do not fret-it leads only to evil.
Psalms37:9 For evil men will be cut off
Psalms37:10 A little while
Psalms37:11 But the meek will inherit the land and enjoy great peace.
Psalms37:12 The wicked plot against the righteous and gnash their teeth at them;
Psalms37:13 but the Lord laughs at the wicked
Psalms37:14 The wicked draw the sword and bend the bow to bring down the poor and needy
Psalms37:15 But their swords will pierce their own hearts
Psalms37:16 Better the little that the righteous have than the wealth of many wicked;
Psalms37:17 for the power of the wicked will be broken
Psalms37:18 The days of the blameless are known to the LORD
Psalms37:19 In times of disaster they will not wither; in days of famine they will enjoy plenty.
Psalms37:20 But the wicked will perish: The LORD's enemies will be like the beauty of the fields
Psalms37:21 The wicked borrow and do not repay
Psalms37:22 those the LORD blesses will inherit the land
Psalms37:23 If the LORD delights in a man's way
Psalms37:24 though he stumble
Psalms37:25 I was young and now I am old
Psalms37:26 They are always generous and lend freely; their children will be blessed.
Psalms37:27 Turn from evil and do good; then you will dwell in the land forever.
Psalms37:28 For the LORD loves the just and will not forsake his faithful ones. They will be protected forever
Psalms37:29 the righteous will inherit the land and dwell in it forever.
Psalms37:30 The mouth of the righteous man utters wisdom
Psalms37:31 The law of his God is in his heart; his feet do not slip.
Psalms37:32 The wicked lie in wait for the righteous
Psalms37:33 but the LORD will not leave them in their power or let them be condemned when brought to trial.
Psalms37:34 Wait for the LORD and keep his way. He will exalt you to inherit the land; when the wicked are cut off
Psalms37:35 I have seen a wicked and ruthless man flourishing like a green tree in its native soil
Psalms37:36 but he soon passed away and was no more; though I looked for him
Psalms37:37 Consider the blameless
Psalms37:38 But all sinners will be destroyed; the future of the wicked will be cut off.
Psalms37:39 The salvation of the righteous comes from the LORD; he is their stronghold in time of trouble.
Psalms37:40 The LORD helps them and delivers them; he delivers them from the wicked and saves them
Psalms38:1 O LORD
Psalms38:2 For your arrows have pierced me
Psalms38:3 Because of your wrath there is no health in my body; my bones have no soundness because of my sin.
Psalms38:4 My guilt has overwhelmed me like a burden too heavy to bear.
Psalms38:5 My wounds fester and are loathsome because of my sinful folly.
Psalms38:6 I am bowed down and brought very low; all day long I go about mourning.
Psalms38:7 My back is filled with searing pain; there is no health in my body.
Psalms38:8 I am feeble and utterly crushed; I groan in anguish of heart.
Psalms38:9 All my longings lie open before you
Psalms38:10 My heart pounds
Psalms38:11 My friends and companions avoid me because of my wounds; my neighbors stay far away.
Psalms38:12 Those who seek my life set their traps
Psalms38:13 I am like a deaf man
Psalms38:14 I have become like a man who does not hear
Psalms38:15 I wait for you
Psalms38:16 For I said
Psalms38:17 For I am about to fall
Psalms38:18 I confess my iniquity; I am troubled by my sin.
Psalms38:19 Many are those who are my vigorous enemies; those who hate me without reason are numerous.
Psalms38:20 Those who repay my good with evil slander me when I pursue what is good.
Psalms38:21 O LORD
Psalms38:22 Come quickly to help me
Psalms39:1 I said
Psalms39:2 But when I was silent and still
Psalms39:3 My heart grew hot within me
Psalms39:4 Show me
Psalms39:5 You have made my days a mere handbreadth; the span of my years is as nothing before you. Each man's life is but a breath. Selah
Psalms39:6 Man is a mere phantom as he goes to and fro: He bustles about
Psalms39:7 But now
Psalms39:8 Save me from all my transgressions; do not make me the scorn of fools.
Psalms39:9 I was silent; I would not open my mouth
Psalms39:10 Remove your scourge from me; I am overcome by the blow of your hand.
Psalms39:11 You rebuke and discipline men for their sin; you consume their wealth like a moth- each man is but a breath. Selah
Psalms39:12 Hear my prayer
Psalms39:13 Look away from me

Psalms40:3 He put a new song in my mouth
Psalms40:4 Blessed is the man who makes the LORD his trust
Psalms40:5 Many

Psalms40:7 Then I said
Psalms40:8 I desire to do your will
Psalms40:9 I proclaim righteousness in the great assembly; I do not seal my lips

Psalms40:12 For troubles without number surround me; my sins have overtaken me

Psalms41:1 Blessed is he who has regard for the weak; the LORD delivers him in times of trouble.
Psalms41:2 The LORD will protect him and preserve his life; he will bless him in the land and not surrender him to the desire of his foes.
Psalms41:3 The LORD will sustain him on his sickbed and restore him from his bed of illness.
Psalms41:4 I said
Psalms41:5 My enemies say of me in malice
Psalms41:6 Whenever one comes to see me
Psalms41:7 All my enemies whisper together against me; they imagine the worst for me
Psalms41:8 A vile disease has beset him; he will never get up from the place where he lies.
Psalms41:9 Even my close friend
Psalms41:10 But you
Psalms41:11 I know that you are pleased with me
Psalms41:12 In my integrity you uphold me and set me in your presence forever.
Psalms41:13 Praise be to the LORD
Psalms42:1 As the deer pants for streams of water
Psalms42:2 My soul thirsts for God
Psalms42:3 My tears have been my food day and night
Psalms42:4 These things I remember as I pour out my soul: how I used to go with the multitude
Psalms42:5 Why are you downcast
Psalms42:6 my God. My soul is downcast within me; therefore I will remember you from the land of the Jordan
Psalms42:7 Deep calls to deep in the roar of your waterfalls; all your waves and breakers have swept over me.
Psalms42:8 By day the LORD directs his love
Psalms42:9 I say to God my Rock
Psalms42:10 My bones suffer mortal agony as my foes taunt me
Psalms42:11 Why are you downcast

Psalms43:2 You are God my stronghold. Why have you rejected me? Why must I go about mourning
Psalms43:3 Send forth your light and your truth
Psalms43:4 Then will I go to the altar of God
Psalms43:5 Why are you downcast
Psalms44:1 We have heard with our ears
Psalms44:2 With your hand you drove out the nations and planted our fathers; you crushed the peoples and made our fathers flourish.
Psalms44:3 It was not by their sword that they won the land
Psalms44:4 You are my King and my God
Psalms44:5 Through you we push back our enemies; through your name we trample our foes.
Psalms44:6 I do not trust in my bow
Psalms44:7 but you give us victory over our enemies
Psalms44:8 In God we make our boast all day long
Psalms44:9 But now you have rejected and humbled us; you no longer go out with our armies.
Psalms44:10 You made us retreat before the enemy
Psalms44:11 You gave us up to be devoured like sheep and have scattered us among the nations.
Psalms44:12 You sold your people for a pittance
Psalms44:13 You have made us a reproach to our neighbors
Psalms44:14 You have made us a byword among the nations; the peoples shake their heads at us.
Psalms44:15 My disgrace is before me all day long
Psalms44:16 at the taunts of those who reproach and revile me
Psalms44:17 All this happened to us
Psalms44:18 Our hearts had not turned back; our feet had not strayed from your path.
Psalms44:19 But you crushed us and made us a haunt for jackals and covered us over with deep darkness.
Psalms44:20 If we had forgotten the name of our God or spread out our hands to a foreign god
Psalms44:21 would not God have discovered it
Psalms44:22 Yet for your sake we face death all day long; we are considered as sheep to be slaughtered.
Psalms44:23 Awake
Psalms44:24 Why do you hide your face and forget our misery and oppression?
Psalms44:25 We are brought down to the dust; our bodies cling to the ground.
Psalms44:26 Rise up and help us; redeem us because of your unfailing love.
Psalms45:1 My heart is stirred by a noble theme as I recite my verses for the king; my tongue is the pen of a skillful writer.
Psalms45:2 You are the most excellent of men and your lips have been anointed with grace
Psalms45:3 Gird your sword upon your side
Psalms45:4 In your majesty ride forth victoriously in behalf of truth
Psalms45:5 Let your sharp arrows pierce the hearts of the king's enemies; let the nations fall beneath your feet.
Psalms45:6 Your throne
Psalms45:7 You love righteousness and hate wickedness; therefore God
Psalms45:8 All your robes are fragrant with myrrh and aloes and cassia; from palaces adorned with ivory the music of the strings makes you glad.
Psalms45:9 Daughters of kings are among your honored women; at your right hand is the royal bride in gold of Ophir.
Psalms45:10 Listen
Psalms45:11 The king is enthralled by your beauty; honor him
Psalms45:12 The Daughter of Tyre will come with a gift
Psalms45:13 All glorious is the princess within her chamber; her gown is interwoven with gold.
Psalms45:14 In embroidered garments she is led to the king; her virgin companions follow her and are brought to you.
Psalms45:15 They are led in with joy and gladness; they enter the palace of the king.
Psalms45:16 Your sons will take the place of your fathers; you will make them princes throughout the land.
Psalms45:17 I will perpetuate your memory through all generations; therefore the nations will praise you for ever and ever.

Psalms48:2 It is beautiful in its loftiness
Psalms48:3 God is in her citadels; he has shown himself to be her fortress.
Psalms48:4 When the kings joined forces
Psalms48:5 they saw her and were astounded; they fled in terror.
Psalms48:6 Trembling seized them there
Psalms48:7 You destroyed them like ships of Tarshish shattered by an east wind.

Psalms48:9 Within your temple
Psalms48:10 Like your name
Psalms48:11 Mount Zion rejoices
Psalms48:12 Walk about Zion
Psalms48:13 consider well her ramparts
Psalms48:14 For this God is our God for ever and ever; he will be our guide even to the end.
Psalms49:1 Hear this
Psalms49:2 both low and high
Psalms49:3 My mouth will speak words of wisdom; the utterance from my heart will give understanding.
Psalms49:4 I will turn my ear to a proverb; with the harp I will expound my riddle:
Psalms49:5 Why should I fear when evil days come
Psalms49:6 those who trust in their wealth and boast of their great riches?
Psalms49:7 No man can redeem the life of another or give to God a ransom for him-
Psalms49:8 the ransom for a life is costly
Psalms49:9 that he should live on forever and not see decay.
Psalms49:10 For all can see that wise men die; the foolish and the senseless alike perish and leave their wealth to others.
Psalms49:11 Their tombs will remain their houses forever
Psalms49:12 But man
Psalms49:13 This is the fate of those who trust in themselves
Psalms49:14 Like sheep they are destined for the grave
Psalms49:15 But God will redeem my life from the grave; he will surely take me to himself. Selah
Psalms49:16 Do not be overawed when a man grows rich
Psalms49:17 for he will take nothing with him when he dies
Psalms49:18 Though while he lived he counted himself blessed- and men praise you when you prosper-
Psalms49:19 he will join the generation of his fathers
Psalms49:20 A man who has riches without understanding is like the beasts that perish.

Psalms50:2 From Zion
Psalms50:3 Our God comes and will not be silent; a fire devours before him
Psalms50:4 He summons the heavens above
Psalms50:5 Gather to me my consecrated ones
Psalms50:6 And the heavens proclaim his righteousness
Psalms50:7 Hear
Psalms50:8 I do not rebuke you for your sacrifices or your burnt offerings
Psalms50:9 I have no need of a bull from your stall or of goats from your pens
Psalms50:10 for every animal of the forest is mine
Psalms50:11 I know every bird in the mountains
Psalms50:12 If I were hungry I would not tell you
Psalms50:13 Do I eat the flesh of bulls or drink the blood of goats?
Psalms50:14 Sacrifice thank offerings to God
Psalms50:15 and call upon me in the day of trouble; I will deliver you
Psalms50:16 But to the wicked
Psalms50:17 You hate my instruction and cast my words behind you.
Psalms50:18 When you see a thief
Psalms50:19 You use your mouth for evil and harness your tongue to deceit.
Psalms50:20 You speak continually against your brother and slander your own mother's son.
Psalms50:21 These things you have done and I kept silent; you thought I was altogether like you. But I will rebuke you and accuse you to your face.
Psalms50:22 Consider this
Psalms50:23 He who sacrifices thank offerings honors me

Psalms51:2 Wash away all my iniquity and cleanse me from my sin.
Psalms51:3 For I know my transgressions
Psalms51:4 Against you
Psalms51:5 Surely I was sinful at birth
Psalms51:6 Surely you desire truth in the inner parts; you teach me wisdom in the inmost place.

Psalms51:8 Let me hear joy and gladness; let the bones you have crushed rejoice.

Psalms56:7 On no account let them escape; in your anger

Psalms58:1 Do you rulers indeed speak justly? Do you judge uprightly among men?
Psalms58:2 No
Psalms58:3 Even from birth the wicked go astray; from the womb they are wayward and speak lies.
Psalms58:4 Their venom is like the venom of a snake
Psalms58:5 that will not heed the tune of the charmer
Psalms58:6 Break the teeth in their mouths
Psalms58:7 Let them vanish like water that flows away; when they draw the bow
Psalms58:8 Like a slug melting away as it moves along
Psalms58:9 Before your pots can feel the heat of the thorns- whether they be green or dry-the wicked will be swept away.
Psalms58:10 The righteous will be glad when they are avenged
Psalms58:11 Then men will say
Psalms59:1 Deliver me from my enemies
Psalms59:2 Deliver me from evildoers and save me from bloodthirsty men.
Psalms59:3 See how they lie in wait for me! Fierce men conspire against me for no offense or sin of mine
Psalms59:4 I have done no wrong
Psalms59:5 O LORD God Almighty
Psalms59:6 They return at evening
Psalms59:7 See what they spew from their mouths- they spew out swords from their lips
Psalms59:8 But you
Psalms59:9 O my Strength
Psalms59:10 my loving God. God will go before me and will let me gloat over those who slander me.
Psalms59:11 But do not kill them
Psalms59:12 For the sins of their mouths
Psalms59:13 consume them in wrath
Psalms59:14 They return at evening
Psalms59:15 They wander about for food and howl if not satisfied.
Psalms59:16 But I will sing of your strength
Psalms59:17 O my Strength
Psalms60:1 You have rejected us
Psalms60:2 You have shaken the land and torn it open; mend its fractures
Psalms60:3 You have shown your people desperate times; you have given us wine that makes us stagger.
Psalms60:4 But for those who fear you
Psalms60:5 Save us and help us with your right hand
Psalms60:6 God has spoken from his sanctuary: In triumph I will parcel out Shechem and measure off the Valley of Succoth.
Psalms60:7 Gilead is mine
Psalms60:8 Moab is my washbasin
Psalms60:9 Who will bring me to the fortified city? Who will lead me to Edom?
Psalms60:10 Is it not you
Psalms60:11 Give us aid against the enemy
Psalms60:12 With God we will gain the victory

Psalms61:2 From the ends of the earth I call to you
Psalms61:3 For you have been my refuge
Psalms61:4 I long to dwell in your tent forever and take refuge in the shelter of your wings. Selah
Psalms61:5 For you have heard my vows
Psalms61:6 Increase the days of the king's life
Psalms61:7 May he be enthroned in God's presence forever; appoint your love and faithfulness to protect him.
Psalms61:8 Then will I ever sing praise to your name and fulfill my vows day after day.

Psalms62:6 He alone is my rock and my salvation; he is my fortress

Psalms62:8 Trust in him at all times
Psalms62:9 Lowborn men are but a breath
Psalms62:10 Do not trust in extortion or take pride in stolen goods; though your riches increase

Psalms63:1 O God
Psalms63:2 I have seen you in the sanctuary and beheld your power and your glory.
Psalms63:3 Because your love is better than life
Psalms63:4 I will praise you as long as I live
Psalms63:5 My soul will be satisfied as with the richest of foods; with singing lips my mouth will praise you.
Psalms63:6 On my bed I remember you; I think of you through the watches of the night.
Psalms63:7 Because you are my help
Psalms63:8 My soul clings to you; your right hand upholds me.
Psalms63:9 They who seek my life will be destroyed; they will go down to the depths of the earth.
Psalms63:10 They will be given over to the sword and become food for jackals.
Psalms63:11 But the king will rejoice in God; all who swear by God's name will praise him
Psalms64:1 Hear me
Psalms64:2 Hide me from the conspiracy of the wicked
Psalms64:3 They sharpen their tongues like swords and aim their words like deadly arrows.
Psalms64:4 They shoot from ambush at the innocent man; they shoot at him suddenly
Psalms64:5 They encourage each other in evil plans
Psalms64:6 They plot injustice and say
Psalms64:7 But God will shoot them with arrows; suddenly they will be struck down.
Psalms64:8 He will turn their own tongues against them and bring them to ruin; all who see them will shake their heads in scorn.
Psalms64:9 All mankind will fear; they will proclaim the works of God and ponder what he has done.
Psalms64:10 Let the righteous rejoice in the LORD and take refuge in him; let all the upright in heart praise him!
Psalms65:1 Praise awaits you
Psalms65:2 O you who hear prayer
Psalms65:3 When we were overwhelmed by sins
Psalms65:4 Blessed are those you choose and bring near to live in your courts! We are filled with the good things of your house
Psalms65:5 You answer us with awesome deeds of righteousness
Psalms65:6 who formed the mountains by your power
Psalms65:7 who stilled the roaring of the seas
Psalms65:8 Those living far away fear your wonders; where morning dawns and evening fades you call forth songs of joy.
Psalms65:9 You care for the land and water it; you enrich it abundantly. The streams of God are filled with water to provide the people with grain
Psalms65:10 You drench its furrows and level its ridges; you soften it with showers and bless its crops.
Psalms65:11 You crown the year with your bounty
Psalms65:12 The grasslands of the desert overflow; the hills are clothed with gladness.
Psalms65:13 The meadows are covered with flocks and the valleys are mantled with grain; they shout for joy and sing.

Psalms66:6 He turned the sea into dry land

Psalms69:1 Save me
Psalms69:2 I sink in the miry depths
Psalms69:3 I am worn out calling for help; my throat is parched. My eyes fail
Psalms69:4 Those who hate me without reason outnumber the hairs of my head; many are my enemies without cause
Psalms69:5 You know my folly
Psalms69:6 May those who hope in you not be disgraced because of me
Psalms69:7 For I endure scorn for your sake
Psalms69:8 I am a stranger to my brothers
Psalms69:9 for zeal for your house consumes me
Psalms69:10 When I weep and fast
Psalms69:11 when I put on sackcloth
Psalms69:12 Those who sit at the gate mock me
Psalms69:13 But I pray to you
Psalms69:14 Rescue me from the mire
Psalms69:15 Do not let the floodwaters engulf me or the depths swallow me up or the pit close its mouth over me.
Psalms69:16 Answer me
Psalms69:17 Do not hide your face from your servant; answer me quickly
Psalms69:18 Come near and rescue me; redeem me because of my foes.
Psalms69:19 You know how I am scorned
Psalms69:20 Scorn has broken my heart and has left me helpless; I looked for sympathy
Psalms69:21 They put gall in my food and gave me vinegar for my thirst.
Psalms69:22 May the table set before them become a snare; may it become retribution and a trap.
Psalms69:23 May their eyes be darkened so they cannot see
Psalms69:24 Pour out your wrath on them; let your fierce anger overtake them.
Psalms69:25 May their place be deserted; let there be no one to dwell in their tents.
Psalms69:26 For they persecute those you wound and talk about the pain of those you hurt.
Psalms69:27 Charge them with crime upon crime; do not let them share in your salvation.
Psalms69:28 May they be blotted out of the book of life and not be listed with the righteous.
Psalms69:29 I am in pain and distress; may your salvation
Psalms69:30 I will praise God's name in song and glorify him with thanksgiving.
Psalms69:31 This will please the LORD more than an ox
Psalms69:32 The poor will see and be glad- you who seek God
Psalms69:33 The LORD hears the needy and does not despise his captive people.
Psalms69:34 Let heaven and earth praise him
Psalms69:35 for God will save Zion and rebuild the cities of Judah. Then people will settle there and possess it;
Psalms69:36 the children of his servants will inherit it

Psalms71:2 Rescue me and deliver me in your righteousness; turn your ear to me and save me.

Psalms71:4 Deliver me
Psalms71:5 For you have been my hope

Psalms71:8 My mouth is filled with your praise
Psalms71:9 Do not cast me away when I am old; do not forsake me when my strength is gone.
Psalms71:10 For my enemies speak against me; those who wait to kill me conspire together.
Psalms71:11 They say

Psalms71:16 I will come and proclaim your mighty acts

Psalms72:1 Endow the king with your justice
Psalms72:2 He will judge your people in righteousness
Psalms72:3 The mountains will bring prosperity to the people
Psalms72:4 He will defend the afflicted among the people and save the children of the needy; he will crush the oppressor.
Psalms72:5 He will endure as long as the sun
Psalms72:6 He will be like rain falling on a mown field
Psalms72:7 In his days the righteous will flourish; prosperity will abound till the moon is no more.
Psalms72:8 He will rule from sea to sea and from the River to the ends of the earth.
Psalms72:9 The desert tribes will bow before him and his enemies will lick the dust.
Psalms72:10 The kings of Tarshish and of distant shores will bring tribute to him; the kings of Sheba and Seba will present him gifts.
Psalms72:11 All kings will bow down to him and all nations will serve him.
Psalms72:12 For he will deliver the needy who cry out
Psalms72:13 He will take pity on the weak and the needy and save the needy from death.
Psalms72:14 He will rescue them from oppression and violence
Psalms72:15 Long may he live! May gold from Sheba be given him. May people ever pray for him and bless him all day long.
Psalms72:16 Let grain abound throughout the land; on the tops of the hills may it sway. Let its fruit flourish like Lebanon; let it thrive like the grass of the field.
Psalms72:17 May his name endure forever; may it continue as long as the sun. All nations will be blessed through him
Psalms72:18 Praise be to the LORD God
Psalms72:19 Praise be to his glorious name forever; may the whole earth be filled with his glory. Amen and Amen.
Psalms72:20 This concludes the prayers of David son of Jesse.
Psalms73:1 A psalm of Asaph. Surely God is good to Israel
Psalms73:2 But as for me
Psalms73:3 For I envied the arrogant when I saw the prosperity of the wicked.
Psalms73:4 They have no struggles; their bodies are healthy and strong.
Psalms73:5 They are free from the burdens common to man; they are not plagued by human ills.
Psalms73:6 Therefore pride is their necklace; they clothe themselves with violence.
Psalms73:7 From their callous hearts comes iniquity; the evil conceits of their minds know no limits.
Psalms73:8 They scoff
Psalms73:9 Their mouths lay claim to heaven
Psalms73:10 Therefore their people turn to them and drink up waters in abundance.
Psalms73:11 They say
Psalms73:12 This is what the wicked are like- always carefree
Psalms73:13 Surely in vain have I kept my heart pure; in vain have I washed my hands in innocence.
Psalms73:14 All day long I have been plagued; I have been punished every morning.
Psalms73:15 If I had said
Psalms73:16 When I tried to understand all this
Psalms73:17 till I entered the sanctuary of God; then I understood their final destiny.
Psalms73:18 Surely you place them on slippery ground; you cast them down to ruin.
Psalms73:19 How suddenly are they destroyed
Psalms73:20 As a dream when one awakes
Psalms73:21 When my heart was grieved and my spirit embittered
Psalms73:22 I was senseless and ignorant; I was a brute beast before you.
Psalms73:23 Yet I am always with you; you hold me by my right hand.
Psalms73:24 You guide me with your counsel
Psalms73:25 Whom have I in heaven but you? And earth has nothing I desire besides you.
Psalms73:26 My flesh and my heart may fail
Psalms73:27 Those who are far from you will perish; you destroy all who are unfaithful to you.
Psalms73:28 But as for me
Psalms74:1 Why have you rejected us forever
Psalms74:2 Remember the people you purchased of old
Psalms74:3 Turn your steps toward these everlasting ruins
Psalms74:4 Your foes roared in the place where you met with us; they set up their standards as signs.
Psalms74:5 They behaved like men wielding axes to cut through a thicket of trees.
Psalms74:6 They smashed all the carved paneling with their axes and hatchets.
Psalms74:7 They burned your sanctuary to the ground; they defiled the dwelling place of your Name.
Psalms74:8 They said in their hearts
Psalms74:9 We are given no miraculous signs; no prophets are left
Psalms74:10 How long will the enemy mock you
Psalms74:11 Why do you hold back your hand
Psalms74:12 But you
Psalms74:13 It was you who split open the sea by your power; you broke the heads of the monster in the waters.
Psalms74:14 It was you who crushed the heads of Leviathan and gave him as food to the creatures of the desert.
Psalms74:15 It was you who opened up springs and streams; you dried up the ever flowing rivers.
Psalms74:16 The day is yours
Psalms74:17 It was you who set all the boundaries of the earth; you made both summer and winter.
Psalms74:18 Remember how the enemy has mocked you
Psalms74:19 Do not hand over the life of your dove to wild beasts; do not forget the lives of your afflicted people forever.
Psalms74:20 Have regard for your covenant
Psalms74:21 Do not let the oppressed retreat in disgrace; may the poor and needy praise your name.
Psalms74:22 Rise up
Psalms74:23 Do not ignore the clamor of your adversaries

Psalms75:2 You say

Psalms75:5 Do not lift your horns against heaven; do not speak with outstretched neck.'

Psalms75:7 But it is God who judges: He brings one down

Psalms75:10 I will cut off the horns of all the wicked
Psalms76:1 In Judah God is known; his name is great in Israel.
Psalms76:2 His tent is in Salem
Psalms76:3 There he broke the flashing arrows
Psalms76:4 You are resplendent with light
Psalms76:5 Valiant men lie plundered
Psalms76:6 At your rebuke
Psalms76:7 You alone are to be feared. Who can stand before you when you are angry?
Psalms76:8 From heaven you pronounced judgment
Psalms76:9 when you
Psalms76:10 Surely your wrath against men brings you praise
Psalms76:11 Make vows to the LORD your God and fulfill them; let all the neighboring lands bring gifts to the One to be feared.
Psalms76:12 He breaks the spirit of rulers; he is feared by the kings of the earth.
Psalms77:1 I cried out to God for help; I cried out to God to hear me.
Psalms77:2 When I was in distress
Psalms77:3 I remembered you
Psalms77:4 You kept my eyes from closing; I was too troubled to speak.
Psalms77:5 I thought about the former days
Psalms77:6 I remembered my songs in the night. My heart mused and my spirit inquired:
Psalms77:7 Will the Lord reject forever? Will he never show his favor again?
Psalms77:8 Has his unfailing love vanished forever? Has his promise failed for all time?
Psalms77:9 Has God forgotten to be merciful? Has he in anger withheld his compassion? Selah
Psalms77:10 Then I thought
Psalms77:11 I will remember the deeds of the LORD; yes
Psalms77:12 I will meditate on all your works and consider all your mighty deeds.
Psalms77:13 Your ways
Psalms77:14 You are the God who performs miracles; you display your power among the peoples.
Psalms77:15 With your mighty arm you redeemed your people
Psalms77:16 The waters saw you
Psalms77:17 The clouds poured down water
Psalms77:18 Your thunder was heard in the whirlwind
Psalms77:19 Your path led through the sea
Psalms77:20 You led your people like a flock by the hand of Moses and Aaron.
Psalms78:1 O my people
Psalms78:2 I will open my mouth in parables
Psalms78:3 what we have heard and known
Psalms78:4 We will not hide them from their children; we will tell the next generation the praiseworthy deeds of the LORD
Psalms78:5 He decreed statutes for Jacob and established the law in Israel
Psalms78:6 so the next generation would know them
Psalms78:7 Then they would put their trust in God and would not forget his deeds but would keep his commands.
Psalms78:8 They would not be like their forefathers- a stubborn and rebellious generation
Psalms78:9 The men of Ephraim
Psalms78:10 they did not keep God's covenant and refused to live by his law.
Psalms78:11 They forgot what he had done
Psalms78:12 He did miracles in the sight of their fathers in the land of Egypt
Psalms78:13 He divided the sea and led them through; he made the water stand firm like a wall.
Psalms78:14 He guided them with the cloud by day and with light from the fire all night.
Psalms78:15 He split the rocks in the desert and gave them water as abundant as the seas;
Psalms78:16 he brought streams out of a rocky crag and made water flow down like rivers.
Psalms78:17 But they continued to sin against him
Psalms78:18 They willfully put God to the test by demanding the food they craved.
Psalms78:19 They spoke against God
Psalms78:20 When he struck the rock
Psalms78:21 When the LORD heard them
Psalms78:22 for they did not believe in God or trust in his deliverance.
Psalms78:23 Yet he gave a command to the skies above and opened the doors of the heavens;
Psalms78:24 he rained down manna for the people to eat
Psalms78:25 Men ate the bread of angels; he sent them all the food they could eat.
Psalms78:26 He let loose the east wind from the heavens and led forth the south wind by his power.
Psalms78:27 He rained meat down on them like dust
Psalms78:28 He made them come down inside their camp
Psalms78:29 They ate till they had more than enough
Psalms78:30 But before they turned from the food they craved
Psalms78:31 God's anger rose against them; he put to death the sturdiest among them
Psalms78:32 In spite of all this
Psalms78:33 So he ended their days in futility and their years in terror.
Psalms78:34 Whenever God slew them
Psalms78:35 They remembered that God was their Rock
Psalms78:36 But then they would flatter him with their mouths
Psalms78:37 their hearts were not loyal to him
Psalms78:38 Yet he was merciful; he forgave their iniquities and did not destroy them. Time after time he restrained his anger and did not stir up his full wrath.
Psalms78:39 He remembered that they were but flesh
Psalms78:40 How often they rebelled against him in the desert and grieved him in the wasteland!
Psalms78:41 Again and again they put God to the test; they vexed the Holy One of Israel.
Psalms78:42 They did not remember his power- the day he redeemed them from the oppressor
Psalms78:43 the day he displayed his miraculous signs in Egypt
Psalms78:44 He turned their rivers to blood; they could not drink from their streams.
Psalms78:45 He sent swarms of flies that devoured them
Psalms78:46 He gave their crops to the grasshopper
Psalms78:47 He destroyed their vines with hail and their sycamore-figs with sleet.
Psalms78:48 He gave over their cattle to the hail
Psalms78:49 He unleashed against them his hot anger
Psalms78:50 He prepared a path for his anger; he did not spare them from death but gave them over to the plague.
Psalms78:51 He struck down all the firstborn of Egypt
Psalms78:52 But he brought his people out like a flock; he led them like sheep through the desert.
Psalms78:53 He guided them safely
Psalms78:54 Thus he brought them to the border of his holy land
Psalms78:55 He drove out nations before them and allotted their lands to them as an inheritance; he settled the tribes of Israel in their homes.
Psalms78:56 But they put God to the test and rebelled against the Most High; they did not keep his statutes.
Psalms78:57 Like their fathers they were disloyal and faithless
Psalms78:58 They angered him with their high places; they aroused his jealousy with their idols.
Psalms78:59 When God heard them
Psalms78:60 He abandoned the tabernacle of Shiloh
Psalms78:61 He sent the ark of his might into captivity
Psalms78:62 He gave his people over to the sword; he was very angry with his inheritance.
Psalms78:63 Fire consumed their young men
Psalms78:64 their priests were put to the sword
Psalms78:65 Then the Lord awoke as from sleep
Psalms78:66 He beat back his enemies; he put them to everlasting shame.
Psalms78:67 Then he rejected the tents of Joseph
Psalms78:68 but he chose the tribe of Judah
Psalms78:69 He built his sanctuary like the heights
Psalms78:70 He chose David his servant and took him from the sheep pens;
Psalms78:71 from tending the sheep he brought him to be the shepherd of his people Jacob
Psalms78:72 And David shepherded them with integrity of heart; with skillful hands he led them.
Psalms79:1 O God
Psalms79:2 They have given the dead bodies of your servants as food to the birds of the air
Psalms79:3 They have poured out blood like water all around Jerusalem
Psalms79:4 We are objects of reproach to our neighbors
Psalms79:5 How long
Psalms79:6 Pour out your wrath on the nations that do not acknowledge you
Psalms79:7 for they have devoured Jacob and destroyed his homeland.
Psalms79:8 Do not hold against us the sins of the fathers; may your mercy come quickly to meet us
Psalms79:9 Help us
Psalms79:10 Why should the nations say
Psalms79:11 May the groans of the prisoners come before you; by the strength of your arm preserve those condemned to die.
Psalms79:12 Pay back into the laps of our neighbors seven times the reproach they have hurled at you
Psalms79:13 Then we your people
Psalms80:1 Hear us
Psalms80:2 before Ephraim
Psalms80:3 Restore us
Psalms80:4 O LORD God Almighty
Psalms80:5 You have fed them with the bread of tears; you have made them drink tears by the bowlful.
Psalms80:6 You have made us a source of contention to our neighbors

Psalms89:10 You crushed Rahab like one of the slain; with your strong arm you scattered your enemies.

Psalms89:13 Your arm is endued with power; your hand is strong
Psalms89:14 Righteousness and justice are the foundation of your throne; love and faithfulness go before you.
Psalms89:15 Blessed are those who have learned to acclaim you
Psalms89:16 They rejoice in your name all day long; they exult in your righteousness.
Psalms89:17 For you are their glory and strength
Psalms89:18 Indeed

Psalms89:23 I will crush his foes before him and strike down his adversaries.
Psalms89:24 My faithful love will be with him
Psalms89:25 I will set his hand over the sea
Psalms89:26 He will call out to me
Psalms89:27 I will also appoint him my firstborn
Psalms89:28 I will maintain my love to him forever
Psalms89:29 I will establish his line forever
Psalms89:30 If his sons forsake my law and do not follow my statutes
Psalms89:31 if they violate my decrees and fail to keep my commands
Psalms89:32 I will punish their sin with the rod
Psalms89:33 but I will not take my love from him
Psalms89:34 I will not violate my covenant or alter what my lips have uttered.
Psalms89:35 Once for all
Psalms89:36 that his line will continue forever and his throne endure before me like the sun;
Psalms89:37 it will be established forever like the moon
Psalms89:38 But you have rejected
Psalms89:39 You have renounced the covenant with your servant and have defiled his crown in the dust.
Psalms89:40 You have broken through all his walls and reduced his strongholds to ruins.
Psalms89:41 All who pass by have plundered him; he has become the scorn of his neighbors.
Psalms89:42 You have exalted the right hand of his foes; you have made all his enemies rejoice.
Psalms89:43 You have turned back the edge of his sword and have not supported him in battle.
Psalms89:44 You have put an end to his splendor and cast his throne to the ground.
Psalms89:45 You have cut short the days of his youth; you have covered him with a mantle of shame. Selah
Psalms89:46 How long
Psalms89:47 Remember how fleeting is my life. For what futility you have created all men!
Psalms89:48 What man can live and not see death

Psalms89:50 Remember
Psalms89:51 the taunts with which your enemies have mocked

Psalms104:1 Praise the LORD

Psalms104:5 He set the earth on its foundations; it can never be moved.

Psalms104:8 they flowed over the mountains

Psalms105:26 He sent Moses his servant
Psalms105:27 They performed his miraculous signs among them
Psalms105:28 He sent darkness and made the land dark- for had they not rebelled against his words?
Psalms105:29 He turned their waters into blood
Psalms105:30 Their land teemed with frogs
Psalms105:31 He spoke
Psalms105:32 He turned their rain into hail
Psalms105:33 he struck down their vines and fig trees and shattered the trees of their country.
Psalms105:34 He spoke
Psalms105:35 they ate up every green thing in their land
Psalms105:36 Then he struck down all the firstborn in their land
Psalms105:37 He brought out Israel
Psalms105:38 Egypt was glad when they left
Psalms105:39 He spread out a cloud as a covering
Psalms105:40 They asked
Psalms105:41 He opened the rock
Psalms105:42 For he remembered his holy promise given to his servant Abraham.
Psalms105:43 He brought out his people with rejoicing
Psalms105:44 he gave them the lands of the nations
Psalms105:45 that they might keep his precepts and observe his laws. Praise the LORD.
Psalms106:1 Praise the LORD. Give thanks to the LORD
Psalms106:2 Who can proclaim the mighty acts of the LORD or fully declare his praise?
Psalms106:3 Blessed are they who maintain justice
Psalms106:4 Remember me
Psalms106:5 that I may enjoy the prosperity of your chosen ones
Psalms106:6 We have sinned
Psalms106:7 When our fathers were in Egypt
Psalms106:8 Yet he saved them for his name's sake
Psalms106:9 He rebuked the Red Sea
Psalms106:10 He saved them from the hand of the foe; from the hand of the enemy he redeemed them.
Psalms106:11 The waters covered their adversaries; not one of them survived.
Psalms106:12 Then they believed his promises and sang his praise.
Psalms106:13 But they soon forgot what he had done and did not wait for his counsel.
Psalms106:14 In the desert they gave in to their craving; in the wasteland they put God to the test.
Psalms106:15 So he gave them what they asked for
Psalms106:16 In the camp they grew envious of Moses and of Aaron
Psalms106:17 The earth opened up and swallowed Dathan; it buried the company of Abiram.
Psalms106:18 Fire blazed among their followers; a flame consumed the wicked.
Psalms106:19 At Horeb they made a calf and worshiped an idol cast from metal.
Psalms106:20 They exchanged their Glory for an image of a bull
Psalms106:21 They forgot the God who saved them
Psalms106:22 miracles in the land of Ham and awesome deeds by the Red Sea.
Psalms106:23 So he said he would destroy them- had not Moses
Psalms106:24 Then they despised the pleasant land; they did not believe his promise.
Psalms106:25 They grumbled in their tents and did not obey the LORD.
Psalms106:26 So he swore to them with uplifted hand that he would make them fall in the desert
Psalms106:27 make their descendants fall among the nations and scatter them throughout the lands.
Psalms106:28 They yoked themselves to the Baal of Peor and ate sacrifices offered to lifeless gods;
Psalms106:29 they provoked the LORD to anger by their wicked deeds
Psalms106:30 But Phinehas stood up and intervened
Psalms106:31 This was credited to him as righteousness for endless generations to come.
Psalms106:32 By the waters of Meribah they angered the LORD
Psalms106:33 for they rebelled against the Spirit of God
Psalms106:34 They did not destroy the peoples as the LORD had commanded them
Psalms106:35 but they mingled with the nations and adopted their customs.
Psalms106:36 They worshiped their idols
Psalms106:37 They sacrificed their sons and their daughters to demons.
Psalms106:38 They shed innocent blood
Psalms106:39 They defiled themselves by what they did; by their deeds they prostituted themselves.
Psalms106:40 Therefore the LORD was angry with his people and abhorred his inheritance.
Psalms106:41 He handed them over to the nations
Psalms106:42 Their enemies oppressed them and subjected them to their power.
Psalms106:43 Many times he delivered them
Psalms106:44 But he took note of their distress when he heard their cry;
Psalms106:45 for their sake he remembered his covenant and out of his great love he relented.
Psalms106:46 He caused them to be pitied by all who held them captive.
Psalms106:47 Save us
Psalms106:48 Praise be to the LORD
Psalms107:1 Give thanks to the LORD
Psalms107:2 Let the redeemed of the LORD say this- those he redeemed from the hand of the foe
Psalms107:3 those he gathered from the lands
Psalms107:4 Some wandered in desert wastelands
Psalms107:5 They were hungry and thirsty
Psalms107:6 Then they cried out to the LORD in their trouble
Psalms107:7 He led them by a straight way to a city where they could settle.
Psalms107:8 Let them give thanks to the LORD for his unfailing love and his wonderful deeds for men
Psalms107:9 for he satisfies the thirsty and fills the hungry with good things.
Psalms107:10 Some sat in darkness and the deepest gloom
Psalms107:11 for they had rebelled against the words of God and despised the counsel of the Most High.
Psalms107:12 So he subjected them to bitter labor; they stumbled
Psalms107:13 Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble
Psalms107:14 He brought them out of darkness and the deepest gloom and broke away their chains.
Psalms107:15 Let them give thanks to the LORD for his unfailing love and his wonderful deeds for men
Psalms107:16 for he breaks down gates of bronze and cuts through bars of iron.
Psalms107:17 Some became fools through their rebellious ways and suffered affliction because of their iniquities.
Psalms107:18 They loathed all food and drew near the gates of death.
Psalms107:19 Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble
Psalms107:20 He sent forth his word and healed them; he rescued them from the grave.
Psalms107:21 Let them give thanks to the LORD for his unfailing love and his wonderful deeds for men.
Psalms107:22 Let them sacrifice thank offerings and tell of his works with songs of joy.
Psalms107:23 Others went out on the sea in ships; they were merchants on the mighty waters.
Psalms107:24 They saw the works of the LORD
Psalms107:25 For he spoke and stirred up a tempest that lifted high the waves.
Psalms107:26 They mounted up to the heavens and went down to the depths; in their peril their courage melted away.
Psalms107:27 They reeled and staggered like drunken men; they were at their wits' end.
Psalms107:28 Then they cried out to the LORD in their trouble
Psalms107:29 He stilled the storm to a whisper; the waves of the sea were hushed.
Psalms107:30 They were glad when it grew calm
Psalms107:31 Let them give thanks to the LORD for his unfailing love and his wonderful deeds for men.
Psalms107:32 Let them exalt him in the assembly of the people and praise him in the council of the elders.
Psalms107:33 He turned rivers into a desert
Psalms107:34 and fruitful land into a salt waste
Psalms107:35 He turned the desert into pools of water and the parched ground into flowing springs;
Psalms107:36 there he brought the hungry to live
Psalms107:37 They sowed fields and planted vineyards that yielded a fruitful harvest;
Psalms107:38 he blessed them
Psalms107:39 Then their numbers decreased
Psalms107:40 he who pours contempt on nobles made them wander in a trackless waste.
Psalms107:41 But he lifted the needy out of their affliction and increased their families like flocks.
Psalms107:42 The upright see and rejoice
Psalms107:43 Whoever is wise

Psalms109:1 O God
Psalms109:2 for wicked and deceitful men have opened their mouths against me; they have spoken against me with lying tongues.
Psalms109:3 With words of hatred they surround me; they attack me without cause.
Psalms109:4 In return for my friendship they accuse me
Psalms109:5 They repay me evil for good
Psalms109:6 Appoint an evil man to oppose him; let an accuser stand at his right hand.
Psalms109:7 When he is tried
Psalms109:8 May his days be few; may another take his place of leadership.
Psalms109:9 May his children be fatherless and his wife a widow.
Psalms109:10 May his children be wandering beggars; may they be driven from their ruined homes.
Psalms109:11 May a creditor seize all he has; may strangers plunder the fruits of his labor.
Psalms109:12 May no one extend kindness to him or take pity on his fatherless children.
Psalms109:13 May his descendants be cut off
Psalms109:14 May the iniquity of his fathers be remembered before the LORD; may the sin of his mother never be blotted out.
Psalms109:15 May their sins always remain before the LORD
Psalms109:16 For he never thought of doing a kindness
Psalms109:17 He loved to pronounce a curse- may it come on him; he found no pleasure in blessing- may it be far from him.
Psalms109:18 He wore cursing as his garment; it entered into his body like water
Psalms109:19 May it be like a cloak wrapped about him
Psalms109:20 May this be the LORD's payment to my accusers
Psalms109:21 But you
Psalms109:22 For I am poor and needy
Psalms109:23 I fade away like an evening shadow; I am shaken off like a locust.
Psalms109:24 My knees give way from fasting; my body is thin and gaunt.
Psalms109:25 I am an object of scorn to my accusers; when they see me
Psalms109:26 Help me
Psalms109:27 Let them know that it is your hand
Psalms109:28 They may curse
Psalms109:29 My accusers will be clothed with disgrace and wrapped in shame as in a cloak.

Psalms119:1 Blessed are they whose ways are blameless
Psalms119:2 Blessed are they who keep his statutes and seek him with all their heart.
Psalms119:3 They do nothing wrong; they walk in his ways.
Psalms119:4 You have laid down precepts that are to be fully obeyed.
Psalms119:5 Oh
Psalms119:6 Then I would not be put to shame when I consider all your commands.
Psalms119:7 I will praise you with an upright heart as I learn your righteous laws.
Psalms119:8 I will obey your decrees; do not utterly forsake me.
Psalms119:9 How can a young man keep his way pure? By living according to your word.
Psalms119:10 I seek you with all my heart; do not let me stray from your commands.
Psalms119:11 I have hidden your word in my heart that I might not sin against you.

Psalms119:13 With my lips I recount all the laws that come from your mouth.
Psalms119:14 I rejoice in following your statutes as one rejoices in great riches.

Psalms119:16 I delight in your decrees; I will not neglect your word.
Psalms119:17 Do good to your servant
Psalms119:18 Open my eyes that I may see wonderful things in your law.
Psalms119:19 I am a stranger on earth; do not hide your commands from me.

Psalms119:21 You rebuke the arrogant
Psalms119:22 Remove from me scorn and contempt

Psalms119:27 Let me understand the teaching of your precepts; then I will meditate on your wonders.
Psalms119:28 My soul is weary with sorrow; strengthen me according to your word.
Psalms119:29 Keep me from deceitful ways; be gracious to me through your law.
Psalms119:30 I have chosen the way of truth; I have set my heart on your laws.

Psalms119:39 Take away the disgrace I dread

Psalms119:42 then I will answer the one who taunts me
Psalms119:43 Do not snatch the word of truth from my mouth

Psalms119:45 I will walk about in freedom

Psalms136:25 and who gives food to every creature. His love endures forever.

Psalms147:5 Great is our Lord and mighty in power; his understanding has no limit.
Psalms147:6 The LORD sustains the humble but casts the wicked to the ground.

Psalms147:8 He covers the sky with clouds; he supplies the earth with rain and makes grass grow on the hills.

Psalms147:11 the LORD delights in those who fear him

Psalms147:13 for he strengthens the bars of your gates and blesses your people within you.
Psalms147:14 He grants peace to your borders and satisfies you with the finest of wheat.
Psalms147:15 He sends his command to the earth; his word runs swiftly.
Psalms147:16 He spreads the snow like wool and scatters the frost like ashes.

Psalms147:19 He has revealed his word to Jacob
Psalms147:20 He has done this for no other nation; they do not know his laws. Praise the LORD.

Proverbs1:1 The proverbs of Solomon son of David
Proverbs1:2 for attaining wisdom and discipline; for understanding words of insight;
Proverbs1:3 for acquiring a disciplined and prudent life
Proverbs1:4 for giving prudence to the simple
Proverbs1:5 let the wise listen and add to their learning
Proverbs1:6 for understanding proverbs and parables
Proverbs1:7 The fear of the Lord is the beginning of knowledge
Proverbs1:8 Listen
Proverbs1:9 They will be a garland to grace your head and a chain to adorn your neck.
Proverbs1:10 My son
Proverbs1:11 If they say
Proverbs1:12 let's swallow them alive
Proverbs1:13 we will get all sorts of valuable things and fill our houses with plunder;
Proverbs1:14 throw in your lot with us
Proverbs1:15 my son
Proverbs1:16 for their feet rush into sin
Proverbs1:17 How useless to spread a net in full view of all the birds!
Proverbs1:18 These men lie in wait for their own blood; they waylay only themselves!
Proverbs1:19 Such is the end of all who go after ill-gotten gain; it takes away the lives of those who get it.
Proverbs1:20 Wisdom calls aloud in the street
Proverbs1:21 at the head of the noisy streets she cries out
Proverbs1:22 How long will you simple ones love your simple ways? How long will mockers delight in mockery and fools hate knowledge?
Proverbs1:23 If you had responded to my rebuke
Proverbs1:24 But since you rejected me when I called and no one gave heed when I stretched out my hand
Proverbs1:25 since you ignored all my advice and would not accept my rebuke
Proverbs1:26 I in turn will laugh at your disaster; I will mock when calamity overtakes you-
Proverbs1:27 when calamity overtakes you like a storm
Proverbs1:28 Then they will call to me but I will not answer; they will look for me but will not find me.
Proverbs1:29 Since they hated knowledge and did not choose to fear the LORD
Proverbs1:30 since they would not accept my advice and spurned my rebuke
Proverbs1:31 they will eat the fruit of their ways and be filled with the fruit of their schemes.
Proverbs1:32 For the waywardness of the simple will kill them
Proverbs1:33 but whoever listens to me will live in safety and be at ease
Proverbs2:1 My son
Proverbs2:2 turning your ear to wisdom and applying your heart to understanding
Proverbs2:3 and if you call out for insight and cry aloud for understanding
Proverbs2:4 and if you look for it as for silver and search for it as for hidden treasure
Proverbs2:5 then you will understand the fear of the LORD and find the knowledge of God.
Proverbs2:6 For the LORD gives wisdom
Proverbs2:7 He holds victory in store for the upright
Proverbs2:8 for he guards the course of the just and protects the way of his faithful ones.
Proverbs2:9 Then you will understand what is right and just and fair-every good path.
Proverbs2:10 For wisdom will enter your heart
Proverbs2:11 Discretion will protect you
Proverbs2:12 Wisdom will save you from the ways of wicked men
Proverbs2:13 who leave the straight paths to walk in dark ways
Proverbs2:14 who delight in doing wrong and rejoice in the perverseness of evil
Proverbs2:15 whose paths are crooked and who are devious in their ways.
Proverbs2:16 It will save you also from the adulteress
Proverbs2:17 who has left the partner of her youth and ignored the covenant she made before God.
Proverbs2:18 For her house leads down to death and her paths to the spirits of the dead.
Proverbs2:19 None who go to her return or attain the paths of life.
Proverbs2:20 Thus you will walk in the ways of good men and keep to the paths of the righteous.
Proverbs2:21 For the upright will live in the land
Proverbs2:22 but the wicked will be cut off from the land
Proverbs3:1 My son
Proverbs3:2 for they will prolong your life many years and bring you prosperity.
Proverbs3:3 Let love and faithfulness never leave you; bind them around your neck
Proverbs3:4 Then you will win favor and a good name in the sight of God and man.
Proverbs3:5 Trust in the LORD with all your heart and lean not on your own understanding;
Proverbs3:6 in all your ways acknowledge him
Proverbs3:7 Do not be wise in your own eyes; fear the LORD and shun evil.
Proverbs3:8 This will bring health to your body and nourishment to your bones.
Proverbs3:9 Honor the LORD with your wealth
Proverbs3:10 then your barns will be filled to overflowing
Proverbs3:11 My son
Proverbs3:12 because the LORD disciplines those he loves
Proverbs3:13 Blessed is the man who finds wisdom
Proverbs3:14 for she is more profitable than silver and yields better returns than gold.
Proverbs3:15 She is more precious than rubies; nothing you desire can compare with her.
Proverbs3:16 Long life is in her right hand; in her left hand are riches and honor.
Proverbs3:17 Her ways are pleasant ways
Proverbs3:18 She is a tree of life to those who embrace her; those who lay hold of her will be blessed.
Proverbs3:19 By wisdom the LORD laid the earth's foundations
Proverbs3:20 by his knowledge the deeps were divided
Proverbs3:21 My son
Proverbs3:22 they will be life for you
Proverbs3:23 Then you will go on your way in safety
Proverbs3:24 when you lie down
Proverbs3:25 Have no fear of sudden disaster or of the ruin that overtakes the wicked
Proverbs3:26 for the LORD will be your confidence and will keep your foot from being snared.
Proverbs3:27 Do not withhold good from those who deserve it
Proverbs3:28 Do not say to your neighbor
Proverbs3:29 Do not plot harm against your neighbor
Proverbs3:30 Do not accuse a man for no reason- when he has done you no harm.
Proverbs3:31 Do not envy a violent man or choose any of his ways
Proverbs3:32 for the LORD detests a perverse man but takes the upright into his confidence.
Proverbs3:33 The LORD's curse is on the house of the wicked
Proverbs3:34 He mocks proud mockers but gives grace to the humble.
Proverbs3:35 The wise inherit honor
Proverbs4:1 Listen
Proverbs4:2 I give you sound learning
Proverbs4:3 When I was a boy in my father's house
Proverbs4:4 he taught me and said
Proverbs4:5 Get wisdom
Proverbs4:6 Do not forsake wisdom
Proverbs4:7 Wisdom is supreme; therefore get wisdom. Though it cost all you have
Proverbs4:8 Esteem her
Proverbs4:9 She will set a garland of grace on your head and present you with a crown of splendor.
Proverbs4:10 Listen
Proverbs4:11 I guide you in the way of wisdom and lead you along straight paths.
Proverbs4:12 When you walk
Proverbs4:13 Hold on to instruction
Proverbs4:14 Do not set foot on the path of the wicked or walk in the way of evil men.
Proverbs4:15 Avoid it
Proverbs4:16 For they cannot sleep till they do evil; they are robbed of slumber till they make someone fall.
Proverbs4:17 They eat the bread of wickedness and drink the wine of violence.
Proverbs4:18 The path of the righteous is like the first gleam of dawn
Proverbs4:19 But the way of the wicked is like deep darkness; they do not know what makes them stumble.
Proverbs4:20 My son
Proverbs4:21 Do not let them out of your sight
Proverbs4:22 for they are life to those who find them and health to a man's whole body.
Proverbs4:23 Above all else
Proverbs4:24 Put away perversity from your mouth; keep corrupt talk far from your lips.
Proverbs4:25 Let your eyes look straight ahead
Proverbs4:26 Make level paths for your feet and take only ways that are firm.
Proverbs4:27 Do not swerve to the right or the left; keep your foot from evil.
Proverbs5:1 My son
Proverbs5:2 that you may maintain discretion and your lips may preserve knowledge.
Proverbs5:3 For the lips of an adulteress drip honey
Proverbs5:4 but in the end she is bitter as gall
Proverbs5:5 Her feet go down to death; her steps lead straight to the grave.
Proverbs5:6 She gives no thought to the way of life; her paths are crooked
Proverbs5:7 Now then
Proverbs5:8 Keep to a path far from her
Proverbs5:9 lest you give your best strength to others and your years to one who is cruel
Proverbs5:10 lest strangers feast on your wealth and your toil enrich another man's house.
Proverbs5:11 At the end of your life you will groan
Proverbs5:12 You will say
Proverbs5:13 I would not obey my teachers or listen to my instructors.
Proverbs5:14 I have come to the brink of utter ruin in the midst of the whole assembly.
Proverbs5:15 Drink water from your own cistern
Proverbs5:16 Should your springs overflow in the streets
Proverbs5:17 Let them be yours alone
Proverbs5:18 May your fountain be blessed
Proverbs5:19 A loving doe
Proverbs5:20 Why be captivated
Proverbs5:21 For a man's ways are in full view of the LORD
Proverbs5:22 The evil deeds of a wicked man ensnare him; the cords of his sin hold him fast.
Proverbs5:23 He will die for lack of discipline
Proverbs6:1 My son
Proverbs6:2 if you have been trapped by what you said
Proverbs6:3 then do this
Proverbs6:4 Allow no sleep to your eyes
Proverbs6:5 Free yourself
Proverbs6:6 Go to the ant
Proverbs6:7 It has no commander
Proverbs6:8 yet it stores its provisions in summer and gathers its food at harvest.
Proverbs6:9 How long will you lie there
Proverbs6:10 A little sleep
Proverbs6:11 and poverty will come on you like a bandit and scarcity like an armed man.
Proverbs6:12 A scoundrel and villain
Proverbs6:13 who winks with his eye
Proverbs6:14 who plots evil with deceit in his heart- he always stirs up dissension.
Proverbs6:15 Therefore disaster will overtake him in an instant; he will suddenly be destroyed-without remedy.
Proverbs6:16 There are six things the LORD hates
Proverbs6:17 haughty eyes
Proverbs6:18 a heart that devises wicked schemes
Proverbs6:19 a false witness who pours out lies and a man who stirs up dissension among brothers.
Proverbs6:20 My son
Proverbs6:21 Bind them upon your heart forever; fasten them around your neck.
Proverbs6:22 When you walk
Proverbs6:23 For these commands are a lamp
Proverbs6:24 keeping you from the immoral woman
Proverbs6:25 Do not lust in your heart after her beauty or let her captivate you with her eyes
Proverbs6:26 for the prostitute reduces you to a loaf of bread
Proverbs6:27 Can a man scoop fire into his lap without his clothes being burned?
Proverbs6:28 Can a man walk on hot coals without his feet being scorched?
Proverbs6:29 So is he who sleeps with another man's wife; no one who touches her will go unpunished.
Proverbs6:30 Men do not despise a thief if he steals to satisfy his hunger when he is starving.
Proverbs6:31 Yet if he is caught
Proverbs6:32 But a man who commits adultery lacks judgment; whoever does so destroys himself.
Proverbs6:33 Blows and disgrace are his lot
Proverbs6:34 for jealousy arouses a husband's fury
Proverbs6:35 He will not accept any compensation; he will refuse the bribe
Proverbs7:1 My son
Proverbs7:2 Keep my commands and you will live; guard my teachings as the apple of your eye.
Proverbs7:3 Bind them on your fingers; write them on the tablet of your heart.
Proverbs7:4 Say to wisdom
Proverbs7:5 they will keep you from the adulteress
Proverbs7:6 At the window of my house I looked out through the lattice.
Proverbs7:7 I saw among the simple
Proverbs7:8 He was going down the street near her corner
Proverbs7:9 at twilight
Proverbs7:10 Then out came a woman to meet him
Proverbs7:11 (She is loud and defiant
Proverbs7:12 now in the street
Proverbs7:13 She took hold of him and kissed him and with a brazen face she said:
Proverbs7:14 I have fellowship offerings at home; today I fulfilled my vows.
Proverbs7:15 So I came out to meet you; I looked for you and have found you!
Proverbs7:16 I have covered my bed with colored linens from Egypt.
Proverbs7:17 I have perfumed my bed with myrrh
Proverbs7:18 Come
Proverbs7:19 My husband is not at home; he has gone on a long journey.
Proverbs7:20 He took his purse filled with money and will not be home till full moon.
Proverbs7:21 With persuasive words she led him astray; she seduced him with her smooth talk.
Proverbs7:22 All at once he followed her like an ox going to the slaughter
Proverbs7:23 till an arrow pierces his liver
Proverbs7:24 Now then
Proverbs7:25 Do not let your heart turn to her ways or stray into her paths.
Proverbs7:26 Many are the victims she has brought down; her slain are a mighty throng.
Proverbs7:27 Her house is a highway to the grave
Proverbs8:1 Does not wisdom call out? Does not understanding raise her voice?
Proverbs8:2 On the heights along the way
Proverbs8:3 beside the gates leading into the city
Proverbs8:4 To you
Proverbs8:5 You who are simple
Proverbs8:6 Listen
Proverbs8:7 My mouth speaks what is true
Proverbs8:8 All the words of my mouth are just; none of them is crooked or perverse.
Proverbs8:9 To the discerning all of them are right; they are faultless to those who have knowledge.
Proverbs8:10 Choose my instruction instead of silver
Proverbs8:11 for wisdom is more precious than rubies
Proverbs8:12 I
Proverbs8:13 To fear the LORD is to hate evil; I hate pride and arrogance
Proverbs8:14 Counsel and sound judgment are mine; I have understanding and power.
Proverbs8:15 By me kings reign and rulers make laws that are just;
Proverbs8:16 by me princes govern
Proverbs8:17 I love those who love me
Proverbs8:18 With me are riches and honor
Proverbs8:19 My fruit is better than fine gold; what I yield surpasses choice silver.
Proverbs8:20 I walk in the way of righteousness
Proverbs8:21 bestowing wealth on those who love me and making their treasuries full.
Proverbs8:22 The LORD brought me forth as the first of his works
Proverbs8:23 I was appointed from eternity
Proverbs8:24 When there were no oceans
Proverbs8:25 before the mountains were settled in place
Proverbs8:26 before he made the earth or its fields or any of the dust of the world.
Proverbs8:27 I was there when he set the heavens in place
Proverbs8:28 when he established the clouds above and fixed securely the fountains of the deep
Proverbs8:29 when he gave the sea its boundary so the waters would not overstep his command
Proverbs8:30 Then I was the craftsman at his side. I was filled with delight day after day
Proverbs8:31 rejoicing in his whole world and delighting in mankind.
Proverbs8:32 Now then
Proverbs8:33 Listen to my instruction and be wise; do not ignore it.
Proverbs8:34 Blessed is the man who listens to me
Proverbs8:35 For whoever finds me finds life and receives favor from the LORD.
Proverbs8:36 But whoever fails to find me harms himself; all who hate me love death.
Proverbs9:1 Wisdom has built her house; she has hewn out its seven pillars.
Proverbs9:2 She has prepared her meat and mixed her wine; she has also set her table.
Proverbs9:3 She has sent out her maids
Proverbs9:4 Let all who are simple come in here! she says to those who lack judgment.
Proverbs9:5 Come
Proverbs9:6 Leave your simple ways and you will live; walk in the way of understanding.
Proverbs9:7 Whoever corrects a mocker invites insult; whoever rebukes a wicked man incurs abuse.
Proverbs9:8 Do not rebuke a mocker or he will hate you; rebuke a wise man and he will love you.
Proverbs9:9 Instruct a wise man and he will be wiser still; teach a righteous man and he will add to his learning.
Proverbs9:10 The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom
Proverbs9:11 For through me your days will be many
Proverbs9:12 If you are wise
Proverbs9:13 The woman Folly is loud; she is undisciplined and without knowledge.
Proverbs9:14 She sits at the door of her house
Proverbs9:15 calling out to those who pass by
Proverbs9:16 Let all who are simple come in here! she says to those who lack judgment.
Proverbs9:17 Stolen water is sweet; food eaten in secret is delicious!
Proverbs9:18 But little do they know that the dead are there
