Proverbs31:1 The sayings of King Lemuel-an oracle his mother taught him:
Proverbs31:2 O my son
Proverbs31:3 do not spend your strength on women
Proverbs31:4 It is not for kings
Proverbs31:5 lest they drink and forget what the law decrees
Proverbs31:6 Give beer to those who are perishing
Proverbs31:7 let them drink and forget their poverty and remember their misery no more.
Proverbs31:8 Speak up for those who cannot speak for themselves
Proverbs31:9 Speak up and judge fairly; defend the rights of the poor and needy. Epilogue: The Wife of Noble Character
Proverbs31:10 A wife of noble character who can find? She is worth far more than rubies.
Proverbs31:11 Her husband has full confidence in her and lacks nothing of value.
Proverbs31:12 She brings him good
Proverbs31:13 She selects wool and flax and works with eager hands.
Proverbs31:14 She is like the merchant ships
Proverbs31:15 She gets up while it is still dark; she provides food for her family and portions for her servant girls.
Proverbs31:16 She considers a field and buys it; out of her earnings she plants a vineyard.
Proverbs31:17 She sets about her work vigorously; her arms are strong for her tasks.
Proverbs31:18 She sees that her trading is profitable
Proverbs31:19 In her hand she holds the distaff and grasps the spindle with her fingers.
Proverbs31:20 She opens her arms to the poor and extends her hands to the needy.
Proverbs31:21 When it snows
Proverbs31:22 She makes coverings for her bed; she is clothed in fine linen and purple.
Proverbs31:23 Her husband is respected at the city gate
Proverbs31:24 She makes linen garments and sells them
Proverbs31:25 She is clothed with strength and dignity; she can laugh at the days to come.
Proverbs31:26 She speaks with wisdom
Proverbs31:27 She watches over the affairs of her household and does not eat the bread of idleness.
Proverbs31:28 Her children arise and call her blessed; her husband also
Proverbs31:29 Many women do noble things
Proverbs31:30 Charm is deceptive
Proverbs31:31 Give her the reward she has earned
Ecclesiastes1:1 The words of the Teacher
Ecclesiastes1:2 Meaningless! Meaningless! says the Teacher. Utterly meaningless! Everything is meaningless.
Ecclesiastes1:3 What does man gain from all his labor at which he toils under the sun?
Ecclesiastes1:4 Generations come and generations go
Ecclesiastes1:5 The sun rises and the sun sets
Ecclesiastes1:6 The wind blows to the south and turns to the north; round and round it goes
Ecclesiastes1:7 All streams flow into the sea
Ecclesiastes1:8 All things are wearisome
Ecclesiastes1:9 What has been will be again
Ecclesiastes1:10 Is there anything of which one can say
Ecclesiastes1:11 There is no remembrance of men of old
Ecclesiastes1:12 I
Ecclesiastes1:13 I devoted myself to study and to explore by wisdom all that is done under heaven. What a heavy burden God has laid on men!
Ecclesiastes1:14 I have seen all the things that are done under the sun; all of them are meaningless
Ecclesiastes1:15 What is twisted cannot be straightened; what is lacking cannot be counted.
Ecclesiastes1:16 I thought to myself
Ecclesiastes1:17 Then I applied myself to the understanding of wisdom
Ecclesiastes1:18 For with much wisdom comes much sorrow; the more knowledge
Ecclesiastes2:1 I thought in my heart
Ecclesiastes2:2 Laughter
Ecclesiastes2:3 I tried cheering myself with wine
Ecclesiastes2:4 I undertook great projects: I built houses for myself and planted vineyards.
Ecclesiastes2:5 I made gardens and parks and planted all kinds of fruit trees in them.
Ecclesiastes2:6 I made reservoirs to water groves of flourishing trees.
Ecclesiastes2:7 I bought male and female slaves and had other slaves who were born in my house. I also owned more herds and flocks than anyone in Jerusalem before me.
Ecclesiastes2:8 I amassed silver and gold for myself
Ecclesiastes2:9 I became greater by far than anyone in Jerusalem before me. In all this my wisdom stayed with me.
Ecclesiastes2:10 I denied myself nothing my eyes desired; I refused my heart no pleasure. My heart took delight in all my work
Ecclesiastes2:11 Yet when I surveyed all that my hands had done and what I had toiled to achieve
Ecclesiastes2:12 Then I turned my thoughts to consider wisdom
Ecclesiastes2:13 I saw that wisdom is better than folly
Ecclesiastes2:14 The wise man has eyes in his head
Ecclesiastes2:15 Then I thought in my heart
Ecclesiastes2:16 For the wise man
Ecclesiastes2:17 So I hated life
Ecclesiastes2:18 I hated all the things I had toiled for under the sun
Ecclesiastes2:19 And who knows whether he will be a wise man or a fool? Yet he will have control over all the work into which I have poured my effort and skill under the sun. This too is meaningless.
Ecclesiastes2:20 So my heart began to despair over all my toilsome labor under the sun.
Ecclesiastes2:21 For a man may do his work with wisdom
Ecclesiastes2:22 What does a man get for all the toil and anxious striving with which he labors under the sun?
Ecclesiastes2:23 All his days his work is pain and grief; even at night his mind does not rest. This too is meaningless.
Ecclesiastes2:24 A man can do nothing better than to eat and drink and find satisfaction in his work. This too
Ecclesiastes2:25 for without him
Ecclesiastes2:26 To the man who pleases him
Ecclesiastes3:1 There is a time for everything
Ecclesiastes3:2 a time to be born and a time to die
Ecclesiastes3:3 a time to kill and a time to heal
Ecclesiastes3:4 a time to weep and a time to laugh
Ecclesiastes3:5 a time to scatter stones and a time to gather them
Ecclesiastes3:6 a time to search and a time to give up
Ecclesiastes3:7 a time to tear and a time to mend
Ecclesiastes3:8 a time to love and a time to hate
Ecclesiastes3:9 What does the worker gain from his toil?
Ecclesiastes3:10 I have seen the burden God has laid on men.
Ecclesiastes3:11 He has made everything beautiful in its time. He has also set eternity in the hearts of men; yet they cannot fathom what God has done from beginning to end.
Ecclesiastes3:12 I know that there is nothing better for men than to be happy and do good while they live.
Ecclesiastes3:13 That everyone may eat and drink
Ecclesiastes3:14 I know that everything God does will endure forever; nothing can be added to it and nothing taken from it. God does it so that men will revere him.
Ecclesiastes3:15 Whatever is has already been
Ecclesiastes3:16 And I saw something else under the sun: In the place of judgment-wickedness was there
Ecclesiastes3:17 I thought in my heart
Ecclesiastes3:18 I also thought
Ecclesiastes3:19 Man's fate is like that of the animals; the same fate awaits them both: As one dies
Ecclesiastes3:20 All go to the same place; all come from dust
Ecclesiastes3:21 Who knows if the spirit of man rises upward and if the spirit of the animal goes down into the earth?
Ecclesiastes3:22 So I saw that there is nothing better for a man than to enjoy his work
Ecclesiastes4:1 Again I looked and saw all the oppression that was taking place under the sun: I saw the tears of the oppressed- and they have no comforter; power was on the side of their oppressors- and they have no comforter.
Ecclesiastes4:2 And I declared that the dead
Ecclesiastes4:3 But better than both is he who has not yet been
Ecclesiastes4:4 And I saw that all labor and all achievement spring from man's envy of his neighbor. This too is meaningless
Ecclesiastes4:5 The fool folds his hands and ruins himself.
Ecclesiastes4:6 Better one handful with tranquillity than two handfuls with toil and chasing after the wind.
Ecclesiastes4:7 Again I saw something meaningless under the sun:
Ecclesiastes4:8 There was a man all alone; he had neither son nor brother. There was no end to his toil
Ecclesiastes4:9 Two are better than one
Ecclesiastes4:10 If one falls down
Ecclesiastes4:11 Also
Ecclesiastes4:12 Though one may be overpowered
Ecclesiastes4:13 Better a poor but wise youth than an old but foolish king who no longer knows how to take warning.
Ecclesiastes4:14 The youth may have come from prison to the kingship
Ecclesiastes4:15 I saw that all who lived and walked under the sun followed the youth
Ecclesiastes4:16 There was no end to all the people who were before them. But those who came later were not pleased with the successor. This too is meaningless
Ecclesiastes5:1 Guard your steps when you go to the house of God. Go near to listen rather than to offer the sacrifice of fools
Ecclesiastes5:2 Do not be quick with your mouth
Ecclesiastes5:3 As a dream comes when there are many cares
Ecclesiastes5:4 When you make a vow to God
Ecclesiastes5:5 It is better not to vow than to make a vow and not fulfill it.
Ecclesiastes5:6 Do not let your mouth lead you into sin. And do not protest to the temple messenger
Ecclesiastes5:7 Much dreaming and many words are meaningless. Therefore stand in awe of God.
Ecclesiastes5:8 If you see the poor oppressed in a district
Ecclesiastes5:9 The increase from the land is taken by all; the king himself profits from the fields.
Ecclesiastes5:10 Whoever loves money never has money enough; whoever loves wealth is never satisfied with his income. This too is meaningless.
Ecclesiastes5:11 As goods increase
Ecclesiastes5:12 The sleep of a laborer is sweet
Ecclesiastes5:13 I have seen a grievous evil under the sun: wealth hoarded to the harm of its owner
Ecclesiastes5:14 or wealth lost through some misfortune
Ecclesiastes5:15 Naked a man comes from his mother's womb
Ecclesiastes5:16 This too is a grievous evil: As a man comes
Ecclesiastes5:17 All his days he eats in darkness
Ecclesiastes5:18 Then I realized that it is good and proper for a man to eat and drink
Ecclesiastes5:19 Moreover
Ecclesiastes5:20 He seldom reflects on the days of his life
Ecclesiastes6:1 I have seen another evil under the sun
Ecclesiastes6:2 God gives a man wealth
Ecclesiastes6:3 A man may have a hundred children and live many years; yet no matter how long he lives
Ecclesiastes6:4 It comes without meaning
Ecclesiastes6:5 Though it never saw the sun or knew anything
Ecclesiastes6:6 even if he lives a thousand years twice over but fails to enjoy his prosperity. Do not all go to the same place?
Ecclesiastes6:7 All man's efforts are for his mouth
Ecclesiastes6:8 What advantage has a wise man over a fool? What does a poor man gain by knowing how to conduct himself before others?
Ecclesiastes6:9 Better what the eye sees than the roving of the appetite. This too is meaningless
Ecclesiastes6:10 Whatever exists has already been named
Ecclesiastes6:11 The more the words
Ecclesiastes6:12 For who knows what is good for a man in life
Ecclesiastes7:1 A good name is better than fine perfume
Ecclesiastes7:2 It is better to go to a house of mourning than to go to a house of feasting
Ecclesiastes7:3 Sorrow is better than laughter
Ecclesiastes7:4 The heart of the wise is in the house of mourning
Ecclesiastes7:5 It is better to heed a wise man's rebuke than to listen to the song of fools.
Ecclesiastes7:6 Like the crackling of thorns under the pot
Ecclesiastes7:7 Extortion turns a wise man into a fool
Ecclesiastes7:8 The end of a matter is better than its beginning
Ecclesiastes7:9 Do not be quickly provoked in your spirit
Ecclesiastes7:10 Do not say
Ecclesiastes7:11 Wisdom
Ecclesiastes7:12 Wisdom is a shelter as money is a shelter
Ecclesiastes7:13 Consider what God has done: Who can straighten what he has made crooked?
Ecclesiastes7:14 When times are good
Ecclesiastes7:15 In this meaningless life of mine I have seen both of these: a righteous man perishing in his righteousness
Ecclesiastes7:16 Do not be overrighteous
Ecclesiastes7:17 Do not be overwicked
Ecclesiastes7:18 It is good to grasp the one and not let go of the other. The man who fears God will avoid all extremes.
Ecclesiastes7:19 Wisdom makes one wise man more powerful than ten rulers in a city.
Ecclesiastes7:20 There is not a righteous man on earth who does what is right and never sins.
Ecclesiastes7:21 Do not pay attention to every word people say
Ecclesiastes7:22 for you know in your heart that many times you yourself have cursed others.
Ecclesiastes7:23 All this I tested by wisdom and I said
Ecclesiastes7:24 Whatever wisdom may be
Ecclesiastes7:25 So I turned my mind to understand
Ecclesiastes7:26 I find more bitter than death the woman who is a snare
Ecclesiastes7:27 Look
Ecclesiastes7:28 while I was still searching but not finding- I found one upright man among a thousand
Ecclesiastes7:29 This only have I found: God made mankind upright
Ecclesiastes8:1 Who is like the wise man? Who knows the explanation of things? Wisdom brightens a man's face and changes its hard appearance.
Ecclesiastes8:2 Obey the king's command
Ecclesiastes8:3 Do not be in a hurry to leave the king's presence. Do not stand up for a bad cause
Ecclesiastes8:4 Since a king's word is supreme
Ecclesiastes8:5 Whoever obeys his command will come to no harm
Ecclesiastes8:6 For there is a proper time and procedure for every matter
Ecclesiastes8:7 Since no man knows the future
Ecclesiastes8:8 No man has power over the wind to contain it; so no one has power over the day of his death. As no one is discharged in time of war
Ecclesiastes8:9 All this I saw
Ecclesiastes8:10 Then too
Ecclesiastes8:11 When the sentence for a crime is not quickly carried out
Ecclesiastes8:12 Although a wicked man commits a hundred crimes and still lives a long time
Ecclesiastes8:13 Yet because the wicked do not fear God
Ecclesiastes8:14 There is something else meaningless that occurs on earth: righteous men who get what the wicked deserve
Ecclesiastes8:15 So I commend the enjoyment of life
Ecclesiastes8:16 When I applied my mind to know wisdom and to observe man's labor on earth-his eyes not seeing sleep day or night-
Ecclesiastes8:17 then I saw all that God has done. No one can comprehend what goes on under the sun. Despite all his efforts to search it out
Ecclesiastes9:1 So I reflected on all this and concluded that the righteous and the wise and what they do are in God's hands
Ecclesiastes9:2 All share a common destiny-the righteous and the wicked
Ecclesiastes9:3 This is the evil in everything that happens under the sun: The same destiny overtakes all. The hearts of men
Ecclesiastes9:4 Anyone who is among the living has hope -even a live dog is better off than a dead lion!
Ecclesiastes9:5 For the living know that they will die
Ecclesiastes9:6 Their love
Ecclesiastes9:7 Go
Ecclesiastes9:8 Always be clothed in white
Ecclesiastes9:9 Enjoy life with your wife
Ecclesiastes9:10 Whatever your hand finds to do
Ecclesiastes9:11 I have seen something else under the sun: The race is not to the swift or the battle to the strong
Ecclesiastes9:12 Moreover
Ecclesiastes9:13 I also saw under the sun this example of wisdom that greatly impressed me:
Ecclesiastes9:14 There was once a small city with only a few people in it. And a powerful king came against it
Ecclesiastes9:15 Now there lived in that city a man poor but wise
Ecclesiastes9:16 So I said
Ecclesiastes9:17 The quiet words of the wise are more to be heeded than the shouts of a ruler of fools.
Ecclesiastes9:18 Wisdom is better than weapons of war
Ecclesiastes10:1 As dead flies give perfume a bad smell
Ecclesiastes10:2 The heart of the wise inclines to the right
Ecclesiastes10:3 Even as he walks along the road
Ecclesiastes10:4 If a ruler's anger rises against you
Ecclesiastes10:5 There is an evil I have seen under the sun
Ecclesiastes10:6 Fools are put in many high positions
Ecclesiastes10:7 I have seen slaves on horseback
Ecclesiastes10:8 Whoever digs a pit may fall into it; whoever breaks through a wall may be bitten by a snake.
Ecclesiastes10:9 Whoever quarries stones may be injured by them; whoever splits logs may be endangered by them.
Ecclesiastes10:10 If the ax is dull and its edge unsharpened
Ecclesiastes10:11 If a snake bites before it is charmed
Ecclesiastes10:12 Words from a wise man's mouth are gracious
Ecclesiastes10:13 At the beginning his words are folly; at the end they are wicked madness-
Ecclesiastes10:14 and the fool multiplies words. No one knows what is coming- who can tell him what will happen after him?
Ecclesiastes10:15 A fool's work wearies him; he does not know the way to town.
Ecclesiastes10:16 Woe to you
Ecclesiastes10:17 Blessed are you
Ecclesiastes10:18 If a man is lazy
Ecclesiastes10:19 A feast is made for laughter
Ecclesiastes10:20 Do not revile the king even in your thoughts
Ecclesiastes11:1 Cast your bread upon the waters
Ecclesiastes11:2 Give portions to seven
Ecclesiastes11:3 If clouds are full of water
Ecclesiastes11:4 Whoever watches the wind will not plant; whoever looks at the clouds will not reap.
Ecclesiastes11:5 As you do not know the path of the wind
Ecclesiastes11:6 Sow your seed in the morning
Ecclesiastes11:7 Light is sweet
Ecclesiastes11:8 However many years a man may live
Ecclesiastes11:9 Be happy
Ecclesiastes11:10 So then
Ecclesiastes12:1 Remember your Creator in the days of your youth
Ecclesiastes12:2 before the sun and the light and the moon and the stars grow dark
Ecclesiastes12:3 when the keepers of the house tremble
Ecclesiastes12:4 when the doors to the street are closed and the sound of grinding fades; when men rise up at the sound of birds
Ecclesiastes12:5 when men are afraid of heights and of dangers in the streets; when the almond tree blossoms and the grasshopper drags himself along and desire no longer is stirred. Then man goes to his eternal home and mourners go about the streets.
Ecclesiastes12:6 Remember him-before the silver cord is severed
Ecclesiastes12:7 and the dust returns to the ground it came from
Ecclesiastes12:8 Meaningless! Meaningless! says the Teacher. Everything is meaningless!
Ecclesiastes12:9 Not only was the Teacher wise
Ecclesiastes12:10 The Teacher searched to find just the right words
Ecclesiastes12:11 The words of the wise are like goads
Ecclesiastes12:12 Be warned
Ecclesiastes12:13 Now all has been heard; here is the conclusion of the matter: Fear God and keep his commandments
Ecclesiastes12:14 For God will bring every deed into judgment
SongOfSongs1:1 Solomon's Song of Songs.

SongOfSongs1:3 Pleasing is the fragrance of your perfumes; your name is like perfume poured out. No wonder the maidens love you!
SongOfSongs1:4 Take me away with you-let us hurry! Let the king bring me into his chambers. We rejoice and delight in you; we will praise your love more than wine. How right they are to adore you!

SongOfSongs1:17 The beams of our house are cedars; our rafters are firs.

SongOfSongs2:6 His left arm is under my head

SongOfSongs2:10 My lover spoke and said to me

Isaiah1:1 The vision concerning Judah and Jerusalem that Isaiah son of Amoz saw during the reigns of Uzziah
Isaiah1:2 Hear
Isaiah1:3 The ox knows his master
Isaiah1:4 Ah
Isaiah1:5 Why should you be beaten anymore? Why do you persist in rebellion? Your whole head is injured
Isaiah1:6 From the sole of your foot to the top of your head there is no soundness- only wounds and welts and open sores
Isaiah1:7 Your country is desolate
Isaiah1:8 The Daughter of Zion is left like a shelter in a vineyard
Isaiah1:9 Unless the LORD Almighty had left us some survivors
Isaiah1:10 Hear the word of the LORD
Isaiah1:11 The multitude of your sacrifices- what are they to me? says the LORD. I have more than enough of burnt offerings
Isaiah1:12 When you come to appear before me
Isaiah1:13 Stop bringing meaningless offerings! Your incense is detestable to me. New Moons
Isaiah1:14 Your New Moon festivals and your appointed feasts my soul hates. They have become a burden to me; I am weary of bearing them.
Isaiah1:15 When you spread out your hands in prayer
Isaiah1:16 wash and make yourselves clean. Take your evil deeds out of my sight! Stop doing wrong
Isaiah1:17 learn to do right! Seek justice
Isaiah1:18 Come now
Isaiah1:19 If you are willing and obedient
Isaiah1:20 but if you resist and rebel
Isaiah1:21 See how the faithful city has become a harlot! She once was full of justice; righteousness used to dwell in her- but now murderers!
Isaiah1:22 Your silver has become dross
Isaiah1:23 Your rulers are rebels
Isaiah1:24 Therefore the Lord
Isaiah1:25 I will turn my hand against you; I will thoroughly purge away your dross and remove all your impurities.
Isaiah1:26 I will restore your judges as in days of old
Isaiah1:27 Zion will be redeemed with justice
Isaiah1:28 But rebels and sinners will both be broken
Isaiah1:29 You will be ashamed because of the sacred oaks in which you have delighted; you will be disgraced because of the gardens that you have chosen.
Isaiah1:30 You will be like an oak with fading leaves
Isaiah1:31 The mighty man will become tinder and his work a spark; both will burn together
Isaiah2:1 This is what Isaiah son of Amoz saw concerning Judah and Jerusalem:
Isaiah2:2 In the last days the mountain of the LORD's temple will be established as chief among the mountains; it will be raised above the hills
Isaiah2:3 Many peoples will come and say
Isaiah2:4 He will judge between the nations and will settle disputes for many peoples. They will beat their swords into plowshares and their spears into pruning hooks. Nation will not take up sword against nation
Isaiah2:5 Come
Isaiah2:6 You have abandoned your people
Isaiah2:7 Their land is full of silver and gold; there is no end to their treasures. Their land is full of horses; there is no end to their chariots.
Isaiah2:8 Their land is full of idols; they bow down to the work of their hands
Isaiah2:9 So man will be brought low and mankind humbled- do not forgive them.
Isaiah2:10 Go into the rocks
Isaiah2:11 The eyes of the arrogant man will be humbled and the pride of men brought low; the LORD alone will be exalted in that day.
Isaiah2:12 The LORD Almighty has a day in store for all the proud and lofty
Isaiah2:13 for all the cedars of Lebanon
Isaiah2:14 for all the towering mountains and all the high hills
Isaiah2:15 for every lofty tower and every fortified wall
Isaiah2:16 for every trading ship and every stately vessel.
Isaiah2:17 The arrogance of man will be brought low and the pride of men humbled; the LORD alone will be exalted in that day
Isaiah2:18 and the idols will totally disappear.
Isaiah2:19 Men will flee to caves in the rocks and to holes in the ground from dread of the LORD and the splendor of his majesty
Isaiah2:20 In that day men will throw away to the rodents and bats their idols of silver and idols of gold
Isaiah2:21 They will flee to caverns in the rocks and to the overhanging crags from dread of the LORD and the splendor of his majesty
Isaiah2:22 Stop trusting in man
Isaiah3:1 See now
Isaiah3:2 the hero and warrior
Isaiah3:3 the captain of fifty and man of rank
Isaiah3:4 I will make boys their officials; mere children will govern them.
Isaiah3:5 People will oppress each other- man against man
Isaiah3:6 A man will seize one of his brothers at his father's home
Isaiah3:7 But in that day he will cry out
Isaiah3:8 Jerusalem staggers
Isaiah3:9 The look on their faces testifies against them; they parade their sin like Sodom; they do not hide it. Woe to them! They have brought disaster upon themselves.
Isaiah3:10 Tell the righteous it will be well with them
Isaiah3:11 Woe to the wicked! Disaster is upon them! They will be paid back for what their hands have done.
Isaiah3:12 Youths oppress my people
Isaiah3:13 The LORD takes his place in court; he rises to judge the people.
Isaiah3:14 The LORD enters into judgment against the elders and leaders of his people: It is you who have ruined my vineyard; the plunder from the poor is in your houses.
Isaiah3:15 What do you mean by crushing my people and grinding the faces of the poor? declares the Lord
Isaiah3:16 The LORD says
Isaiah3:17 Therefore the Lord will bring sores on the heads of the women of Zion; the LORD will make their scalps bald.
Isaiah3:18 In that day the Lord will snatch away their finery: the bangles and headbands and crescent necklaces
Isaiah3:19 the earrings and bracelets and veils
Isaiah3:20 the headdresses and ankle chains and sashes
Isaiah3:21 the signet rings and nose rings
Isaiah3:22 the fine robes and the capes and cloaks
Isaiah3:23 and mirrors
Isaiah3:24 Instead of fragrance there will be a stench; instead of a sash
Isaiah3:25 Your men will fall by the sword
Isaiah3:26 The gates of Zion will lament and mourn; destitute
Isaiah4:1 In that day seven women will take hold of one man and say
Isaiah4:2 In that day the Branch of the LORD will be beautiful and glorious
Isaiah4:3 Those who are left in Zion
Isaiah4:4 The Lord will wash away the filth of the women of Zion; he will cleanse the bloodstains from Jerusalem by a spirit of judgment and a spirit of fire.
Isaiah4:5 Then the LORD will create over all of Mount Zion and over those who assemble there a cloud of smoke by day and a glow of flaming fire by night; over all the glory will be a canopy.
Isaiah4:6 It will be a shelter and shade from the heat of the day
Isaiah5:1 I will sing for the one I love a song about his vineyard: My loved one had a vineyard on a fertile hillside.
Isaiah5:2 He dug it up and cleared it of stones and planted it with the choicest vines. He built a watchtower in it and cut out a winepress as well. Then he looked for a crop of good grapes
Isaiah5:3 Now you dwellers in Jerusalem and men of Judah
Isaiah5:4 What more could have been done for my vineyard than I have done for it? When I looked for good grapes
Isaiah5:5 Now I will tell you what I am going to do to my vineyard: I will take away its hedge
Isaiah5:6 I will make it a wasteland
Isaiah5:7 The vineyard of the LORD Almighty is the house of Israel
Isaiah5:8 Woe to you who add house to house and join field to field till no space is left and you live alone in the land.
Isaiah5:9 The LORD Almighty has declared in my hearing: Surely the great houses will become desolate
Isaiah5:10 A ten-acre vineyard will produce only a bath of wine
Isaiah5:11 Woe to those who rise early in the morning to run after their drinks
Isaiah5:12 They have harps and lyres at their banquets
Isaiah5:13 Therefore my people will go into exile for lack of understanding; their men of rank will die of hunger and their masses will be parched with thirst.
Isaiah5:14 Therefore the grave enlarges its appetite and opens its mouth without limit; into it will descend their nobles and masses with all their brawlers and revelers.
Isaiah5:15 So man will be brought low and mankind humbled
Isaiah5:16 But the LORD Almighty will be exalted by his justice
Isaiah5:17 Then sheep will graze as in their own pasture; lambs will feed among the ruins of the rich.
Isaiah5:18 Woe to those who draw sin along with cords of deceit
Isaiah5:19 to those who say
Isaiah5:20 Woe to those who call evil good and good evil
Isaiah5:21 Woe to those who are wise in their own eyes and clever in their own sight.
Isaiah5:22 Woe to those who are heroes at drinking wine and champions at mixing drinks
Isaiah5:23 who acquit the guilty for a bribe
Isaiah5:24 Therefore
Isaiah5:25 Therefore the LORD's anger burns against his people; his hand is raised and he strikes them down. The mountains shake
Isaiah5:26 He lifts up a banner for the distant nations
Isaiah5:27 Not one of them grows tired or stumbles
Isaiah5:28 Their arrows are sharp
Isaiah5:29 Their roar is like that of the lion
Isaiah5:30 In that day they will roar over it like the roaring of the sea. And if one looks at the land
Isaiah6:1 In the year that King Uzziah died
Isaiah6:2 Above him were seraphs
Isaiah6:3 And they were calling to one another: Holy
Isaiah6:4 At the sound of their voices the doorposts and thresholds shook and the temple was filled with smoke.
Isaiah6:5 Woe to me! I cried. I am ruined! For I am a man of unclean lips
Isaiah6:6 Then one of the seraphs flew to me with a live coal in his hand
Isaiah6:7 With it he touched my mouth and said
Isaiah6:8 Then I heard the voice of the Lord saying
Isaiah6:9 He said
Isaiah6:10 Make the heart of this people calloused; make their ears dull and close their eyes. Otherwise they might see with their eyes
Isaiah6:11 Then I said
Isaiah6:12 until the LORD has sent everyone far away and the land is utterly forsaken.
Isaiah6:13 And though a tenth remains in the land
Isaiah7:1 When Ahaz son of Jotham
Isaiah7:2 Now the house of David was told
Isaiah7:3 Then the LORD said to Isaiah
Isaiah7:4 Say to him
Isaiah7:5 Aram
Isaiah7:6 Let us invade Judah; let us tear it apart and divide it among ourselves
Isaiah7:7 Yet this is what the Sovereign LORD says: 'It will not take place
Isaiah7:8 for the head of Aram is Damascus
Isaiah7:9 The head of Ephraim is Samaria
Isaiah7:10 Again the LORD spoke to Ahaz
Isaiah7:11 Ask the LORD your God for a sign
Isaiah7:12 But Ahaz said
Isaiah7:13 Then Isaiah said
Isaiah7:14 Therefore the Lord himself will give you a sign: The virgin will be with child and will give birth to a son
Isaiah7:15 He will eat curds and honey when he knows enough to reject the wrong and choose the right.
Isaiah7:16 But before the boy knows enough to reject the wrong and choose the right
Isaiah7:17 The LORD will bring on you and on your people and on the house of your father a time unlike any since Ephraim broke away from Judah-he will bring the king of Assyria.
Isaiah7:18 In that day the LORD will whistle for flies from the distant streams of Egypt and for bees from the land of Assyria.
Isaiah7:19 They will all come and settle in the steep ravines and in the crevices in the rocks
Isaiah7:20 In that day the Lord will use a razor hired from beyond the River -the king of Assyria-to shave your head and the hair of your legs
Isaiah7:21 In that day
Isaiah7:22 And because of the abundance of the milk they give
Isaiah7:23 In that day
Isaiah7:24 Men will go there with bow and arrow
Isaiah7:25 As for all the hills once cultivated by the hoe
Isaiah8:1 The LORD said to me
Isaiah8:2 And I will call in Uriah the priest and Zechariah son of Jeberekiah as reliable witnesses for me.
Isaiah8:3 Then I went to the prophetess
Isaiah8:4 Before the boy knows how to say 'My father' or 'My mother
Isaiah8:5 The LORD spoke to me again:
Isaiah8:6 Because this people has rejected the gently flowing waters of Shiloah and rejoices over Rezin and the son of Remaliah
Isaiah8:7 therefore the Lord is about to bring against them the mighty floodwaters of the River - the king of Assyria with all his pomp. It will overflow all its channels
Isaiah8:8 and sweep on into Judah
Isaiah8:9 Raise the war cry
Isaiah8:10 Devise your strategy
Isaiah8:11 The LORD spoke to me with his strong hand upon me
Isaiah8:12 Do not call conspiracy everything that these people call conspiracy; do not fear what they fear
Isaiah8:13 The LORD Almighty is the one you are to regard as holy
Isaiah8:14 and he will be a sanctuary; but for both houses of Israel he will be a stone that causes men to stumble and a rock that makes them fall. And for the people of Jerusalem he will be a trap and a snare.
Isaiah8:15 Many of them will stumble; they will fall and be broken
Isaiah8:16 Bind up the testimony and seal up the law among my disciples.
Isaiah8:17 I will wait for the LORD
Isaiah8:18 Here am I
Isaiah8:19 When men tell you to consult mediums and spiritists
Isaiah8:20 To the law and to the testimony! If they do not speak according to this word
Isaiah8:21 Distressed and hungry
Isaiah8:22 Then they will look toward the earth and see only distress and darkness and fearful gloom
Isaiah9:1 Nevertheless
Isaiah9:2 The people walking in darkness have seen a great light; on those living in the land of the shadow of death a light has dawned.
Isaiah9:3 You have enlarged the nation and increased their joy; they rejoice before you as people rejoice at the harvest
Isaiah9:4 For as in the day of Midian's defeat
Isaiah9:5 Every warrior's boot used in battle and every garment rolled in blood will be destined for burning
Isaiah9:6 For to us a child is born
Isaiah9:7 Of the increase of his government and peace there will be no end. He will reign on David's throne and over his kingdom
Isaiah9:8 The Lord has sent a message against Jacob; it will fall on Israel.
Isaiah9:9 All the people will know it- Ephraim and the inhabitants of Samaria- who say with pride and arrogance of heart
Isaiah9:10 The bricks have fallen down
Isaiah9:11 But the LORD has strengthened Rezin's foes against them and has spurred their enemies on.
Isaiah9:12 Arameans from the east and Philistines from the west have devoured Israel with open mouth. Yet for all this
Isaiah9:13 But the people have not returned to him who struck them
Isaiah9:14 So the LORD will cut off from Israel both head and tail
Isaiah9:15 the elders and prominent men are the head
Isaiah9:16 Those who guide this people mislead them
Isaiah9:17 Therefore the Lord will take no pleasure in the young men
Isaiah9:18 Surely wickedness burns like a fire; it consumes briers and thorns
Isaiah9:19 By the wrath of the LORD Almighty the land will be scorched and the people will be fuel for the fire; no one will spare his brother.
Isaiah9:20 On the right they will devour
Isaiah9:21 Manasseh will feed on Ephraim
Isaiah10:1 Woe to those who make unjust laws
Isaiah10:2 to deprive the poor of their rights and withhold justice from the oppressed of my people
Isaiah10:3 What will you do on the day of reckoning
Isaiah10:4 Nothing will remain but to cringe among the captives or fall among the slain. Yet for all this
Isaiah10:5 Woe to the Assyrian
Isaiah10:6 I send him against a godless nation
Isaiah10:7 But this is not what he intends
Isaiah10:8 'Are not my commanders all kings?' he says.
Isaiah10:9 'Has not Calno fared like Carchemish? Is not Hamath like Arpad
Isaiah10:10 As my hand seized the kingdoms of the idols
Isaiah10:11 shall I not deal with Jerusalem and her images as I dealt with Samaria and her idols?'
Isaiah10:12 When the Lord has finished all his work against Mount Zion and Jerusalem
Isaiah10:13 For he says: 'By the strength of my hand I have done this
Isaiah10:14 As one reaches into a nest
Isaiah10:15 Does the ax raise itself above him who swings it
Isaiah10:16 Therefore
Isaiah10:17 The Light of Israel will become a fire
Isaiah10:18 The splendor of his forests and fertile fields it will completely destroy
Isaiah10:19 And the remaining trees of his forests will be so few that a child could write them down.
Isaiah10:20 In that day the remnant of Israel
Isaiah10:21 A remnant will return
Isaiah10:22 Though your people
Isaiah10:23 The Lord
Isaiah10:24 Therefore
Isaiah10:25 Very soon my anger against you will end and my wrath will be directed to their destruction.
Isaiah10:26 The LORD Almighty will lash them with a whip
Isaiah10:27 In that day their burden will be lifted from your shoulders
Isaiah10:28 They enter Aiath; they pass through Migron; they store supplies at Micmash.
Isaiah10:29 They go over the pass
Isaiah10:30 Cry out
Isaiah10:31 Madmenah is in flight; the people of Gebim take cover.
Isaiah10:32 This day they will halt at Nob; they will shake their fist at the mount of the Daughter of Zion
Isaiah10:33 See
Isaiah10:34 He will cut down the forest thickets with an ax; Lebanon will fall before the Mighty One.
Isaiah11:1 A shoot will come up from the stump of Jesse; from his roots a Branch will bear fruit.
Isaiah11:2 The Spirit of the LORD will rest on him- the Spirit of wisdom and of understanding
Isaiah11:3 and he will delight in the fear of the LORD. He will not judge by what he sees with his eyes
Isaiah11:4 but with righteousness he will judge the needy
Isaiah11:5 Righteousness will be his belt and faithfulness the sash around his waist.
Isaiah11:6 The wolf will live with the lamb
Isaiah11:7 The cow will feed with the bear
Isaiah11:8 The infant will play near the hole of the cobra
Isaiah11:9 They will neither harm nor destroy on all my holy mountain
Isaiah11:10 In that day the Root of Jesse will stand as a banner for the peoples; the nations will rally to him
Isaiah11:11 In that day the Lord will reach out his hand a second time to reclaim the remnant that is left of his people from Assyria
Isaiah11:12 He will raise a banner for the nations and gather the exiles of Israel; he will assemble the scattered people of Judah from the four quarters of the earth.
Isaiah11:13 Ephraim's jealousy will vanish
Isaiah11:14 They will swoop down on the slopes of Philistia to the west; together they will plunder the people to the east. They will lay hands on Edom and Moab
Isaiah11:15 The LORD will dry up the gulf of the Egyptian sea; with a scorching wind he will sweep his hand over the Euphrates River. He will break it up into seven streams so that men can cross over in sandals.
Isaiah11:16 There will be a highway for the remnant of his people that is left from Assyria

Isaiah12:2 Surely God is my salvation; I will trust and not be afraid. The LORD
Isaiah12:3 With joy you will draw water from the wells of salvation.

Isaiah13:1 An oracle concerning Babylon that Isaiah son of Amoz saw:
Isaiah13:2 Raise a banner on a bare hilltop
Isaiah13:3 I have commanded my holy ones; I have summoned my warriors to carry out my wrath- those who rejoice in my triumph.
Isaiah13:4 Listen
Isaiah13:5 They come from faraway lands
Isaiah13:6 Wail
Isaiah13:7 Because of this
Isaiah13:8 Terror will seize them
Isaiah13:9 See
Isaiah13:10 The stars of heaven and their constellations will not show their light. The rising sun will be darkened and the moon will not give its light.
Isaiah13:11 I will punish the world for its evil
Isaiah13:12 I will make man scarcer than pure gold
Isaiah13:13 Therefore I will make the heavens tremble; and the earth will shake from its place at the wrath of the LORD Almighty
Isaiah13:14 Like a hunted gazelle
Isaiah13:15 Whoever is captured will be thrust through; all who are caught will fall by the sword.
Isaiah13:16 Their infants will be dashed to pieces before their eyes; their houses will be looted and their wives ravished.
Isaiah13:17 See
Isaiah13:18 Their bows will strike down the young men; they will have no mercy on infants nor will they look with compassion on children.
Isaiah13:19 Babylon
Isaiah13:20 She will never be inhabited or lived in through all generations; no Arab will pitch his tent there
Isaiah13:21 But desert creatures will lie there
Isaiah13:22 Hyenas will howl in her strongholds
Isaiah14:1 The LORD will have compassion on Jacob; once again he will choose Israel and will settle them in their own land. Aliens will join them and unite with the house of Jacob.
Isaiah14:2 Nations will take them and bring them to their own place. And the house of Israel will possess the nations as menservants and maidservants in the LORD's land. They will make captives of their captors and rule over their oppressors.
Isaiah14:3 On the day the LORD gives you relief from suffering and turmoil and cruel bondage
Isaiah14:4 you will take up this taunt against the king of Babylon: How the oppressor has come to an end! How his fury has ended!
Isaiah14:5 The LORD has broken the rod of the wicked
Isaiah14:6 which in anger struck down peoples with unceasing blows
Isaiah14:7 All the lands are at rest and at peace; they break into singing.
Isaiah14:8 Even the pine trees and the cedars of Lebanon exult over you and say
Isaiah14:9 The grave below is all astir to meet you at your coming; it rouses the spirits of the departed to greet you- all those who were leaders in the world; it makes them rise from their thrones- all those who were kings over the nations.
Isaiah14:10 They will all respond
Isaiah14:11 All your pomp has been brought down to the grave
Isaiah14:12 How you have fallen from heaven
Isaiah14:13 You said in your heart
Isaiah14:14 I will ascend above the tops of the clouds; I will make myself like the Most High.
Isaiah14:15 But you are brought down to the grave
Isaiah14:16 Those who see you stare at you
Isaiah14:17 the man who made the world a desert
Isaiah14:18 All the kings of the nations lie in state
Isaiah14:19 But you are cast out of your tomb like a rejected branch; you are covered with the slain
Isaiah14:20 you will not join them in burial
Isaiah14:21 Prepare a place to slaughter his sons for the sins of their forefathers; they are not to rise to inherit the land and cover the earth with their cities.
Isaiah14:22 I will rise up against them
Isaiah14:23 I will turn her into a place for owls and into swampland; I will sweep her with the broom of destruction
Isaiah14:24 The LORD Almighty has sworn
Isaiah14:25 I will crush the Assyrian in my land; on my mountains I will trample him down. His yoke will be taken from my people
Isaiah14:26 This is the plan determined for the whole world; this is the hand stretched out over all nations.
Isaiah14:27 For the LORD Almighty has purposed
Isaiah14:28 This oracle came in the year King Ahaz died:
Isaiah14:29 Do not rejoice
Isaiah14:30 The poorest of the poor will find pasture
Isaiah14:31 Wail
Isaiah14:32 What answer shall be given to the envoys of that nation? The LORD has established Zion
Isaiah15:1 An oracle concerning Moab: Ar in Moab is ruined
Isaiah15:2 Dibon goes up to its temple
Isaiah15:3 In the streets they wear sackcloth; on the roofs and in the public squares they all wail
Isaiah15:4 Heshbon and Elealeh cry out
Isaiah15:5 My heart cries out over Moab; her fugitives flee as far as Zoar
Isaiah15:6 The waters of Nimrim are dried up and the grass is withered; the vegetation is gone and nothing green is left.
Isaiah15:7 So the wealth they have acquired and stored up they carry away over the Ravine of the Poplars.
Isaiah15:8 Their outcry echoes along the border of Moab; their wailing reaches as far as Eglaim

Isaiah16:2 Like fluttering birds pushed from the nest
Isaiah16:3 Give us counsel
Isaiah16:4 Let the Moabite fugitives stay with you; be their shelter from the destroyer. The oppressor will come to an end
Isaiah16:5 In love a throne will be established; in faithfulness a man will sit on it- one from the house of David- one who in judging seeks justice and speeds the cause of righteousness.
Isaiah16:6 We have heard of Moab's pride- her overweening pride and conceit
Isaiah16:7 Therefore the Moabites wail
Isaiah16:8 The fields of Heshbon wither
Isaiah16:9 So I weep
Isaiah16:10 Joy and gladness are taken away from the orchards; no one sings or shouts in the vineyards; no one treads out wine at the presses
Isaiah16:11 My heart laments for Moab like a harp
Isaiah16:12 When Moab appears at her high place
Isaiah16:13 This is the word the LORD has already spoken concerning Moab.
Isaiah16:14 But now the LORD says: Within three years

Isaiah19:1 An oracle concerning Egypt: See
Isaiah19:2 I will stir up Egyptian against Egyptian- brother will fight against brother
Isaiah19:3 The Egyptians will lose heart
Isaiah19:4 I will hand the Egyptians over to the power of a cruel master
Isaiah19:5 The waters of the river will dry up
Isaiah19:6 The canals will stink; the streams of Egypt will dwindle and dry up. The reeds and rushes will wither
Isaiah19:7 also the plants along the Nile
Isaiah19:8 The fishermen will groan and lament
Isaiah19:9 Those who work with combed flax will despair
Isaiah19:10 The workers in cloth will be dejected
Isaiah19:11 The officials of Zoan are nothing but fools; the wise counselors of Pharaoh give senseless advice. How can you say to Pharaoh
Isaiah19:12 Where are your wise men now? Let them show you and make known what the LORD Almighty has planned against Egypt.
Isaiah19:13 The officials of Zoan have become fools
Isaiah19:14 The LORD has poured into them a spirit of dizziness; they make Egypt stagger in all that she does
Isaiah19:15 There is nothing Egypt can do- head or tail
Isaiah19:16 In that day the Egyptians will be like women. They will shudder with fear at the uplifted hand that the LORD Almighty raises against them.
Isaiah19:17 And the land of Judah will bring terror to the Egyptians; everyone to whom Judah is mentioned will be terrified
Isaiah19:18 In that day five cities in Egypt will speak the language of Canaan and swear allegiance to the LORD Almighty. One of them will be called the City of Destruction.
Isaiah19:19 In that day there will be an altar to the LORD in the heart of Egypt
Isaiah19:20 It will be a sign and witness to the LORD Almighty in the land of Egypt. When they cry out to the LORD because of their oppressors
Isaiah19:21 So the LORD will make himself known to the Egyptians
Isaiah19:22 The LORD will strike Egypt with a plague; he will strike them and heal them. They will turn to the LORD
Isaiah19:23 In that day there will be a highway from Egypt to Assyria. The Assyrians will go to Egypt and the Egyptians to Assyria. The Egyptians and Assyrians will worship together.
Isaiah19:24 In that day Israel will be the third
Isaiah19:25 The LORD Almighty will bless them

Isaiah24:1 See
Isaiah24:2 it will be the same for priest as for people
Isaiah24:3 The earth will be completely laid waste and totally plundered. The LORD has spoken this word.
Isaiah24:4 The earth dries up and withers
Isaiah24:5 The earth is defiled by its people; they have disobeyed the laws
Isaiah24:6 Therefore a curse consumes the earth; its people must bear their guilt. Therefore earth's inhabitants are burned up
Isaiah24:7 The new wine dries up and the vine withers; all the merrymakers groan.
Isaiah24:8 The gaiety of the tambourines is stilled
Isaiah24:9 No longer do they drink wine with a song; the beer is bitter to its drinkers.
Isaiah24:10 The ruined city lies desolate; the entrance to every house is barred.
Isaiah24:11 In the streets they cry out for wine; all joy turns to gloom
Isaiah24:12 The city is left in ruins
Isaiah24:13 So will it be on the earth and among the nations
Isaiah24:14 They raise their voices
Isaiah24:15 Therefore in the east give glory to the LORD; exalt the name of the LORD
Isaiah24:16 From the ends of the earth we hear singing: Glory to the Righteous One. But I said
Isaiah24:17 Terror and pit and snare await you
Isaiah24:18 Whoever flees at the sound of terror will fall into a pit; whoever climbs out of the pit will be caught in a snare. The floodgates of the heavens are opened
Isaiah24:19 The earth is broken up
Isaiah24:20 The earth reels like a drunkard
Isaiah24:21 In that day the LORD will punish the powers in the heavens above and the kings on the earth below.
Isaiah24:22 They will be herded together like prisoners bound in a dungeon; they will be shut up in prison and be punished after many days.
Isaiah24:23 The moon will be abashed
Isaiah25:1 O LORD
Isaiah25:2 You have made the city a heap of rubble
Isaiah25:3 Therefore strong peoples will honor you; cities of ruthless nations will revere you.
Isaiah25:4 You have been a refuge for the poor
Isaiah25:5 and like the heat of the desert. You silence the uproar of foreigners; as heat is reduced by the shadow of a cloud
Isaiah25:6 On this mountain the LORD Almighty will prepare a feast of rich food for all peoples
Isaiah25:7 On this mountain he will destroy the shroud that enfolds all peoples
Isaiah25:8 he will swallow up death forever. The Sovereign LORD will wipe away the tears from all faces; he will remove the disgrace of his people from all the earth. The LORD has spoken.

Isaiah44:11 He and his kind will be put to shame; craftsmen are nothing but men. Let them all come together and take their stand; they will be brought down to terror and infamy.
Isaiah44:12 The blacksmith takes a tool and works with it in the coals; he shapes an idol with hammers
Isaiah44:13 The carpenter measures with a line and makes an outline with a marker; he roughs it out with chisels and marks it with compasses. He shapes it in the form of man
Isaiah44:14 He cut down cedars
Isaiah44:15 It is man's fuel for burning; some of it he takes and warms himself
Isaiah44:16 Half of the wood he burns in the fire; over it he prepares his meal
Isaiah44:17 From the rest he makes a god

Jeremiah1:1 The words of Jeremiah son of Hilkiah
Jeremiah1:2 The word of the LORD came to him in the thirteenth year of the reign of Josiah son of Amon king of Judah
Jeremiah1:3 and through the reign of Jehoiakim son of Josiah king of Judah
Jeremiah1:4 The word of the LORD came to me
Jeremiah1:5 Before I formed you in the womb I knew you
Jeremiah1:6 Ah
Jeremiah1:7 But the LORD said to me
Jeremiah1:8 Do not be afraid of them
Jeremiah1:9 Then the LORD reached out his hand and touched my mouth and said to me
Jeremiah1:10 See

Jeremiah14:2 Judah mourns
Jeremiah14:3 The nobles send their servants for water; they go to the cisterns but find no water. They return with their jars unfilled; dismayed and despairing
Jeremiah14:4 The ground is cracked because there is no rain in the land; the farmers are dismayed and cover their heads.

Jeremiah15:14 I will enslave you to your enemies in a land you do not know

Jeremiah15:17 I never sat in the company of revelers

Jeremiah15:20 I will make you a wall to this people
Jeremiah15:21 I will save you from the hands of the wicked and redeem you from the grasp of the cruel.
Jeremiah16:1 Then the word of the LORD came to me:
Jeremiah16:2 You must not marry and have sons or daughters in this place.
Jeremiah16:3 For this is what the LORD says about the sons and daughters born in this land and about the women who are their mothers and the men who are their fathers:
Jeremiah16:4 They will die of deadly diseases. They will not be mourned or buried but will be like refuse lying on the ground. They will perish by sword and famine
Jeremiah16:5 For this is what the LORD says: Do not enter a house where there is a funeral meal; do not go to mourn or show sympathy
Jeremiah16:6 Both high and low will die in this land. They will not be buried or mourned
Jeremiah16:7 No one will offer food to comfort those who mourn for the dead-not even for a father or a mother-nor will anyone give them a drink to console them.
Jeremiah16:8 And do not enter a house where there is feasting and sit down to eat and drink.
Jeremiah16:9 For this is what the LORD Almighty
Jeremiah16:10 When you tell these people all this and they ask you
Jeremiah16:11 then say to them
Jeremiah16:12 But you have behaved more wickedly than your fathers. See how each of you is following the stubbornness of his evil heart instead of obeying me.
Jeremiah16:13 So I will throw you out of this land into a land neither you nor your fathers have known
Jeremiah16:14 However
Jeremiah16:15 but they will say
Jeremiah16:16 But now I will send for many fishermen
Jeremiah16:17 My eyes are on all their ways; they are not hidden from me
Jeremiah16:18 I will repay them double for their wickedness and their sin
Jeremiah16:19 O LORD
Jeremiah16:20 Do men make their own gods? Yes
Jeremiah16:21 Therefore I will teach them- this time I will teach them my power and might. Then they will know that my name is the LORD.
Jeremiah17:1 Judah's sin is engraved with an iron tool
Jeremiah17:2 Even their children remember their altars and Asherah poles beside the spreading trees and on the high hills.
Jeremiah17:3 My mountain in the land and your wealth and all your treasures I will give away as plunder
Jeremiah17:4 Through your own fault you will lose the inheritance I gave you. I will enslave you to your enemies in a land you do not know
Jeremiah17:5 This is what the LORD says: Cursed is the one who trusts in man
Jeremiah17:6 He will be like a bush in the wastelands; he will not see prosperity when it comes. He will dwell in the parched places of the desert
Jeremiah17:7 But blessed is the man who trusts in the LORD
Jeremiah17:8 He will be like a tree planted by the water that sends out its roots by the stream. It does not fear when heat comes; its leaves are always green. It has no worries in a year of drought and never fails to bear fruit.
Jeremiah17:9 The heart is deceitful above all things and beyond cure. Who can understand it?
Jeremiah17:10 I the LORD search the heart and examine the mind
Jeremiah17:11 Like a partridge that hatches eggs it did not lay is the man who gains riches by unjust means. When his life is half gone
Jeremiah17:12 A glorious throne
Jeremiah17:13 O LORD
Jeremiah17:14 Heal me
Jeremiah17:15 They keep saying to me
Jeremiah17:16 I have not run away from being your shepherd; you know I have not desired the day of despair. What passes my lips is open before you.
Jeremiah17:17 Do not be a terror to me; you are my refuge in the day of disaster.
Jeremiah17:18 Let my persecutors be put to shame
Jeremiah17:19 This is what the LORD said to me: Go and stand at the gate of the people
Jeremiah17:20 Say to them
Jeremiah17:21 This is what the LORD says: Be careful not to carry a load on the Sabbath day or bring it through the gates of Jerusalem.
Jeremiah17:22 Do not bring a load out of your houses or do any work on the Sabbath
Jeremiah17:23 Yet they did not listen or pay attention; they were stiff-necked and would not listen or respond to discipline.
Jeremiah17:24 But if you are careful to obey me
Jeremiah17:25 then kings who sit on David's throne will come through the gates of this city with their officials. They and their officials will come riding in chariots and on horses
Jeremiah17:26 People will come from the towns of Judah and the villages around Jerusalem
Jeremiah17:27 But if you do not obey me to keep the Sabbath day holy by not carrying any load as you come through the gates of Jerusalem on the Sabbath day

Jeremiah18:8 and if that nation I warned repents of its evil
Jeremiah18:9 And if at another time I announce that a nation or kingdom is to be built up and planted

Jeremiah23:12 Therefore their path will become slippery; they will be banished to darkness and there they will fall. I will bring disaster on them in the year they are punished

Jeremiah23:14 And among the prophets of Jerusalem I have seen something horrible: They commit adultery and live a lie. They strengthen the hands of evildoers
Jeremiah23:15 Therefore

Jeremiah23:19 See
Jeremiah23:20 The anger of the LORD will not turn back until he fully accomplishes the purposes of his heart. In days to come you will understand it clearly.

Jeremiah23:22 But if they had stood in my council
Jeremiah23:23 Am I only a God nearby
Jeremiah23:24 Can anyone hide in secret places so that I cannot see him? declares the LORD. Do not I fill heaven and earth? declares the LORD.

Jeremiah30:10 'So do not fear

Jeremiah30:12 This is what the LORD says: 'Your wound is incurable
Jeremiah30:13 There is no one to plead your cause
Jeremiah30:14 All your allies have forgotten you; they care nothing for you. I have struck you as an enemy would and punished you as would the cruel
Jeremiah30:15 Why do you cry out over your wound
Jeremiah30:16 'But all who devour you will be devoured; all your enemies will go into exile. Those who plunder you will be plundered; all who make spoil of you I will despoil.
Jeremiah30:17 But I will restore you to health and heal your wounds
Jeremiah30:18 This is what the LORD says: 'I will restore the fortunes of Jacob's tents and have compassion on his dwellings; the city will be rebuilt on her ruins
Jeremiah30:19 From them will come songs of thanksgiving and the sound of rejoicing. I will add to their numbers
Jeremiah30:20 Their children will be as in days of old
Jeremiah30:21 Their leader will be one of their own; their ruler will arise from among them. I will bring him near and he will come close to me
Jeremiah30:22 'So you will be my people
Jeremiah30:23 See
Jeremiah30:24 The fierce anger of the LORD will not turn back until he fully accomplishes the purposes of his heart. In days to come you will understand this.
Jeremiah31:1 At that time
Jeremiah31:2 This is what the LORD says: The people who survive the sword will find favor in the desert; I will come to give rest to Israel.
Jeremiah31:3 The LORD appeared to us in the past
Jeremiah31:4 I will build you up again and you will be rebuilt
Jeremiah31:5 Again you will plant vineyards on the hills of Samaria; the farmers will plant them and enjoy their fruit.

Jeremiah31:11 For the LORD will ransom Jacob and redeem them from the hand of those stronger than they.
Jeremiah31:12 They will come and shout for joy on the heights of Zion; they will rejoice in the bounty of the LORD - the grain
Jeremiah31:13 Then maidens will dance and be glad
Jeremiah31:14 I will satisfy the priests with abundance

Jeremiah31:16 This is what the LORD says: Restrain your voice from weeping and your eyes from tears
Jeremiah31:17 So there is hope for your future

Jeremiah31:19 After I strayed
Jeremiah31:20 Is not Ephraim my dear son

Jeremiah31:22 How long will you wander
Jeremiah31:23 This is what the LORD Almighty
Jeremiah31:24 People will live together in Judah and all its towns-farmers and those who move about with their flocks.
Jeremiah31:25 I will refresh the weary and satisfy the faint.
Jeremiah31:26 At this I awoke and looked around. My sleep had been pleasant to me.

Jeremiah31:28 Just as I watched over them to uproot and tear down
Jeremiah31:29 In those days people will no longer say
Jeremiah31:30 Instead

Jeremiah31:34 No longer will a man teach his neighbor
Jeremiah31:35 This is what the LORD says

Jeremiah31:37 This is what the LORD says: Only if the heavens above can be measured and the foundations of the earth below be searched out will I reject all the descendants of Israel because of all they have done

Jeremiah31:39 The measuring line will stretch from there straight to the hill of Gareb and then turn to Goah.

Jeremiah32:1 This is the word that came to Jeremiah from the LORD in the tenth year of Zedekiah king of Judah
Jeremiah32:2 The army of the king of Babylon was then besieging Jerusalem

Jeremiah32:17 Ah

Jeremiah32:19 great are your purposes and mighty are your deeds. Your eyes are open to all the ways of men; you reward everyone according to his conduct and as his deeds deserve.
Jeremiah32:20 You performed miraculous signs and wonders in Egypt and have continued them to this day
Jeremiah32:21 You brought your people Israel out of Egypt with signs and wonders
Jeremiah32:22 You gave them this land you had sworn to give their forefathers
Jeremiah32:23 They came in and took possession of it
Jeremiah32:24 See how the siege ramps are built up to take the city. Because of the sword

Jeremiah32:27 I am the LORD
Jeremiah32:28 Therefore

Jeremiah32:32 The people of Israel and Judah have provoked me by all the evil they have done-they
Jeremiah32:33 They turned their backs to me and not their faces; though I taught them again and again
Jeremiah32:34 They set up their abominable idols in the house that bears my Name and defiled it.
Jeremiah32:35 They built high places for Baal in the Valley of Ben Hinnom to sacrifice their sons and daughters to Molech
Jeremiah32:36 You are saying about this city
Jeremiah32:37 I will surely gather them from all the lands where I banish them in my furious anger and great wrath; I will bring them back to this place and let them live in safety.
Jeremiah32:38 They will be my people
Jeremiah32:39 I will give them singleness of heart and action
Jeremiah32:40 I will make an everlasting covenant with them: I will never stop doing good to them
Jeremiah32:41 I will rejoice in doing them good and will assuredly plant them in this land with all my heart and soul.
Jeremiah32:42 This is what the LORD says: As I have brought all this great calamity on this people
Jeremiah32:43 Once more fields will be bought in this land of which you say
Jeremiah32:44 Fields will be bought for silver

Jeremiah34:9 Everyone was to free his Hebrew slaves
Jeremiah34:10 So all the officials and people who entered into this covenant agreed that they would free their male and female slaves and no longer hold them in bondage. They agreed
Jeremiah34:11 But afterward they changed their minds and took back the slaves they had freed and enslaved them again.
Jeremiah34:12 Then the word of the LORD came to Jeremiah:
Jeremiah34:13 This is what the LORD

Jeremiah34:15 Recently you repented and did what is right in my sight: Each of you proclaimed freedom to his countrymen. You even made a covenant before me in the house that bears my Name.
Jeremiah34:16 But now you have turned around and profaned my name; each of you has taken back the male and female slaves you had set free to go where they wished. You have forced them to become your slaves again.
Jeremiah34:17 Therefore
Jeremiah34:18 The men who have violated my covenant and have not fulfilled the terms of the covenant they made before me
Jeremiah34:19 The leaders of Judah and Jerusalem

Jeremiah43:1 When Jeremiah finished telling the people all the words of the LORD their God-everything the LORD had sent him to tell them-

Jeremiah43:12 He will set fire to the temples of the gods of Egypt; he will burn their temples and take their gods captive. As a shepherd wraps his garment around him
Jeremiah43:13 There in the temple of the sun in Egypt he will demolish the sacred pillars and will burn down the temples of the gods of Egypt.'
Jeremiah44:1 This word came to Jeremiah concerning all the Jews living in Lower Egypt-in Migdol
Jeremiah44:2 This is what the LORD Almighty
Jeremiah44:3 because of the evil they have done. They provoked me to anger by burning incense and by worshiping other gods that neither they nor you nor your fathers ever knew.
Jeremiah44:4 Again and again I sent my servants the prophets
Jeremiah44:5 But they did not listen or pay attention; they did not turn from their wickedness or stop burning incense to other gods.
Jeremiah44:6 Therefore
Jeremiah44:7 Now this is what the LORD God Almighty
Jeremiah44:8 Why provoke me to anger with what your hands have made
Jeremiah44:9 Have you forgotten the wickedness committed by your fathers and by the kings and queens of Judah and the wickedness committed by you and your wives in the land of Judah and the streets of Jerusalem?
Jeremiah44:10 To this day they have not humbled themselves or shown reverence
Jeremiah44:11 Therefore
Jeremiah44:12 I will take away the remnant of Judah who were determined to go to Egypt to settle there. They will all perish in Egypt; they will fall by the sword or die from famine. From the least to the greatest
Jeremiah44:13 I will punish those who live in Egypt with the sword
Jeremiah44:14 None of the remnant of Judah who have gone to live in Egypt will escape or survive to return to the land of Judah
Jeremiah44:15 Then all the men who knew that their wives were burning incense to other gods
Jeremiah44:16 We will not listen to the message you have spoken to us in the name of the LORD!
Jeremiah44:17 We will certainly do everything we said we would: We will burn incense to the Queen of Heaven and will pour out drink offerings to her just as we and our fathers
Jeremiah44:18 But ever since we stopped burning incense to the Queen of Heaven and pouring out drink offerings to her
Jeremiah44:19 The women added
Jeremiah44:20 Then Jeremiah said to all the people
Jeremiah44:21 Did not the LORD remember and think about the incense burned in the towns of Judah and the streets of Jerusalem by you and your fathers
Jeremiah44:22 When the LORD could no longer endure your wicked actions and the detestable things you did
Jeremiah44:23 Because you have burned incense and have sinned against the LORD and have not obeyed him or followed his law or his decrees or his stipulations
Jeremiah44:24 Then Jeremiah said to all the people
Jeremiah44:25 This is what the LORD Almighty
Jeremiah44:26 But hear the word of the LORD
Jeremiah44:27 For I am watching over them for harm
Jeremiah44:28 Those who escape the sword and return to the land of Judah from Egypt will be very few. Then the whole remnant of Judah who came to live in Egypt will know whose word will stand-mine or theirs.
Jeremiah44:29 'This will be the sign to you that I will punish you in this place
Jeremiah44:30 This is what the LORD says: 'I am going to hand Pharaoh Hophra king of Egypt over to his enemies who seek his life

Jeremiah48:1 Concerning Moab: This is what the LORD Almighty

Jeremiah48:22 to Dibon

Jeremiah48:24 to Kerioth and Bozrah- to all the towns of Moab

Jeremiah48:26 Make her drunk
Jeremiah48:27 Was not Israel the object of your ridicule? Was she caught among thieves
Jeremiah48:28 Abandon your towns and dwell among the rocks
Jeremiah48:29 We have heard of Moab's pride- her overweening pride and conceit
Jeremiah48:30 I know her insolence but it is futile
Jeremiah48:31 Therefore I wail over Moab
Jeremiah48:32 I weep for you
Jeremiah48:33 Joy and gladness are gone from the orchards and fields of Moab. I have stopped the flow of wine from the presses; no one treads them with shouts of joy. Although there are shouts
Jeremiah48:34 The sound of their cry rises from Heshbon to Elealeh and Jahaz
Jeremiah48:35 In Moab I will put an end to those who make offerings on the high places and burn incense to their gods
Jeremiah48:36 So my heart laments for Moab like a flute; it laments like a flute for the men of Kir Hareseth. The wealth they acquired is gone.
Jeremiah48:37 Every head is shaved and every beard cut off; every hand is slashed and every waist is covered with sackcloth.
Jeremiah48:38 On all the roofs in Moab and in the public squares there is nothing but mourning
Jeremiah48:39 How shattered she is! How they wail! How Moab turns her back in shame! Moab has become an object of ridicule
Jeremiah48:40 This is what the LORD says: Look! An eagle is swooping down
Jeremiah48:41 Kerioth will be captured and the strongholds taken. In that day the hearts of Moab's warriors will be like the heart of a woman in labor.
Jeremiah48:42 Moab will be destroyed as a nation because she defied the LORD.

Jeremiah48:45 In the shadow of Heshbon the fugitives stand helpless
Jeremiah48:46 Woe to you
Jeremiah48:47 Yet I will restore the fortunes of Moab in days to come

Jeremiah49:9 If grape pickers came to you

Jeremiah49:11 Leave your orphans; I will protect their lives. Your widows too can trust in me.

Jeremiah49:19 Like a lion coming up from Jordan's thickets to a rich pastureland

Jeremiah49:26 Surely

Jeremiah50:1 This is the word the LORD spoke through Jeremiah the prophet concerning Babylon and the land of the Babylonians:
Jeremiah50:2 Announce and proclaim among the nations
Jeremiah50:3 A nation from the north will attack her and lay waste her land. No one will live in it; both men and animals will flee away.
Jeremiah50:4 In those days
Jeremiah50:5 They will ask the way to Zion and turn their faces toward it. They will come and bind themselves to the LORD in an everlasting covenant that will not be forgotten.
Jeremiah50:6 My people have been lost sheep; their shepherds have led them astray and caused them to roam on the mountains. They wandered over mountain and hill and forgot their own resting place.
Jeremiah50:7 Whoever found them devoured them; their enemies said
Jeremiah50:8 Flee out of Babylon; leave the land of the Babylonians
Jeremiah50:9 For I will stir up and bring against Babylon an alliance of great nations from the land of the north. They will take up their positions against her
Jeremiah50:10 So Babylonia will be plundered; all who plunder her will have their fill
Jeremiah50:11 Because you rejoice and are glad
Jeremiah50:12 your mother will be greatly ashamed; she who gave you birth will be disgraced. She will be the least of the nations- a wilderness
Jeremiah50:13 Because of the LORD's anger she will not be inhabited but will be completely desolate. All who pass Babylon will be horrified and scoff because of all her wounds.
Jeremiah50:14 Take up your positions around Babylon
Jeremiah50:15 Shout against her on every side! She surrenders
Jeremiah50:16 Cut off from Babylon the sower
Jeremiah50:17 Israel is a scattered flock that lions have chased away. The first to devour him was the king of Assyria; the last to crush his bones was Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon.
Jeremiah50:18 Therefore this is what the LORD Almighty
Jeremiah50:19 But I will bring Israel back to his own pasture and he will graze on Carmel and Bashan; his appetite will be satisfied on the hills of Ephraim and Gilead.
Jeremiah50:20 In those days
Jeremiah50:21 Attack the land of Merathaim and those who live in Pekod. Pursue
Jeremiah50:22 The noise of battle is in the land
Jeremiah50:23 How broken and shattered is the hammer of the whole earth! How desolate is Babylon among the nations!
Jeremiah50:24 I set a trap for you
Jeremiah50:25 The LORD has opened his arsenal and brought out the weapons of his wrath
Jeremiah50:26 Come against her from afar. Break open her granaries; pile her up like heaps of grain. Completely destroy her and leave her no remnant.
Jeremiah50:27 Kill all her young bulls; let them go down to the slaughter! Woe to them! For their day has come
Jeremiah50:28 Listen to the fugitives and refugees from Babylon declaring in Zion how the LORD our God has taken vengeance
Jeremiah50:29 Summon archers against Babylon
Jeremiah50:30 Therefore
Jeremiah50:31 See
Jeremiah50:32 The arrogant one will stumble and fall and no one will help her up; I will kindle a fire in her towns that will consume all who are around her.
Jeremiah50:33 This is what the LORD Almighty says: The people of Israel are oppressed
Jeremiah50:34 Yet their Redeemer is strong; the LORD Almighty is his name. He will vigorously defend their cause so that he may bring rest to their land
Jeremiah50:35 A sword against the Babylonians! declares the LORD - against those who live in Babylon and against her officials and wise men!
Jeremiah50:36 A sword against her false prophets! They will become fools. A sword against her warriors! They will be filled with terror.
Jeremiah50:37 A sword against her horses and chariots and all the foreigners in her ranks! They will become women. A sword against her treasures! They will be plundered.
Jeremiah50:38 A drought on her waters! They will dry up. For it is a land of idols
Jeremiah50:39 So desert creatures and hyenas will live there
Jeremiah50:40 As God overthrew Sodom and Gomorrah along with their neighboring towns
Jeremiah50:41 Look! An army is coming from the north; a great nation and many kings are being stirred up from the ends of the earth.
Jeremiah50:42 They are armed with bows and spears; they are cruel and without mercy. They sound like the roaring sea as they ride on their horses; they come like men in battle formation to attack you
Jeremiah50:43 The king of Babylon has heard reports about them
Jeremiah50:44 Like a lion coming up from Jordan's thickets to a rich pastureland
Jeremiah50:45 Therefore
Jeremiah50:46 At the sound of Babylon's capture the earth will tremble; its cry will resound among the nations.

Jeremiah51:5 For Israel and Judah have not been forsaken by their God

Jeremiah51:8 Babylon will suddenly fall and be broken. Wail over her! Get balm for her pain; perhaps she can be healed.

Jeremiah51:16 When he thunders

Jeremiah51:36 Therefore
Jeremiah51:37 Babylon will be a heap of ruins
Jeremiah51:38 Her people all roar like young lions
Jeremiah51:39 But while they are aroused
Jeremiah51:40 I will bring them down like lambs to the slaughter
Jeremiah51:41 How Sheshach will be captured
Jeremiah51:42 The sea will rise over Babylon; its roaring waves will cover her.
Jeremiah51:43 Her towns will be desolate
Jeremiah51:44 I will punish Bel in Babylon and make him spew out what he has swallowed. The nations will no longer stream to him. And the wall of Babylon will fall.
Jeremiah51:45 Come out of her
Jeremiah51:46 Do not lose heart or be afraid when rumors are heard in the land; one rumor comes this year
Jeremiah51:47 For the time will surely come when I will punish the idols of Babylon; her whole land will be disgraced and her slain will all lie fallen within her.
Jeremiah51:48 Then heaven and earth and all that is in them will shout for joy over Babylon
Jeremiah51:49 Babylon must fall because of Israel's slain
Jeremiah51:50 You who have escaped the sword
Jeremiah51:51 We are disgraced

Jeremiah51:54 The sound of a cry comes from Babylon
