Obadiah1:3 The pride of your heart has deceived you
Obadiah1:4 Though you soar like the eagle and make your nest among the stars
Obadiah1:5 If thieves came to you
Obadiah1:6 But how Esau will be ransacked
Obadiah1:7 All your allies will force you to the border; your friends will deceive and overpower you; those who eat your bread will set a trap for you
Obadiah1:8 In that day
Obadiah1:9 Your warriors
Obadiah1:10 Because of the violence against your brother Jacob
Obadiah1:11 On the day you stood aloof while strangers carried off his wealth and foreigners entered his gates and cast lots for Jerusalem
Obadiah1:12 You should not look down on your brother in the day of his misfortune
Obadiah1:13 You should not march through the gates of my people in the day of their disaster
Obadiah1:14 You should not wait at the crossroads to cut down their fugitives
Obadiah1:15 The day of the LORD is near for all nations. As you have done
Obadiah1:16 Just as you drank on my holy hill
Obadiah1:17 But on Mount Zion will be deliverance; it will be holy
Obadiah1:18 The house of Jacob will be a fire and the house of Joseph a flame; the house of Esau will be stubble
Obadiah1:19 People from the Negev will occupy the mountains of Esau
Obadiah1:20 This company of Israelite exiles who are in Canaan will possess the land as far as Zarephath; the exiles from Jerusalem who are in Sepharad will possess the towns of the Negev.
Obadiah1:21 Deliverers will go up on Mount Zion to govern the mountains of Esau. And the kingdom will be the LORD's.
Jonah1:1 The word of the LORD came to Jonah son of Amittai:
Jonah1:2 Go to the great city of Nineveh and preach against it
Jonah1:3 But Jonah ran away from the LORD and headed for Tarshish. He went down to Joppa
Jonah1:4 Then the LORD sent a great wind on the sea
Jonah1:5 All the sailors were afraid and each cried out to his own god. And they threw the cargo into the sea to lighten the ship. But Jonah had gone below deck
Jonah1:6 The captain went to him and said
Jonah1:7 Then the sailors said to each other
Jonah1:8 So they asked him
Jonah1:9 He answered
Jonah1:10 This terrified them and they asked
Jonah1:11 The sea was getting rougher and rougher. So they asked him
Jonah1:12 Pick me up and throw me into the sea
Jonah1:13 Instead
Jonah1:14 Then they cried to the LORD
Jonah1:15 Then they took Jonah and threw him overboard
Jonah1:16 At this the men greatly feared the LORD
Jonah1:17 But the LORD provided a great fish to swallow Jonah
Jonah2:1 From inside the fish Jonah prayed to the LORD his God.
Jonah2:2 He said: In my distress I called to the LORD
Jonah2:3 You hurled me into the deep
Jonah2:4 I said
Jonah2:5 The engulfing waters threatened me
Jonah2:6 To the roots of the mountains I sank down; the earth beneath barred me in forever. But you brought my life up from the pit
Jonah2:7 When my life was ebbing away
Jonah2:8 Those who cling to worthless idols forfeit the grace that could be theirs.
Jonah2:9 But I
Jonah2:10 And the LORD commanded the fish
Jonah3:1 Then the word of the LORD came to Jonah a second time:
Jonah3:2 Go to the great city of Nineveh and proclaim to it the message I give you.
Jonah3:3 Jonah obeyed the word of the LORD and went to Nineveh. Now Nineveh was a very important city-a visit required three days.
Jonah3:4 On the first day
Jonah3:5 The Ninevites believed God. They declared a fast
Jonah3:6 When the news reached the king of Nineveh
Jonah3:7 Then he issued a proclamation in Nineveh: By the decree of the king and his nobles: Do not let any man or beast
Jonah3:8 But let man and beast be covered with sackcloth. Let everyone call urgently on God. Let them give up their evil ways and their violence.
Jonah3:9 Who knows? God may yet relent and with compassion turn from his fierce anger so that we will not perish.
Jonah3:10 When God saw what they did and how they turned from their evil ways
Jonah4:1 But Jonah was greatly displeased and became angry.
Jonah4:2 He prayed to the LORD
Jonah4:3 Now
Jonah4:4 But the LORD replied
Jonah4:5 Jonah went out and sat down at a place east of the city. There he made himself a shelter
Jonah4:6 Then the LORD God provided a vine and made it grow up over Jonah to give shade for his head to ease his discomfort
Jonah4:7 But at dawn the next day God provided a worm
Jonah4:8 When the sun rose
Jonah4:9 But God said to Jonah
Jonah4:10 But the LORD said
Jonah4:11 But Nineveh has more than a hundred and twenty thousand people who cannot tell their right hand from their left
Micah1:1 The word of the LORD that came to Micah of Moresheth during the reigns of Jotham
Micah1:2 Hear
Micah1:3 Look! The LORD is coming from his dwelling place; he comes down and treads the high places of the earth.
Micah1:4 The mountains melt beneath him and the valleys split apart
Micah1:5 All this is because of Jacob's transgression
Micah1:6 Therefore I will make Samaria a heap of rubble
Micah1:7 All her idols will be broken to pieces; all her temple gifts will be burned with fire; I will destroy all her images. Since she gathered her gifts from the wages of prostitutes
Micah1:8 Because of this I will weep and wail; I will go about barefoot and naked. I will howl like a jackal and moan like an owl.
Micah1:9 For her wound is incurable; it has come to Judah. It has reached the very gate of my people
Micah1:10 Tell it not in Gath; weep not at all. In Beth Ophrah roll in the dust.
Micah1:11 Pass on in nakedness and shame
Micah1:12 Those who live in Maroth writhe in pain
Micah1:13 You who live in Lachish
Micah1:14 Therefore you will give parting gifts to Moresheth Gath. The town of Aczib will prove deceptive to the kings of Israel.
Micah1:15 I will bring a conqueror against you who live in Mareshah. He who is the glory of Israel will come to Adullam.
Micah1:16 Shave your heads in mourning for the children in whom you delight; make yourselves as bald as the vulture
Micah2:1 Woe to those who plan iniquity
Micah2:2 They covet fields and seize them
Micah2:3 Therefore
Micah2:4 In that day men will ridicule you; they will taunt you with this mournful song: 'We are utterly ruined; my people's possession is divided up. He takes it from me! He assigns our fields to traitors.'
Micah2:5 Therefore you will have no one in the assembly of the LORD to divide the land by lot.
Micah2:6 Do not prophesy
Micah2:7 Should it be said
Micah2:8 Lately my people have risen up like an enemy. You strip off the rich robe from those who pass by without a care
Micah2:9 You drive the women of my people from their pleasant homes. You take away my blessing from their children forever.
Micah2:10 Get up
Micah2:11 If a liar and deceiver comes and says
Micah2:12 I will surely gather all of you
Micah2:13 One who breaks open the way will go up before them; they will break through the gate and go out. Their king will pass through before them

Micah3:6 Therefore night will come over you
Micah3:7 The seers will be ashamed and the diviners disgraced. They will all cover their faces because there is no answer from God.
Micah3:8 But as for me
Micah3:9 Hear this
Micah3:10 who build Zion with bloodshed
Micah3:11 Her leaders judge for a bribe

Micah4:2 Many nations will come and say
Micah4:3 He will judge between many peoples and will settle disputes for strong nations far and wide. They will beat their swords into plowshares and their spears into pruning hooks. Nation will not take up sword against nation
Micah4:4 Every man will sit under his own vine and under his own fig tree
Micah4:5 All the nations may walk in the name of their gods; we will walk in the name of the LORD our God for ever and ever. The LORD 's Plan

Micah4:7 I will make the lame a remnant

Micah4:11 But now many nations are gathered against you. They say
Micah4:12 But they do not know the thoughts of the LORD; they do not understand his plan

Micah5:2 But you

Micah5:5 And he will be their peace. When the Assyrian invades our land and marches through our fortresses

Micah6:2 Hear
Micah6:3 My people

Micah6:10 Am I still to forget
Micah6:11 Shall I acquit a man with dishonest scales
Micah6:12 Her rich men are violent; her people are liars and their tongues speak deceitfully.
Micah6:13 Therefore
Micah6:14 You will eat but not be satisfied; your stomach will still be empty. You will store up but save nothing
Micah6:15 You will plant but not harvest; you will press olives but not use the oil on yourselves
Micah6:16 You have observed the statutes of Omri and all the practices of Ahab's house
Micah7:1 What misery is mine! I am like one who gathers summer fruit at the gleaning of the vineyard; there is no cluster of grapes to eat
Micah7:2 The godly have been swept from the land; not one upright man remains. All men lie in wait to shed blood; each hunts his brother with a net.
Micah7:3 Both hands are skilled in doing evil; the ruler demands gifts
Micah7:4 The best of them is like a brier
Micah7:5 Do not trust a neighbor; put no confidence in a friend. Even with her who lies in your embrace be careful of your words.
Micah7:6 For a son dishonors his father
Micah7:7 But as for me
Micah7:8 Do not gloat over me
Micah7:9 Because I have sinned against him
Micah7:10 Then my enemy will see it and will be covered with shame

Micah7:12 In that day people will come to you from Assyria and the cities of Egypt
Micah7:13 The earth will become desolate because of its inhabitants

Micah7:15 As in the days when you came out of Egypt
Micah7:16 Nations will see and be ashamed
Micah7:17 They will lick dust like a snake
Micah7:18 Who is a God like you
Micah7:19 You will again have compassion on us; you will tread our sins underfoot and hurl all our iniquities into the depths of the sea.
Micah7:20 You will be true to Jacob
Nahum1:1 An oracle concerning Nineveh. The book of the vision of Nahum the Elkoshite.
Nahum1:2 The LORD is a jealous and avenging God; the LORD takes vengeance and is filled with wrath. The LORD takes vengeance on his foes and maintains his wrath against his enemies.
Nahum1:3 The LORD is slow to anger and great in power; the LORD will not leave the guilty unpunished. His way is in the whirlwind and the storm
Nahum1:4 He rebukes the sea and dries it up; he makes all the rivers run dry. Bashan and Carmel wither and the blossoms of Lebanon fade.
Nahum1:5 The mountains quake before him and the hills melt away. The earth trembles at his presence
Nahum1:6 Who can withstand his indignation? Who can endure his fierce anger? His wrath is poured out like fire; the rocks are shattered before him.
Nahum1:7 The LORD is good
Nahum1:8 but with an overwhelming flood he will make an end of Nineveh; he will pursue his foes into darkness.
Nahum1:9 Whatever they plot against the LORD he will bring to an end; trouble will not come a second time.
Nahum1:10 They will be entangled among thorns and drunk from their wine; they will be consumed like dry stubble.
Nahum1:11 From you
Nahum1:12 This is what the LORD says: Although they have allies and are numerous
Nahum1:13 Now I will break their yoke from your neck and tear your shackles away.
Nahum1:14 The LORD has given a command concerning you
Nahum1:15 Look
Nahum2:1 An attacker advances against you
Nahum2:2 The LORD will restore the splendor of Jacob like the splendor of Israel
Nahum2:3 The shields of his soldiers are red; the warriors are clad in scarlet. The metal on the chariots flashes on the day they are made ready; the spears of pine are brandished.
Nahum2:4 The chariots storm through the streets
Nahum2:5 He summons his picked troops
Nahum2:6 The river gates are thrown open and the palace collapses.
Nahum2:7 It is decreed that the city be exiled and carried away. Its slave girls moan like doves and beat upon their breasts.
Nahum2:8 Nineveh is like a pool
Nahum2:9 Plunder the silver! Plunder the gold! The supply is endless
Nahum2:10 She is pillaged
Nahum2:11 Where now is the lions' den
Nahum2:12 The lion killed enough for his cubs and strangled the prey for his mate
Nahum2:13 I am against you
Nahum3:1 Woe to the city of blood
Nahum3:2 The crack of whips
Nahum3:3 Charging cavalry
Nahum3:4 all because of the wanton lust of a harlot
Nahum3:5 I am against you
Nahum3:6 I will pelt you with filth
Nahum3:7 All who see you will flee from you and say
Nahum3:8 Are you better than Thebes
Nahum3:9 Cush and Egypt were her boundless strength; Put and Libya were among her allies.
Nahum3:10 Yet she was taken captive and went into exile. Her infants were dashed to pieces at the head of every street. Lots were cast for her nobles
Nahum3:11 You too will become drunk; you will go into hiding and seek refuge from the enemy.
Nahum3:12 All your fortresses are like fig trees with their first ripe fruit; when they are shaken
Nahum3:13 Look at your troops- they are all women! The gates of your land are wide open to your enemies; fire has consumed their bars.
Nahum3:14 Draw water for the siege
Nahum3:15 There the fire will devour you; the sword will cut you down and
Nahum3:16 You have increased the number of your merchants till they are more than the stars of the sky
Nahum3:17 Your guards are like locusts
Nahum3:18 O king of Assyria
Nahum3:19 Nothing can heal your wound; your injury is fatal. Everyone who hears the news about you claps his hands at your fall
Habakkuk1:1 The oracle that Habakkuk the prophet received.
Habakkuk1:2 How long
Habakkuk1:3 Why do you make me look at injustice? Why do you tolerate wrong? Destruction and violence are before me; there is strife
Habakkuk1:4 Therefore the law is paralyzed
Habakkuk1:5 Look at the nations and watch- and be utterly amazed. For I am going to do something in your days that you would not believe
Habakkuk1:6 I am raising up the Babylonians
Habakkuk1:7 They are a feared and dreaded people; they are a law to themselves and promote their own honor.
Habakkuk1:8 Their horses are swifter than leopards
Habakkuk1:9 they all come bent on violence. Their hordes advance like a desert wind and gather prisoners like sand.
Habakkuk1:10 They deride kings and scoff at rulers. They laugh at all fortified cities; they build earthen ramps and capture them.
Habakkuk1:11 Then they sweep past like the wind and go on- guilty men
Habakkuk1:12 O LORD
Habakkuk1:13 Your eyes are too pure to look on evil; you cannot tolerate wrong. Why then do you tolerate the treacherous? Why are you silent while the wicked swallow up those more righteous than themselves?
Habakkuk1:14 You have made men like fish in the sea
Habakkuk1:15 The wicked foe pulls all of them up with hooks
Habakkuk1:16 Therefore he sacrifices to his net and burns incense to his dragnet
Habakkuk1:17 Is he to keep on emptying his net
Habakkuk2:1 I will stand at my watch and station myself on the ramparts; I will look to see what he will say to me
Habakkuk2:2 Then the LORD replied: Write down the revelation and make it plain on tablets so that a herald may run with it.
Habakkuk2:3 For the revelation awaits an appointed time; it speaks of the end and will not prove false. Though it linger
Habakkuk2:4 See
Habakkuk2:5 indeed
Habakkuk2:6 Will not all of them taunt him with ridicule and scorn
Habakkuk2:7 Will not your debtors suddenly arise? Will they not wake up and make you tremble? Then you will become their victim.
Habakkuk2:8 Because you have plundered many nations
Habakkuk2:9 Woe to him who builds his realm by unjust gain to set his nest on high
Habakkuk2:10 You have plotted the ruin of many peoples
Habakkuk2:11 The stones of the wall will cry out
Habakkuk2:12 Woe to him who builds a city with bloodshed and establishes a town by crime!
Habakkuk2:13 Has not the LORD Almighty determined that the people's labor is only fuel for the fire
Habakkuk2:14 For the earth will be filled with the knowledge of the glory of the LORD
Habakkuk2:15 Woe to him who gives drink to his neighbors
Habakkuk2:16 You will be filled with shame instead of glory. Now it is your turn! Drink and be exposed! The cup from the LORD's right hand is coming around to you
Habakkuk2:17 The violence you have done to Lebanon will overwhelm you
Habakkuk2:18 Of what value is an idol
Habakkuk2:19 Woe to him who says to wood
Habakkuk2:20 But the LORD is in his holy temple; let all the earth be silent before him.

Habakkuk3:3 God came from Teman
Habakkuk3:4 His splendor was like the sunrise; rays flashed from his hand
Habakkuk3:5 Plague went before him; pestilence followed his steps.
Habakkuk3:6 He stood
Habakkuk3:7 I saw the tents of Cushan in distress
Habakkuk3:8 Were you angry with the rivers
Habakkuk3:9 You uncovered your bow
Habakkuk3:10 the mountains saw you and writhed. Torrents of water swept by; the deep roared and lifted its waves on high.
Habakkuk3:11 Sun and moon stood still in the heavens at the glint of your flying arrows
Habakkuk3:12 In wrath you strode through the earth and in anger you threshed the nations.
Habakkuk3:13 You came out to deliver your people
Habakkuk3:14 With his own spear you pierced his head when his warriors stormed out to scatter us
Habakkuk3:15 You trampled the sea with your horses
Habakkuk3:16 I heard and my heart pounded
Habakkuk3:17 Though the fig tree does not bud and there are no grapes on the vines
Habakkuk3:18 yet I will rejoice in the LORD
Habakkuk3:19 The Sovereign LORD is my strength; he makes my feet like the feet of a deer

Haggai2:8 'The silver is mine and the gold is mine

Matthew1:1 A record of the genealogy of Jesus Christ the son of David
Matthew1:2 Abraham was the father of Isaac
Matthew1:3 Judah the father of Perez and Zerah
Matthew1:4 Ram the father of Amminadab
Matthew1:5 Salmon the father of Boaz
Matthew1:6 and Jesse the father of King David. David was the father of Solomon
Matthew1:7 Solomon the father of Rehoboam
Matthew1:8 Asa the father of Jehoshaphat
Matthew1:9 Uzziah the father of Jotham
Matthew1:10 Hezekiah the father of Manasseh
Matthew1:11 and Josiah the father of Jeconiah and his brothers at the time of the exile to Babylon.
Matthew1:12 After the exile to Babylon: Jeconiah was the father of Shealtiel
Matthew1:13 Zerubbabel the father of Abiud
Matthew1:14 Azor the father of Zadok
Matthew1:15 Eliud the father of Eleazar
Matthew1:16 and Jacob the father of Joseph
Matthew1:17 Thus there were fourteen generations in all from Abraham to David
Matthew1:18 This is how the birth of Jesus Christ came about: His mother Mary was pledged to be married to Joseph
Matthew1:19 Because Joseph her husband was a righteous man and did not want to expose her to public disgrace
Matthew1:20 But after he had considered this
Matthew1:21 She will give birth to a son
Matthew1:22 All this took place to fulfill what the Lord had said through the prophet:
Matthew1:23 The virgin will be with child and will give birth to a son
Matthew1:24 When Joseph woke up
Matthew1:25 But he had no union with her until she gave birth to a son. And he gave him the name Jesus.

Matthew6:27 Who of you by worrying can add a single hour to his life?
Matthew6:28 And why do you worry about clothes? See how the lilies of the field grow. They do not labor or spin.
Matthew6:29 Yet I tell you that not even Solomon in all his splendor was dressed like one of these.
Matthew6:30 If that is how God clothes the grass of the field
Matthew6:31 So do not worry
Matthew6:32 For the pagans run after all these things
Matthew6:33 But seek first his kingdom and his righteousness
Matthew6:34 Therefore do not worry about tomorrow
Matthew7:1 Do not judge
Matthew7:2 For in the same way you judge others
Matthew7:3 Why do you look at the speck of sawdust in your brother's eye and pay no attention to the plank in your own eye?
Matthew7:4 How can you say to your brother
Matthew7:5 You hypocrite
Matthew7:6 Do not give dogs what is sacred; do not throw your pearls to pigs. If you do
Matthew7:7 Ask and it will be given to you; seek and you will find; knock and the door will be opened to you.
Matthew7:8 For everyone who asks receives; he who seeks finds; and to him who knocks
Matthew7:9 Which of you
Matthew7:10 Or if he asks for a fish
Matthew7:11 If you
Matthew7:12 So in everything
Matthew7:13 Enter through the narrow gate. For wide is the gate and broad is the road that leads to destruction
Matthew7:14 But small is the gate and narrow the road that leads to life
Matthew7:15 Watch out for false prophets. They come to you in sheep's clothing
Matthew7:16 By their fruit you will recognize them. Do people pick grapes from thornbushes
Matthew7:17 Likewise every good tree bears good fruit
Matthew7:18 A good tree cannot bear bad fruit
Matthew7:19 Every tree that does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.
Matthew7:20 Thus
Matthew7:21 Not everyone who says to me
Matthew7:22 Many will say to me on that day
Matthew7:23 Then I will tell them plainly
Matthew7:24 Therefore everyone who hears these words of mine and puts them into practice is like a wise man who built his house on the rock.
Matthew7:25 The rain came down
Matthew7:26 But everyone who hears these words of mine and does not put them into practice is like a foolish man who built his house on sand.
Matthew7:27 The rain came down
Matthew7:28 When Jesus had finished saying these things
Matthew7:29 because he taught as one who had authority

Matthew8:16 When evening came

Matthew9:18 While he was saying this
Matthew9:19 Jesus got up and went with him

Matthew10:1 He called his twelve disciples to him and gave them authority to drive out evil spirits and to heal every disease and sickness.
Matthew10:2 These are the names of the twelve apostles: first
Matthew10:3 Philip and Bartholomew; Thomas and Matthew the tax collector; James son of Alphaeus
Matthew10:4 Simon the Zealot and Judas Iscariot
Matthew10:5 These twelve Jesus sent out with the following instructions: Do not go among the Gentiles or enter any town of the Samaritans.
Matthew10:6 Go rather to the lost sheep of Israel.
Matthew10:7 As you go
Matthew10:8 Heal the sick
Matthew10:9 Do not take along any gold or silver or copper in your belts;
Matthew10:10 take no bag for the journey
Matthew10:11 Whatever town or village you enter
Matthew10:12 As you enter the home
Matthew10:13 If the home is deserving
Matthew10:14 If anyone will not welcome you or listen to your words
Matthew10:15 I tell you the truth
Matthew10:16 I am sending you out like sheep among wolves. Therefore be as shrewd as snakes and as innocent as doves.
Matthew10:17 Be on your guard against men; they will hand you over to the local councils and flog you in their synagogues.
Matthew10:18 On my account you will be brought before governors and kings as witnesses to them and to the Gentiles.
Matthew10:19 But when they arrest you
Matthew10:20 for it will not be you speaking
Matthew10:21 Brother will betray brother to death
Matthew10:22 All men will hate you because of me
Matthew10:23 When you are persecuted in one place
Matthew10:24 A student is not above his teacher
Matthew10:25 It is enough for the student to be like his teacher
Matthew10:26 So do not be afraid of them. There is nothing concealed that will not be disclosed
Matthew10:27 What I tell you in the dark
Matthew10:28 Do not be afraid of those who kill the body but cannot kill the soul. Rather
Matthew10:29 Are not two sparrows sold for a penny? Yet not one of them will fall to the ground apart from the will of your Father.
Matthew10:30 And even the very hairs of your head are all numbered.
Matthew10:31 So don't be afraid; you are worth more than many sparrows.
Matthew10:32 Whoever acknowledges me before men
Matthew10:33 But whoever disowns me before men
Matthew10:34 Do not suppose that I have come to bring peace to the earth. I did not come to bring peace
Matthew10:35 For I have come to turn 'a man against his father
Matthew10:36 a man's enemies will be the members of his own household.'
Matthew10:37 Anyone who loves his father or mother more than me is not worthy of me; anyone who loves his son or daughter more than me is not worthy of me;
Matthew10:38 and anyone who does not take his cross and follow me is not worthy of me.
Matthew10:39 Whoever finds his life will lose it
Matthew10:40 He who receives you receives me
Matthew10:41 Anyone who receives a prophet because he is a prophet will receive a prophet's reward
Matthew10:42 And if anyone gives even a cup of cold water to one of these little ones because he is my disciple

Matthew11:13 For all the Prophets and the Law prophesied until John.

Matthew12:3 He answered
Matthew12:4 He entered the house of God
Matthew12:5 Or haven't you read in the Law that on the Sabbath the priests in the temple desecrate the day and yet are innocent?
Matthew12:6 I tell you that one greater than the temple is here.
Matthew12:7 If you had known what these words mean
Matthew12:8 For the Son of Man is Lord of the Sabbath.
Matthew12:9 Going on from that place
Matthew12:10 and a man with a shriveled hand was there. Looking for a reason to accuse Jesus
Matthew12:11 He said to them
Matthew12:12 How much more valuable is a man than a sheep! Therefore it is lawful to do good on the Sabbath.
Matthew12:13 Then he said to the man
Matthew12:14 But the Pharisees went out and plotted how they might kill Jesus.
Matthew12:15 Aware of this
Matthew12:16 warning them not to tell who he was.
Matthew12:17 This was to fulfill what was spoken through the prophet Isaiah:
Matthew12:18 Here is my servant whom I have chosen
Matthew12:19 He will not quarrel or cry out; no one will hear his voice in the streets.
Matthew12:20 A bruised reed he will not break
Matthew12:21 In his name the nations will put their hope.
Matthew12:22 Then they brought him a demon-possessed man who was blind and mute
Matthew12:23 All the people were astonished and said
Matthew12:24 But when the Pharisees heard this
Matthew12:25 Jesus knew their thoughts and said to them
Matthew12:26 If Satan drives out Satan
Matthew12:27 And if I drive out demons by Beelzebub
Matthew12:28 But if I drive out demons by the Spirit of God
Matthew12:29 Or again
Matthew12:30 He who is not with me is against me
Matthew12:31 And so I tell you
Matthew12:32 Anyone who speaks a word against the Son of Man will be forgiven
Matthew12:33 Make a tree good and its fruit will be good
Matthew12:34 You brood of vipers
Matthew12:35 The good man brings good things out of the good stored up in him
Matthew12:36 But I tell you that men will have to give account on the day of judgment for every careless word they have spoken.
Matthew12:37 For by your words you will be acquitted
Matthew12:38 Then some of the Pharisees and teachers of the law said to him
Matthew12:39 He answered
Matthew12:40 For as Jonah was three days and three nights in the belly of a huge fish
Matthew12:41 The men of Nineveh will stand up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it; for they repented at the preaching of Jonah
Matthew12:42 The Queen of the South will rise at the judgment with this generation and condemn it; for she came from the ends of the earth to listen to Solomon's wisdom
Matthew12:43 When an evil spirit comes out of a man
Matthew12:44 Then it says
Matthew12:45 Then it goes and takes with it seven other spirits more wicked than itself
Matthew12:46 While Jesus was still talking to the crowd
Matthew12:47 Someone told him
Matthew12:48 He replied to him
Matthew12:49 Pointing to his disciples
Matthew12:50 For whoever does the will of my Father in heaven is my brother and sister and mother.
Matthew13:1 That same day Jesus went out of the house and sat by the lake.
Matthew13:2 Such large crowds gathered around him that he got into a boat and sat in it
Matthew13:3 Then he told them many things in parables
Matthew13:4 As he was scattering the seed
Matthew13:5 Some fell on rocky places
Matthew13:6 But when the sun came up
Matthew13:7 Other seed fell among thorns
Matthew13:8 Still other seed fell on good soil
Matthew13:9 He who has ears
Matthew13:10 The disciples came to him and asked
Matthew13:11 He replied
Matthew13:12 Whoever has will be given more
Matthew13:13 This is why I speak to them in parables: Though seeing
Matthew13:14 In them is fulfilled the prophecy of Isaiah: 'You will be ever hearing but never understanding; you will be ever seeing but never perceiving.
Matthew13:15 For this people's heart has become calloused; they hardly hear with their ears
Matthew13:16 But blessed are your eyes because they see
Matthew13:17 For I tell you the truth
Matthew13:18 Listen then to what the parable of the sower means:
Matthew13:19 When anyone hears the message about the kingdom and does not understand it
Matthew13:20 The one who received the seed that fell on rocky places is the man who hears the word and at once receives it with joy.
Matthew13:21 But since he has no root
Matthew13:22 The one who received the seed that fell among the thorns is the man who hears the word
Matthew13:23 But the one who received the seed that fell on good soil is the man who hears the word and understands it. He produces a crop
Matthew13:24 Jesus told them another parable: The kingdom of heaven is like a man who sowed good seed in his field.
Matthew13:25 But while everyone was sleeping
Matthew13:26 When the wheat sprouted and formed heads
Matthew13:27 The owner's servants came to him and said
Matthew13:28 'An enemy did this
Matthew13:29 'No
Matthew13:30 Let both grow together until the harvest. At that time I will tell the harvesters: First collect the weeds and tie them in bundles to be burned; then gather the wheat and bring it into my barn.'
Matthew13:31 He told them another parable: The kingdom of heaven is like a mustard seed
Matthew13:32 Though it is the smallest of all your seeds
Matthew13:33 He told them still another parable: The kingdom of heaven is like yeast that a woman took and mixed into a large amount of flour until it worked all through the dough.
Matthew13:34 Jesus spoke all these things to the crowd in parables; he did not say anything to them without using a parable.
Matthew13:35 So was fulfilled what was spoken through the prophet: I will open my mouth in parables
Matthew13:36 Then he left the crowd and went into the house. His disciples came to him and said
Matthew13:37 He answered
Matthew13:38 The field is the world
Matthew13:39 and the enemy who sows them is the devil. The harvest is the end of the age
Matthew13:40 As the weeds are pulled up and burned in the fire
Matthew13:41 The Son of Man will send out his angels
Matthew13:42 They will throw them into the fiery furnace
Matthew13:43 Then the righteous will shine like the sun in the kingdom of their Father. He who has ears
Matthew13:44 The kingdom of heaven is like treasure hidden in a field. When a man found it
Matthew13:45 Again
Matthew13:46 When he found one of great value
Matthew13:47 Once again
Matthew13:48 When it was full
Matthew13:49 This is how it will be at the end of the age. The angels will come and separate the wicked from the righteous
Matthew13:50 and throw them into the fiery furnace
Matthew13:51 Have you understood all these things? Jesus asked. Yes
Matthew13:52 He said to them
Matthew13:53 When Jesus had finished these parables
Matthew13:54 Coming to his hometown
Matthew13:55 Isn't this the carpenter's son? Isn't his mother's name Mary
Matthew13:56 Aren't all his sisters with us? Where then did this man get all these things?
Matthew13:57 And they took offense at him. But Jesus said to them
Matthew13:58 And he did not do many miracles there because of their lack of faith.

Matthew15:2 Why do your disciples break the tradition of the elders? They don't wash their hands before they eat!
Matthew15:3 Jesus replied
Matthew15:4 For God said
Matthew15:5 But you say that if a man says to his father or mother
Matthew15:6 he is not to 'honor his father 'with it. Thus you nullify the word of God for the sake of your tradition.
Matthew15:7 You hypocrites! Isaiah was right when he prophesied about you:
Matthew15:8 'These people honor me with their lips
Matthew15:9 They worship me in vain; their teachings are but rules taught by men.'
Matthew15:10 Jesus called the crowd to him and said
Matthew15:11 What goes into a man's mouth does not make him 'unclean

Matthew15:14 Leave them; they are blind guides. If a blind man leads a blind man
Matthew15:15 Peter said
Matthew15:16 Are you still so dull? Jesus asked them.
Matthew15:17 Don't you see that whatever enters the mouth goes into the stomach and then out of the body?
Matthew15:18 But the things that come out of the mouth come from the heart
Matthew15:19 For out of the heart come evil thoughts
Matthew15:20 These are what make a man 'unclean'; but eating with unwashed hands does not make him 'unclean.'
Matthew15:21 Leaving that place
Matthew15:22 A Canaanite woman from that vicinity came to him
Matthew15:23 Jesus did not answer a word. So his disciples came to him and urged him
Matthew15:24 He answered

Matthew15:26 He replied
Matthew15:27 Yes
Matthew15:28 Then Jesus answered

Matthew15:30 Great crowds came to him
Matthew15:31 The people were amazed when they saw the mute speaking
Matthew15:32 Jesus called his disciples to him and said
Matthew15:33 His disciples answered

Matthew15:35 He told the crowd to sit down on the ground.
Matthew15:36 Then he took the seven loaves and the fish
Matthew15:37 They all ate and were satisfied. Afterward the disciples picked up seven basketfuls of broken pieces that were left over.
Matthew15:38 The number of those who ate was four thousand

Matthew20:1 For the kingdom of heaven is like a landowner who went out early in the morning to hire men to work in his vineyard.
Matthew20:2 He agreed to pay them a denarius for the day and sent them into his vineyard.
Matthew20:3 About the third hour he went out and saw others standing in the marketplace doing nothing.
Matthew20:4 He told them
Matthew20:5 So they went.
Matthew20:6 He went out again about the sixth hour and the ninth hour and did the same thing. About the eleventh hour he went out and found still others standing around. He asked them
Matthew20:7 'Because no one has hired us
Matthew20:8 When evening came
Matthew20:9 The workers who were hired about the eleventh hour came and each received a denarius.
Matthew20:10 So when those came who were hired first
Matthew20:11 When they received it
Matthew20:12 'These men who were hired last worked only one hour
Matthew20:13 But he answered one of them
Matthew20:14 Take your pay and go. I want to give the man who was hired last the same as I gave you.

Luke1:1 Many have undertaken to draw up an account of the things that have been fulfilled among us
Luke1:2 just as they were handed down to us by those who from the first were eyewitnesses and servants of the word.
Luke1:3 Therefore
Luke1:4 so that you may know the certainty of the things you have been taught.
Luke1:5 In the time of Herod king of Judea there was a priest named Zechariah
Luke1:6 Both of them were upright in the sight of God
Luke1:7 But they had no children
Luke1:8 Once when Zechariah's division was on duty and he was serving as priest before God
Luke1:9 he was chosen by lot
Luke1:10 And when the time for the burning of incense came
Luke1:11 Then an angel of the Lord appeared to him
Luke1:12 When Zechariah saw him
Luke1:13 But the angel said to him: Do not be afraid
Luke1:14 He will be a joy and delight to you
Luke1:15 for he will be great in the sight of the Lord. He is never to take wine or other fermented drink
Luke1:16 Many of the people of Israel will he bring back to the Lord their God.
Luke1:17 And he will go on before the Lord
Luke1:18 Zechariah asked the angel
Luke1:19 The angel answered
Luke1:20 And now you will be silent and not able to speak until the day this happens
Luke1:21 Meanwhile
Luke1:22 When he came out
Luke1:23 When his time of service was completed
Luke1:24 After this his wife Elizabeth became pregnant and for five months remained in seclusion.
Luke1:25 The Lord has done this for me
Luke1:26 In the sixth month
Luke1:27 to a virgin pledged to be married to a man named Joseph
Luke1:28 The angel went to her and said
Luke1:29 Mary was greatly troubled at his words and wondered what kind of greeting this might be.
Luke1:30 But the angel said to her
Luke1:31 You will be with child and give birth to a son
Luke1:32 He will be great and will be called the Son of the Most High. The Lord God will give him the throne of his father David
Luke1:33 and he will reign over the house of Jacob forever; his kingdom will never end.
Luke1:34 How will this be
Luke1:35 The angel answered
Luke1:36 Even Elizabeth your relative is going to have a child in her old age
Luke1:37 For nothing is impossible with God.
Luke1:38 I am the Lord's servant
Luke1:39 At that time Mary got ready and hurried to a town in the hill country of Judea
Luke1:40 where she entered Zechariah's home and greeted Elizabeth.
Luke1:41 When Elizabeth heard Mary's greeting
Luke1:42 In a loud voice she exclaimed: Blessed are you among women
Luke1:43 But why am I so favored
Luke1:44 As soon as the sound of your greeting reached my ears
Luke1:45 Blessed is she who has believed that what the Lord has said to her will be accomplished!
Luke1:46 And Mary said: My soul glorifies the Lord
Luke1:47 and my spirit rejoices in God my Savior
Luke1:48 for he has been mindful of the humble state of his servant. From now on all generations will call me blessed
Luke1:49 for the Mighty One has done great things for me--holy is his name.
Luke1:50 His mercy extends to those who fear him
Luke1:51 He has performed mighty deeds with his arm; he has scattered those who are proud in their inmost thoughts.
Luke1:52 He has brought down rulers from their thrones but has lifted up the humble.
Luke1:53 He has filled the hungry with good things but has sent the rich away empty.
Luke1:54 He has helped his servant Israel
Luke1:55 to Abraham and his descendants forever
Luke1:56 Mary stayed with Elizabeth for about three months and then returned home.
Luke1:57 When it was time for Elizabeth to have her baby
Luke1:58 Her neighbors and relatives heard that the Lord had shown her great mercy
Luke1:59 On the eighth day they came to circumcise the child
Luke1:60 but his mother spoke up and said
Luke1:61 They said to her
Luke1:62 Then they made signs to his father
Luke1:63 He asked for a writing tablet
Luke1:64 Immediately his mouth was opened and his tongue was loosed
Luke1:65 The neighbors were all filled with awe
Luke1:66 Everyone who heard this wondered about it
Luke1:67 His father Zechariah was filled with the Holy Spirit and prophesied:
Luke1:68 Praise be to the Lord
Luke1:69 He has raised up a horn of salvation for us in the house of his servant David
Luke1:70 (as he said through his holy prophets of long ago)
Luke1:71 salvation from our enemies and from the hand of all who hate us--
Luke1:72 to show mercy to our fathers and to remember his holy covenant
Luke1:73 the oath he swore to our father Abraham:
Luke1:74 to rescue us from the hand of our enemies
Luke1:75 in holiness and righteousness before him all our days.
Luke1:76 And you
Luke1:77 to give his people the knowledge of salvation through the forgiveness of their sins
Luke1:78 because of the tender mercy of our God
Luke1:79 to shine on those living in darkness and in the shadow of death

Luke2:2 (This was the first census that took place while Quirinius was governor of Syria.)

Luke2:29 Sovereign Lord

Luke2:38 Coming up to them at that very moment

Luke8:12 Those along the path are the ones who hear

Luke8:14 The seed that fell among thorns stands for those who hear
Luke8:15 But the seed on good soil stands for those with a noble and good heart

Luke20:10 At harvest time he sent a servant to the tenants so they would give him some of the fruit of the vineyard. But the tenants beat him and sent him away empty-handed.
Luke20:11 He sent another servant
Luke20:12 He sent still a third
Luke20:13 Then the owner of the vineyard said
Luke20:14 But when the tenants saw him
Luke20:15 So they threw him out of the vineyard and killed him.
Luke20:16 What then will the owner of the vineyard do to them? He will come and kill those tenants and give the vineyard to others. When the people heard this
Luke20:17 Jesus looked directly at them and asked
Luke20:18 Everyone who falls on that stone will be broken to pieces
Luke20:19 The teachers of the law and the chief priests looked for a way to arrest him immediately
Luke20:20 Keeping a close watch on him
Luke20:21 So the spies questioned him: Teacher
Luke20:22 Is it right for us to pay taxes to Caesar or not?
Luke20:23 He saw through their duplicity and said to them
Luke20:24 Show me a denarius. Whose portrait and inscription are on it?
Luke20:25 Caesar's
Luke20:26 They were unable to trap him in what he had said there in public. And astonished by his answer
Luke20:27 Some of the Sadducees
Luke20:28 Teacher
Luke20:29 Now there were seven brothers. The first one married a woman and died childless.
Luke20:30 The second
Luke20:31 and then the third married her
Luke20:32 Finally
Luke20:33 Now then
Luke20:34 Jesus replied
Luke20:35 But those who are considered worthy of taking part in that age and in the resurrection from the dead will neither marry nor be given in marriage
Luke20:36 and they can no longer die; for they are like the angels. They are God's children
Luke20:37 But in the account of the bush
Luke20:38 He is not the God of the dead
Luke20:39 Some of the teachers of the law responded
Luke20:40 And no one dared to ask him any more questions.
Luke20:41 Then Jesus said to them
Luke20:42 David himself declares in the Book of Psalms: 'The Lord said to my Lord: Sit at my right hand
Luke20:43 until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet.'
Luke20:44 David calls him 'Lord.' How then can he be his son?
Luke20:45 While all the people were listening
Luke20:46 Beware of the teachers of the law. They like to walk around in flowing robes and love to be greeted in the marketplaces and have the most important seats in the synagogues and the places of honor at banquets.
Luke20:47 They devour widows' houses and for a show make lengthy prayers. Such men will be punished most severely.
Luke21:1 As he looked up
Luke21:2 He also saw a poor widow put in two very small copper coins.
Luke21:3 I tell you the truth
Luke21:4 All these people gave their gifts out of their wealth; but she out of her poverty put in all she had to live on.
Luke21:5 Some of his disciples were remarking about how the temple was adorned with beautiful stones and with gifts dedicated to God. But Jesus said
Luke21:6 As for what you see here
Luke21:7 Teacher
Luke21:8 He replied: Watch out that you are not deceived. For many will come in my name
Luke21:9 When you hear of wars and revolutions
Luke21:10 Then he said to them: Nation will rise against nation
Luke21:11 There will be great earthquakes
Luke21:12 But before all this
Luke21:13 This will result in your being witnesses to them.
Luke21:14 But make up your mind not to worry beforehand how you will defend yourselves.
Luke21:15 For I will give you words and wisdom that none of your adversaries will be able to resist or contradict.
Luke21:16 You will be betrayed even by parents
Luke21:17 All men will hate you because of me.
Luke21:18 But not a hair of your head will perish.
Luke21:19 By standing firm you will gain life.
Luke21:20 When you see Jerusalem being surrounded by armies
Luke21:21 Then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains
Luke21:22 For this is the time of punishment in fulfillment of all that has been written.
Luke21:23 How dreadful it will be in those days for pregnant women and nursing mothers! There will be great distress in the land and wrath against this people.
Luke21:24 They will fall by the sword and will be taken as prisoners to all the nations. Jerusalem will be trampled on by the Gentiles until the times of the Gentiles are fulfilled.
Luke21:25 There will be signs in the sun
Luke21:26 Men will faint from terror
Luke21:27 At that time they will see the Son of Man coming in a cloud with power and great glory.
Luke21:28 When these things begin to take place
Luke21:29 He told them this parable: Look at the fig tree and all the trees.
Luke21:30 When they sprout leaves
Luke21:31 Even so
Luke21:32 I tell you the truth
Luke21:33 Heaven and earth will pass away
Luke21:34 Be careful
Luke21:35 For it will come upon all those who live on the face of the whole earth.
Luke21:36 Be always on the watch
Luke21:37 Each day Jesus was teaching at the temple
Luke21:38 and all the people came early in the morning to hear him at the temple.

John1:2 He was with God in the beginning.

John1:4 In him was life
John1:5 The light shines in the darkness

John1:7 He came as a witness to testify concerning that light
John1:8 He himself was not the light; he came only as a witness to the light.
John1:9 The true light that gives light to every man was coming into the world.

John1:11 He came to that which was his own
John1:12 Yet to all who received him
John1:13 children born not of natural descent

John1:17 For the law was given through Moses; grace and truth came through Jesus Christ.

John3:6 Flesh gives birth to flesh

1Corinthians8:1 Now about food sacrificed to idols: We know that we all possess knowledge. Knowledge puffs up
1Corinthians8:2 The man who thinks he knows something does not yet know as he ought to know.
1Corinthians8:3 But the man who loves God is known by God.
1Corinthians8:4 So then
1Corinthians8:5 For even if there are so-called gods
1Corinthians8:6 yet for us there is but one God
1Corinthians8:7 But not everyone knows this. Some people are still so accustomed to idols that when they eat such food they think of it as having been sacrificed to an idol
1Corinthians8:8 But food does not bring us near to God; we are no worse if we do not eat
1Corinthians8:9 Be careful
1Corinthians8:10 For if anyone with a weak conscience sees you who have this knowledge eating in an idol's temple
1Corinthians8:11 So this weak brother
1Corinthians8:12 When you sin against your brothers in this way and wound their weak conscience

1Corinthians9:1 Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Have I not seen Jesus our Lord? Are you not the result of my work in the Lord?
1Corinthians9:2 Even though I may not be an apostle to others
1Corinthians9:3 This is my defense to those who sit in judgment on me.
1Corinthians9:4 Don't we have the right to food and drink?
1Corinthians9:5 Don't we have the right to take a believing wife along with us
1Corinthians9:6 Or is it only I and Barnabas who must work for a living?
1Corinthians9:7 Who serves as a soldier at his own expense? Who plants a vineyard and does not eat of its grapes? Who tends a flock and does not drink of the milk?
1Corinthians9:8 Do I say this merely from a human point of view? Doesn't the Law say the same thing?
1Corinthians9:9 For it is written in the Law of Moses: Do not muzzle an ox while it is treading out the grain. Is it about oxen that God is concerned?
1Corinthians9:10 Surely he says this for us
1Corinthians9:11 If we have sown spiritual seed among you
1Corinthians9:12 If others have this right of support from you
1Corinthians9:13 But we did not use this right. On the contrary
1Corinthians9:14 In the same way
1Corinthians9:15 But I have not used any of these rights. And I am not writing this in the hope that you will do such things for me. I would rather die than have anyone deprive me of this boast.
1Corinthians9:16 Yet when I preach the gospel
1Corinthians9:17 If I preach voluntarily
1Corinthians9:18 What then is my reward? Just this: that in preaching the gospel I may offer it free of charge
1Corinthians9:19 Though I am free and belong to no man
1Corinthians9:20 To the Jews I became like a Jew
1Corinthians9:21 To those not having the law I became like one not having the law (though I am not free from God's law but am under Christ's law)
1Corinthians9:22 To the weak I became weak
1Corinthians9:23 I do all this for the sake of the gospel
1Corinthians9:24 Do you not know that in a race all the runners run
1Corinthians9:25 Everyone who competes in the games goes into strict training. They do it to get a crown that will not last; but we do it to get a crown that will last forever.
1Corinthians9:26 Therefore I do not run like a man running aimlessly; I do not fight like a man beating the air.
1Corinthians9:27 No

1Corinthians12:9 to another faith by the same Spirit
1Corinthians12:10 to another miraculous powers
1Corinthians12:11 All these are the work of one and the same Spirit

1Corinthians12:13 For we were all baptized by one Spirit into one body--whether Jews or Greeks
1Corinthians12:14 Now the body is not made up of one part but of many.

1Corinthians12:16 And if the ear should say
1Corinthians12:17 If the whole body were an eye
1Corinthians12:18 But in fact God has arranged the parts in the body
1Corinthians12:19 If they were all one part
1Corinthians12:20 As it is
1Corinthians12:21 The eye cannot say to the hand
1Corinthians12:22 On the contrary
1Corinthians12:23 and the parts that we think are less honorable we treat with special honor. And the parts that are unpresentable are treated with special modesty
1Corinthians12:24 while our presentable parts need no special treatment. But God has combined the members of the body and has given greater honor to the parts that lacked it
1Corinthians12:25 so that there should be no division in the body
1Corinthians12:26 If one part suffers

1Corinthians12:30 Do all have gifts of healing? Do all speak in tongues? Do all interpret?
1Corinthians12:31 But eagerly desire the greater gifts. And now I will show you the most excellent way.

1Corinthians15:5 and that he appeared to Peter
1Corinthians15:6 After that
1Corinthians15:7 Then he appeared to James
1Corinthians15:8 and last of all he appeared to me also
1Corinthians15:9 For I am the least of the apostles and do not even deserve to be called an apostle
1Corinthians15:10 But by the grace of God I am what I am
1Corinthians15:11 Whether
1Corinthians15:12 But if it is preached that Christ has been raised from the dead

1Corinthians15:14 And if Christ has not been raised
1Corinthians15:15 More than that
1Corinthians15:16 For if the dead are not raised
1Corinthians15:17 And if Christ has not been raised
1Corinthians15:18 Then those also who have fallen asleep in Christ are lost.
1Corinthians15:19 If only for this life we have hope in Christ
1Corinthians15:20 But Christ has indeed been raised from the dead
1Corinthians15:21 For since death came through a man
1Corinthians15:22 For as in Adam all die
1Corinthians15:23 But each in his own turn: Christ
1Corinthians15:24 Then the end will come
1Corinthians15:25 For he must reign until he has put all his enemies under his feet.
1Corinthians15:26 The last enemy to be destroyed is death.
1Corinthians15:27 For he has put everything under his feet. Now when it says that everything has been put under him
1Corinthians15:28 When he has done this
1Corinthians15:29 Now if there is no resurrection
1Corinthians15:30 And as for us
1Corinthians15:31 I die every day--I mean that
1Corinthians15:32 If I fought wild beasts in Ephesus for merely human reasons
1Corinthians15:33 Do not be misled: Bad company corrupts good character.
1Corinthians15:34 Come back to your senses as you ought
1Corinthians15:35 But someone may ask
1Corinthians15:36 How foolish! What you sow does not come to life unless it dies.
1Corinthians15:37 When you sow
1Corinthians15:38 But God gives it a body as he has determined
1Corinthians15:39 All flesh is not the same: Men have one kind of flesh
1Corinthians15:40 There are also heavenly bodies and there are earthly bodies; but the splendor of the heavenly bodies is one kind
1Corinthians15:41 The sun has one kind of splendor
1Corinthians15:42 So will it be with the resurrection of the dead. The body that is sown is perishable
1Corinthians15:43 it is sown in dishonor
1Corinthians15:44 it is sown a natural body
1Corinthians15:45 So it is written: The first man Adam became a living being; the last Adam
1Corinthians15:46 The spiritual did not come first
1Corinthians15:47 The first man was of the dust of the earth
1Corinthians15:48 As was the earthly man
1Corinthians15:49 And just as we have borne the likeness of the earthly man
1Corinthians15:50 I declare to you
1Corinthians15:51 Listen
1Corinthians15:52 in a flash
1Corinthians15:53 For the perishable must clothe itself with the imperishable
1Corinthians15:54 When the perishable has been clothed with the imperishable
1Corinthians15:55 Where
1Corinthians15:56 The sting of death is sin
1Corinthians15:57 But thanks be to God! He gives us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ.
1Corinthians15:58 Therefore

2Corinthians11:26 I have been constantly on the move. I have been in danger from rivers

2Corinthians11:33 But I was lowered in a basket from a window in the wall and slipped through his hands.

Galatians1:24 And they praised God because of me.

Galatians5:6 For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision nor uncircumcision has any value. The only thing that counts is faith expressing itself through love.

Galatians5:16 So I say
Galatians5:17 For the sinful nature desires what is contrary to the Spirit
Galatians5:18 But if you are led by the Spirit

Ephesians5:16 making the most of every opportunity
Ephesians5:17 Therefore do not be foolish
Ephesians5:18 Do not get drunk on wine
Ephesians5:19 Speak to one another with psalms
Ephesians5:20 always giving thanks to God the Father for everything
Ephesians5:21 Submit to one another out of reverence for Christ.
Ephesians5:22 Wives
Ephesians5:23 For the husband is the head of the wife as Christ is the head of the church
Ephesians5:24 Now as the church submits to Christ
Ephesians5:25 Husbands
Ephesians5:26 to make her holy
Ephesians5:27 and to present her to himself as a radiant church
Ephesians5:28 In this same way
Ephesians5:29 After all
Ephesians5:30 for we are members of his body.
Ephesians5:31 For this reason a man will leave his father and mother and be united to his wife
Ephesians5:32 This is a profound mystery--but I am talking about Christ and the church.
Ephesians5:33 However
Ephesians6:1 Children
Ephesians6:2 Honor your father and mother--which is the first commandment with a promise--
Ephesians6:3 that it may go well with you and that you may enjoy long life on the earth.
Ephesians6:4 Fathers
Ephesians6:5 Slaves
Ephesians6:6 Obey them not only to win their favor when their eye is on you
Ephesians6:7 Serve wholeheartedly
Ephesians6:8 because you know that the Lord will reward everyone for whatever good he does
Ephesians6:9 And masters
Ephesians6:10 Finally
Ephesians6:11 Put on the full armor of God so that you can take your stand against the devil's schemes.
Ephesians6:12 For our struggle is not against flesh and blood
Ephesians6:13 Therefore put on the full armor of God
Ephesians6:14 Stand firm then
Ephesians6:15 and with your feet fitted with the readiness that comes from the gospel of peace.
Ephesians6:16 In addition to all this
Ephesians6:17 Take the helmet of salvation and the sword of the Spirit
Ephesians6:18 And pray in the Spirit on all occasions with all kinds of prayers and requests. With this in mind
Ephesians6:19 Pray also for me
Ephesians6:20 for which I am an ambassador in chains. Pray that I may declare it fearlessly
Ephesians6:21 Tychicus
Ephesians6:22 I am sending him to you for this very purpose
Ephesians6:23 Peace to the brothers
Ephesians6:24 Grace to all who love our Lord Jesus Christ with an undying love.
Philippians1:1 Paul and Timothy
Philippians1:2 Grace and peace to you from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
Philippians1:3 I thank my God every time I remember you.
Philippians1:4 In all my prayers for all of you
Philippians1:5 because of your partnership in the gospel from the first day until now
Philippians1:6 being confident of this
Philippians1:7 It is right for me to feel this way about all of you
Philippians1:8 God can testify how I long for all of you with the affection of Christ Jesus.
Philippians1:9 And this is my prayer: that your love may abound more and more in knowledge and depth of insight
Philippians1:10 so that you may be able to discern what is best and may be pure and blameless until the day of Christ
Philippians1:11 filled with the fruit of righteousness that comes through Jesus Christ--to the glory and praise of God.
Philippians1:12 Now I want you to know
Philippians1:13 As a result
Philippians1:14 Because of my chains
Philippians1:15 It is true that some preach Christ out of envy and rivalry
Philippians1:16 The latter do so in love
Philippians1:17 The former preach Christ out of selfish ambition
Philippians1:18 But what does it matter? The important thing is that in every way
Philippians1:19 Yes
Philippians1:20 I eagerly expect and hope that I will in no way be ashamed
Philippians1:21 For to me
Philippians1:22 If I am to go on living in the body
Philippians1:23 I am torn between the two: I desire to depart and be with Christ
Philippians1:24 but it is more necessary for you that I remain in the body.
Philippians1:25 Convinced of this
Philippians1:26 so that through my being with you again your joy in Christ Jesus will overflow on account of me.
Philippians1:27 Whatever happens
Philippians1:28 without being frightened in any way by those who oppose you. This is a sign to them that they will be destroyed
Philippians1:29 For it has been granted to you on behalf of Christ not only to believe on him
Philippians1:30 since you are going through the same struggle you saw I had
Philippians2:1 If you have any encouragement from being united with Christ
Philippians2:2 then make my joy complete by being like-minded
Philippians2:3 Do nothing out of selfish ambition or vain conceit
Philippians2:4 Each of you should look not only to your own interests
Philippians2:5 Your attitude should be the same as that of Christ Jesus:
Philippians2:6 Who
Philippians2:7 but made himself nothing
Philippians2:8 And being found in appearance as a man
Philippians2:9 Therefore God exalted him to the highest place and gave him the name that is above every name
Philippians2:10 that at the name of Jesus every knee should bow
Philippians2:11 and every tongue confess that Jesus Christ is Lord
Philippians2:12 Therefore
Philippians2:13 for it is God who works in you to will and to act according to his good purpose.
Philippians2:14 Do everything without complaining or arguing
Philippians2:15 so that you may become blameless and pure
Philippians2:16 as you hold out the word of life--in order that I may boast on the day of Christ that I did not run or labor for nothing.
Philippians2:17 But even if I am being poured out like a drink offering on the sacrifice and service coming from your faith
Philippians2:18 So you too should be glad and rejoice with me.
Philippians2:19 I hope in the Lord Jesus to send Timothy to you soon
Philippians2:20 I have no one else like him
Philippians2:21 For everyone looks out for his own interests
Philippians2:22 But you know that Timothy has proved himself
Philippians2:23 I hope
Philippians2:24 And I am confident in the Lord that I myself will come soon.
Philippians2:25 But I think it is necessary to send back to you Epaphroditus
Philippians2:26 For he longs for all of you and is distressed because you heard he was ill.
Philippians2:27 Indeed he was ill
Philippians2:28 Therefore I am all the more eager to send him
Philippians2:29 Welcome him in the Lord with great joy
Philippians2:30 because he almost died for the work of Christ
Philippians3:1 Finally
Philippians3:2 Watch out for those dogs
Philippians3:3 For it is we who are the circumcision
Philippians3:4 though I myself have reasons for such confidence. If anyone else thinks he has reasons to put confidence in the flesh
Philippians3:5 circumcised on the eighth day
Philippians3:6 as for zeal
Philippians3:7 But whatever was to my profit I now consider loss for the sake of Christ.
Philippians3:8 What is more
Philippians3:9 and be found in him
Philippians3:10 I want to know Christ and the power of his resurrection and the fellowship of sharing in his sufferings
Philippians3:11 and so
Philippians3:12 Not that I have already obtained all this
Philippians3:13 Brothers
Philippians3:14 I press on toward the goal to win the prize for which God has called me heavenward in Christ Jesus.
Philippians3:15 All of us who are mature should take such a view of things. And if on some point you think differently
Philippians3:16 Only let us live up to what we have already attained.
Philippians3:17 Join with others in following my example
Philippians3:18 For
Philippians3:19 Their destiny is destruction
Philippians3:20 But our citizenship is in heaven. And we eagerly await a Savior from there
Philippians3:21 who
Philippians4:1 Therefore
Philippians4:2 I plead with Euodia and I plead with Syntyche to agree with each other in the Lord.
Philippians4:3 Yes
Philippians4:4 Rejoice in the Lord always. I will say it again: Rejoice!
Philippians4:5 Let your gentleness be evident to all. The Lord is near.
Philippians4:6 Do not be anxious about anything
Philippians4:7 And the peace of God
Philippians4:8 Finally
Philippians4:9 Whatever you have learned or received or heard from me
Philippians4:10 I rejoice greatly in the Lord that at last you have renewed your concern for me. Indeed
Philippians4:11 I am not saying this because I am in need
Philippians4:12 I know what it is to be in need
Philippians4:13 I can do everything through him who gives me strength.
Philippians4:14 Yet it was good of you to share in my troubles.
Philippians4:15 Moreover
Philippians4:16 for even when I was in Thessalonica
Philippians4:17 Not that I am looking for a gift
Philippians4:18 I have received full payment and even more; I am amply supplied
Philippians4:19 And my God will meet all your needs according to his glorious riches in Christ Jesus.
Philippians4:20 To our God and Father be glory for ever and ever. Amen.

Philippians4:22 All the saints send you greetings

Colossians1:1 Paul
Colossians1:2 To the holy and faithful brothers in Christ at Colosse: Grace and peace to you from God our Father.
Colossians1:3 We always thank God
Colossians1:4 because we have heard of your faith in Christ Jesus and of the love you have for all the saints--
Colossians1:5 the faith and love that spring from the hope that is stored up for you in heaven and that you have already heard about in the word of truth
Colossians1:6 that has come to you. All over the world this gospel is bearing fruit and growing
Colossians1:7 You learned it from Epaphras
Colossians1:8 and who also told us of your love in the Spirit.
Colossians1:9 For this reason
Colossians1:10 And we pray this in order that you may live a life worthy of the Lord and may please him in every way: bearing fruit in every good work
Colossians1:11 being strengthened with all power according to his glorious might so that you may have great endurance and patience
Colossians1:12 giving thanks to the Father
Colossians1:13 For he has rescued us from the dominion of darkness and brought us into the kingdom of the Son he loves
Colossians1:14 in whom we have redemption
Colossians1:15 He is the image of the invisible God
Colossians1:16 For by him all things were created: things in heaven and on earth
Colossians1:17 He is before all things
Colossians1:18 And he is the head of the body
Colossians1:19 For God was pleased to have all his fullness dwell in him
Colossians1:20 and through him to reconcile to himself all things
Colossians1:21 Once you were alienated from God and were enemies in your minds because of your evil behavior.
Colossians1:22 But now he has reconciled you by Christ's physical body through death to present you holy in his sight
Colossians1:23 if you continue in your faith
Colossians1:24 Now I rejoice in what was suffered for you
Colossians1:25 I have become its servant by the commission God gave me to present to you the word of God in its fullness--
Colossians1:26 the mystery that has been kept hidden for ages and generations
Colossians1:27 To them God has chosen to make known among the Gentiles the glorious riches of this mystery
Colossians1:28 We proclaim him
Colossians1:29 To this end I labor
Colossians2:1 I want you to know how much I am struggling for you and for those at Laodicea
Colossians2:2 My purpose is that they may be encouraged in heart and united in love
Colossians2:3 in whom are hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.
Colossians2:4 I tell you this so that no one may deceive you by fine-sounding arguments.
Colossians2:5 For though I am absent from you in body
Colossians2:6 So then
Colossians2:7 rooted and built up in him
Colossians2:8 See to it that no one takes you captive through hollow and deceptive philosophy
Colossians2:9 For in Christ all the fullness of the Deity lives in bodily form
Colossians2:10 and you have been given fullness in Christ
Colossians2:11 In him you were also circumcised
Colossians2:12 having been buried with him in baptism and raised with him through your faith in the power of God
Colossians2:13 When you were dead in your sins and in the uncircumcision of your sinful nature
Colossians2:14 having canceled the written code
Colossians2:15 And having disarmed the powers and authorities
Colossians2:16 Therefore do not let anyone judge you by what you eat or drink
Colossians2:17 These are a shadow of the things that were to come; the reality
Colossians2:18 Do not let anyone who delights in false humility and the worship of angels disqualify you for the prize. Such a person goes into great detail about what he has seen
Colossians2:19 He has lost connection with the Head
Colossians2:20 Since you died with Christ to the basic principles of this world
Colossians2:21 Do not handle! Do not taste! Do not touch!?
Colossians2:22 These are all destined to perish with use
Colossians2:23 Such regulations indeed have an appearance of wisdom

Colossians3:2 Set your minds on things above
Colossians3:3 For you died
Colossians3:4 When Christ
Colossians3:5 Put to death
Colossians3:6 Because of these
Colossians3:7 You used to walk in these ways
Colossians3:8 But now you must rid yourselves of all such things as these: anger
Colossians3:9 Do not lie to each other
Colossians3:10 and have put on the new self
Colossians3:11 Here there is no Greek or Jew
Colossians3:12 Therefore

Colossians3:14 And over all these virtues put on love

Colossians3:16 Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly as you teach and admonish one another with all wisdom
Colossians3:17 And whatever you do

Colossians3:19 Husbands
Colossians3:20 Children
Colossians3:21 Fathers

Colossians4:2 Devote yourselves to prayer
Colossians4:3 And pray for us
Colossians4:4 Pray that I may proclaim it clearly
Colossians4:5 Be wise in the way you act toward outsiders; make the most of every opportunity.

Colossians4:8 I am sending him to you for the express purpose that you may know about our circumstances and that he may encourage your hearts.
Colossians4:9 He is coming with Onesimus

1Thessalonians1:2 We always thank God for all of you
1Thessalonians1:3 We continually remember before our God and Father your work produced by faith
1Thessalonians1:4 For we know
1Thessalonians1:5 because our gospel came to you not simply with words
1Thessalonians1:6 You became imitators of us and of the Lord; in spite of severe suffering
1Thessalonians1:7 And so you became a model to all the believers in Macedonia and Achaia.
1Thessalonians1:8 The Lord's message rang out from you not only in Macedonia and Achaia--your faith in God has become known everywhere. Therefore we do not need to say anything about it
1Thessalonians1:9 for they themselves report what kind of reception you gave us. They tell how you turned to God from idols to serve the living and true God
1Thessalonians1:10 and to wait for his Son from heaven
1Thessalonians2:1 You know
1Thessalonians2:2 We had previously suffered and been insulted in Philippi
1Thessalonians2:3 For the appeal we make does not spring from error or impure motives
1Thessalonians2:4 On the contrary
1Thessalonians2:5 You know we never used flattery
1Thessalonians2:6 We were not looking for praise from men
1Thessalonians2:7 As apostles of Christ we could have been a burden to you
1Thessalonians2:8 We loved you so much that we were delighted to share with you not only the gospel of God but our lives as well
1Thessalonians2:9 Surely you remember
1Thessalonians2:10 You are witnesses
1Thessalonians2:11 For you know that we dealt with each of you as a father deals with his own children
1Thessalonians2:12 encouraging
1Thessalonians2:13 And we also thank God continually because
1Thessalonians2:14 For you
1Thessalonians2:15 who killed the Lord Jesus and the prophets and also drove us out. They displease God and are hostile to all men
1Thessalonians2:16 in their effort to keep us from speaking to the Gentiles so that they may be saved. In this way they always heap up their sins to the limit. The wrath of God has come upon them at last.
1Thessalonians2:17 But
1Thessalonians2:18 For we wanted to come to you--certainly I
1Thessalonians2:19 For what is our hope
1Thessalonians2:20 Indeed
1Thessalonians3:1 So when we could stand it no longer
1Thessalonians3:2 We sent Timothy
1Thessalonians3:3 so that no one would be unsettled by these trials. You know quite well that we were destined for them.
1Thessalonians3:4 In fact
1Thessalonians3:5 For this reason
1Thessalonians3:6 But Timothy has just now come to us from you and has brought good news about your faith and love. He has told us that you always have pleasant memories of us and that you long to see us
1Thessalonians3:7 Therefore
1Thessalonians3:8 For now we really live
1Thessalonians3:9 How can we thank God enough for you in return for all the joy we have in the presence of our God because of you?
1Thessalonians3:10 Night and day we pray most earnestly that we may see you again and supply what is lacking in your faith.
1Thessalonians3:11 Now may our God and Father himself and our Lord Jesus clear the way for us to come to you.
1Thessalonians3:12 May the Lord make your love increase and overflow for each other and for everyone else
1Thessalonians3:13 May he strengthen your hearts so that you will be blameless and holy in the presence of our God and Father when our Lord Jesus comes with all his holy ones.
1Thessalonians4:1 Finally
1Thessalonians4:2 For you know what instructions we gave you by the authority of the Lord Jesus.
1Thessalonians4:3 It is God's will that you should be sanctified: that you should avoid sexual immorality;
1Thessalonians4:4 that each of you should learn to control his own body in a way that is holy and honorable
1Thessalonians4:5 not in passionate lust like the heathen
1Thessalonians4:6 and that in this matter no one should wrong his brother or take advantage of him. The Lord will punish men for all such sins
1Thessalonians4:7 For God did not call us to be impure
1Thessalonians4:8 Therefore
1Thessalonians4:9 Now about brotherly love we do not need to write to you
1Thessalonians4:10 And in fact
1Thessalonians4:11 Make it your ambition to lead a quiet life
1Thessalonians4:12 so that your daily life may win the respect of outsiders and so that you will not be dependent on anybody.
1Thessalonians4:13 Brothers
1Thessalonians4:14 We believe that Jesus died and rose again and so we believe that God will bring with Jesus those who have fallen asleep in him.
1Thessalonians4:15 According to the Lord's own word
1Thessalonians4:16 For the Lord himself will come down from heaven
1Thessalonians4:17 After that
1Thessalonians4:18 Therefore encourage each other with these words.

2Thessalonians1:1 Paul
2Thessalonians1:2 Grace and peace to you from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
2Thessalonians1:3 We ought always to thank God for you
2Thessalonians1:4 Therefore
2Thessalonians1:5 All this is evidence that God's judgment is right
2Thessalonians1:6 God is just: He will pay back trouble to those who trouble you
2Thessalonians1:7 and give relief to you who are troubled
2Thessalonians1:8 He will punish those who do not know God and do not obey the gospel of our Lord Jesus.
2Thessalonians1:9 They will be punished with everlasting destruction and shut out from the presence of the Lord and from the majesty of his power
2Thessalonians1:10 on the day he comes to be glorified in his holy people and to be marveled at among all those who have believed. This includes you
2Thessalonians1:11 With this in mind
2Thessalonians1:12 We pray this so that the name of our Lord Jesus may be glorified in you
2Thessalonians2:1 Concerning the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ and our being gathered to him
2Thessalonians2:2 not to become easily unsettled or alarmed by some prophecy
2Thessalonians2:3 Don't let anyone deceive you in any way
2Thessalonians2:4 He will oppose and will exalt himself over everything that is called God or is worshiped
2Thessalonians2:5 Don't you remember that when I was with you I used to tell you these things?
2Thessalonians2:6 And now you know what is holding him back
2Thessalonians2:7 For the secret power of lawlessness is already at work; but the one who now holds it back will continue to do so till he is taken out of the way.
2Thessalonians2:8 And then the lawless one will be revealed
2Thessalonians2:9 The coming of the lawless one will be in accordance with the work of Satan displayed in all kinds of counterfeit miracles
2Thessalonians2:10 and in every sort of evil that deceives those who are perishing. They perish because they refused to love the truth and so be saved.
2Thessalonians2:11 For this reason God sends them a powerful delusion so that they will believe the lie
2Thessalonians2:12 and so that all will be condemned who have not believed the truth but have delighted in wickedness.
2Thessalonians2:13 But we ought always to thank God for you
2Thessalonians2:14 He called you to this through our gospel
2Thessalonians2:15 So then
2Thessalonians2:16 May our Lord Jesus Christ himself and God our Father
2Thessalonians2:17 encourage your hearts and strengthen you in every good deed and word.
2Thessalonians3:1 Finally
2Thessalonians3:2 And pray that we may be delivered from wicked and evil men
2Thessalonians3:3 But the Lord is faithful
2Thessalonians3:4 We have confidence in the Lord that you are doing and will continue to do the things we command.
2Thessalonians3:5 May the Lord direct your hearts into God's love and Christ's perseverance.
2Thessalonians3:6 In the name of the Lord Jesus Christ
2Thessalonians3:7 For you yourselves know how you ought to follow our example. We were not idle when we were with you
2Thessalonians3:8 nor did we eat anyone's food without paying for it. On the contrary
2Thessalonians3:9 We did this
2Thessalonians3:10 For even when we were with you
2Thessalonians3:11 We hear that some among you are idle. They are not busy; they are busybodies.
2Thessalonians3:12 Such people we command and urge in the Lord Jesus Christ to settle down and earn the bread they eat.
2Thessalonians3:13 And as for you
2Thessalonians3:14 If anyone does not obey our instruction in this letter
2Thessalonians3:15 Yet do not regard him as an enemy
2Thessalonians3:16 Now may the Lord of peace himself give you peace at all times and in every way. The Lord be with all of you.
2Thessalonians3:17 I
2Thessalonians3:18 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all.
1Timothy1:1 Paul
1Timothy1:2 To Timothy my true son in the faith: Grace
1Timothy1:3 As I urged you when I went into Macedonia
1Timothy1:4 nor to devote themselves to myths and endless genealogies. These promote controversies rather than God's work--which is by faith.
1Timothy1:5 The goal of this command is love
1Timothy1:6 Some have wandered away from these and turned to meaningless talk.
1Timothy1:7 They want to be teachers of the law
1Timothy1:8 We know that the law is good if one uses it properly.
1Timothy1:9 We also know that law is made not for the righteous but for lawbreakers and rebels
1Timothy1:10 for adulterers and perverts
1Timothy1:11 that conforms to the glorious gospel of the blessed God
1Timothy1:12 I thank Christ Jesus our Lord
1Timothy1:13 Even though I was once a blasphemer and a persecutor and a violent man
1Timothy1:14 The grace of our Lord was poured out on me abundantly
1Timothy1:15 Here is a trustworthy saying that deserves full acceptance: Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners--of whom I am the worst.
1Timothy1:16 But for that very reason I was shown mercy so that in me
1Timothy1:17 Now to the King eternal
1Timothy1:18 Timothy
1Timothy1:19 holding on to faith and a good conscience. Some have rejected these and so have shipwrecked their faith.
1Timothy1:20 Among them are Hymenaeus and Alexander
1Timothy2:1 I urge
1Timothy2:2 for kings and all those in authority
1Timothy2:3 This is good
1Timothy2:4 who wants all men to be saved and to come to a knowledge of the truth.
1Timothy2:5 For there is one God and one mediator between God and men
1Timothy2:6 who gave himself as a ransom for all men--the testimony given in its proper time.
1Timothy2:7 And for this purpose I was appointed a herald and an apostle--I am telling the truth
1Timothy2:8 I want men everywhere to lift up holy hands in prayer
1Timothy2:9 I also want women to dress modestly
1Timothy2:10 but with good deeds
1Timothy2:11 A woman should learn in quietness and full submission.
1Timothy2:12 I do not permit a woman to teach or to have authority over a man; she must be silent.
1Timothy2:13 For Adam was formed first
1Timothy2:14 And Adam was not the one deceived; it was the woman who was deceived and became a sinner.
1Timothy2:15 But women will be saved through childbearing--if they continue in faith

Hebrews9:1 Now the first covenant had regulations for worship and also an earthly sanctuary.
Hebrews9:2 A tabernacle was set up. In its first room were the lampstand
Hebrews9:3 Behind the second curtain was a room called the Most Holy Place
Hebrews9:4 which had the golden altar of incense and the gold-covered ark of the covenant. This ark contained the gold jar of manna
Hebrews9:5 Above the ark were the cherubim of the Glory
Hebrews9:6 When everything had been arranged like this
Hebrews9:7 But only the high priest entered the inner room
Hebrews9:8 The Holy Spirit was showing by this that the way into the Most Holy Place had not yet been disclosed as long as the first tabernacle was still standing.
Hebrews9:9 This is an illustration for the present time
Hebrews9:10 They are only a matter of food and drink and various ceremonial washings--external regulations applying until the time of the new order.
Hebrews9:11 When Christ came as high priest of the good things that are already here
Hebrews9:12 He did not enter by means of the blood of goats and calves; but he entered the Most Holy Place once for all by his own blood
Hebrews9:13 The blood of goats and bulls and the ashes of a heifer sprinkled on those who are ceremonially unclean sanctify them so that they are outwardly clean.
Hebrews9:14 How much more
Hebrews9:15 For this reason Christ is the mediator of a new covenant
Hebrews9:16 In the case of a will
Hebrews9:17 because a will is in force only when somebody has died; it never takes effect while the one who made it is living.
Hebrews9:18 This is why even the first covenant was not put into effect without blood.
Hebrews9:19 When Moses had proclaimed every commandment of the law to all the people
Hebrews9:20 He said
Hebrews9:21 In the same way
Hebrews9:22 In fact
Hebrews9:23 It was necessary
Hebrews9:24 For Christ did not enter a man-made sanctuary that was only a copy of the true one; he entered heaven itself
Hebrews9:25 Nor did he enter heaven to offer himself again and again
Hebrews9:26 Then Christ would have had to suffer many times since the creation of the world. But now he has appeared once for all at the end of the ages to do away with sin by the sacrifice of himself.
Hebrews9:27 Just as man is destined to die once
Hebrews9:28 so Christ was sacrificed once to take away the sins of many people; and he will appear a second time
